runners.name: Henrythenate
runners.spotlight: Three 5f wins on AW for previous yards, most recently in February, but treading water in that sphere since; ran well for a long way at Chester (5f, good) in May for new trainer but has more on his plate in this company.
runners.comment: Three AW wins at 5f; creditable show at Chester for new yard; more on his plate today

runners.name: Alfa Duplicate
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins at 5f/6f this spring; met her match in Call Margot at Southwell (5f, AW) in April but  showed her fighting spirit when refusing to give up her lead back on turf at Beverley (5f, good to soft) since; much bigger question here, up 4lb in a better race on a very different track.
runners.comment: Hung on to lead at Beverley latest; much bigger question to answer on very different track

runners.name: Chairmanfourtimes
runners.spotlight: Has won on good but the ground was soft for his best 2yo performance, when winning a Listed 5f race at Ayr last September; has done much less well in three runs since, including back in a handicap at York in May; needs the first-time tongue-tie to wave a magic wand.
runners.comment: Best form when winning 5f Listed race (soft) as 2yo; well held since; now tongue tied

runners.name: Naana's Shadow
runners.spotlight: AW winner as 2yo who has done well for new yard this year, making all from the rail draw at Catterick, then adopting the same tactics at York (5f, good) when losing her lead late on but still finishing a solid third of 17 (just ahead of Shes Got A Brother); in the thick of it.
runners.comment: Improved as 3yo; useful front-running 3rd of 17 at York recently; in the thick of it again

runners.name: One And Gone
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 6f novice on Kempton AW as 2yo; has had plenty of chances in handicaps, albeit with his persevering fourth to Wedonttelllies at Goodwood 13 days ago, when switched across the track, putting him on the scene here with the expected strong pace likely to suit; cheekpieces refitted.
runners.comment: Had plenty of chances in handicaps but good 4th at Goodwood puts him on the scene

runners.name: Proof
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner as 2yo; started the year with a 5f Southwell AW win but the style of that success, when stamina finally came into play after he was niggled along from some way out, suggests others will have brighter early speed on the return to 5f; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Has won on heavy; won his return over 5f on AW; others likely to have brighter early speed

runners.name: Artista
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins (good to firm and AW) last summer but she has been weighted to her best this year and was well beaten in a Beverley seller (first-time cheekpieces, now discarded) last month; up against it.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins as 2yo; weighted to best this year; well beaten in a Beverley seller latest

runners.name: Hanney Girl
runners.spotlight: Speedy filly who can lead; two 5f wins on good to firm as 2yo but she has run up to form on good to soft; creditable show when fifth of 17 at York last month but she will do well to win this off her current mark.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins as 2yo; creditable York run last month; will do well to win off current mark

runners.name: Shes Got A Brother
runners.spotlight: All three runs in these aids; made all for two 5f wins on Southwell AW before giving it a good shot on turf debut in a competitive York handicap (5f, good) in May, when just behind Naana's Shadow; open to improvement but lack of experience raises a question on this tricky track.
runners.comment: Made all for two 5f AW wins; good 4th on turf debut at York; open to improvement

runners.name: Temple Of Athena
runners.spotlight: All three wins at 5f, on soft, good to soft and the AW; has had some excuses since the latest in March and the return to a 5f handicap looks in her best interests after she ran in Listed races either side of a modest 6f run at Chester; it's likely she needs to find extra for this, even so.
runners.comment: All 3 wins at 5f; had excuses since the latest in March; return to 5f handicap should help

runners.name: Call Margot
runners.spotlight: Did well to overcome trouble in running on Southwell AW (5f) in April but she wasn't in such good form at York (5f, good) three weeks ago in her first turf handicap; has something to prove after that.
runners.comment: Overcame trouble in running for 5f win on AW in April; never involved back on turf at York

runners.name: Riley Rocks
runners.spotlight: Both wins over 6f (on AW) but he didn't seem inconvenienced by the return to 5f, despite failing to get a clear passage, at Goodwood recently in the race won by Wedonttelllies; capable of better but he's come from off the pace in three of his four starts which will complicate things in this field.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins; coped well with 5f latest despite not getting a clear run; similar worry here

runners.name: Coyy
runners.spotlight: Looked ahead of his mark when returned to 5f at Windsor (good to firm) in May, doing well to overcome a blocked passage, and a subsequent third behind Wedonttelllies at Goodwood (5f, good) after a slow start was creditable; has possibilities if he doesn't give himself too much to do.
runners.comment: Cosy 5f winner at Windsor after met trouble; slowly away when creditable 3rd at Goodwood

runners.name: U S S Charleston
runners.spotlight: Respectable rather than progressive on AW since a Wolverhampton win in December but he found soft turf to his liking when coming clear of his four rivals at Yarmouth (5f) last month; needs more off 7lb higher in this better race but further rain would help him more than some.
runners.comment: Appreciated soft ground when 5f winner at Yarmouth latest; up 7lb but rain helpful

runners.name: Rosie Frith
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; seems to handle most ground but her 5f maiden win at Cork (good to firm) last July was easily bettered by her AW achievements later; dropped right out on her return in a Listed race at Cork (extended 5f, soft) last month and has work to do off this mark back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Front-runs; best form on AW; well held on Listed return; has work to do off this mark

runners.name: Wedonttelllies
runners.spotlight: All turf races have been on good or quicker but dam's best form was on soft; sole 2yo win was over 7f on the AW; ran well for a long way over 6f at Ascot on second run back and proved thoroughly at home over the new 5f trip at Goodwood (good; first-time cheekpieces) since, racing well within himself and coming clear for an impressive win; up 6lb but has better to come.
runners.comment: AW winner at 7f as 2yo but well suited by drop to 5f when Goodwood winner latest; up 6lb

races.race_name: Oddschecker British EBF Woodcote Stakes (Conditions Race) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hickory Lad
runners.spotlight: Has built up a solid record over 5f on good ground; good second in an above-average Lily Agnes at Chester most recently, shaping as if he's ready for a crack at this extra furlong; big player on the figures.
runners.comment: Lily Agnes effort suggests he's ready for a crack at 6f; big player on the figures

runners.name: Rlasthope
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced gelding who is seeking a first win but has sound form, notably close second at Musselburgh to the subsequent Lily Agnes winner who remains unbeaten; ran well upped to 6f at York (good) last time; solid claims.
runners.comment: Thrice-raced gelding who is seeking a first win but is a solid contender on form

runners.name: Possessive
runners.spotlight: Flashed his tail but stuck on to prevail by 1l in novice event (6f, good) at York, recording an RPR that puts him firmly in the mix on this step up to a Woodcote; open to progress to boot; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Recorded a respected RPR in winning at York and is open to progress to boot

runners.name: Wild Terrain
runners.spotlight: Scored with something to spare under 5lb claimer in a maiden (6f, good) at Hamilton, beating five rivals; may show progress and make an impact at this stronger level, with Oisin Murphy booked.
runners.comment: Won comfortably under 5lb claimer at Hamilton; possibilities with Oisin Murphy booked

runners.name: Dandyman Dan
runners.spotlight: Showed his 40-1 win at Bath (5.7f, firm) was no fluke by running well in an admittedly substandard National Stakes at Sandown (5f, good; Listed) where he gave the impression this step back up in trip will suit.
runners.comment: Ran well in an admittedly substandard National Stakes at Sandown last time
runners.owner: Eric Griffiths, Mrs T Burns & Partner

runners.name: The Bookkeeper
runners.spotlight: Out of a 7f winner and proved suited by the step up to that distance at Haydock (good to soft) where he beat his three rivals; something to prove in this much deeper race returned to sprinting.
runners.comment: Haydock winner; something to prove in this deeper race returned to sprinting

runners.name: Havana Flash
runners.spotlight: 55,000euros yearling, 40,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Havana Grey half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Fast Attack (RPR 104), out of 5f/6f Listed scorer (109); sole newcomer in the field; market instructive.
runners.comment: 40,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; by Havana Grey; sole newcomer in the field

runners.name: Time And Effort
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form, having finished a modest fourth of seven at Musselburgh (5f, good) where she was one of the outsiders; nurseries may prove her ideal scene in due course.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form, having finished a modest fourth at Musselburgh

runners.name: Social Spirit
runners.spotlight: Northern raider; rallied for third of nine in maiden (5f, good to firm) at Doncaster, giving the strong impression he'll be suited by this extra furlong; could go well.
runners.comment: Northern raider; rallied for third over 5f at Doncaster; could go well

runners.name: Romidijo
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward with rallying third when upped to 6f at Chepstow (good to soft) but recorded an ordinary RPR and needs to show further improvement upped in class.
runners.comment: Rallying third when upped to 6f at Chepstow but needs another step forward

runners.name: Alpe d'Huez
runners.spotlight: Readily justified favouritism in 6f AW event at Lingfield and although the form isn't strong he looks open to progress; well down this pack on bare figures as things stand, however.
runners.comment: Form of ready AW success at Lingfield isn't strong but he's open to progress

runners.name: El Lambo
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who won well at Bath (5.7f, firm) most recently but that was only an ordinary maiden contest and the time was very slow; probably not up to this level.
runners.comment: Recorded a very slow time in winning an ordinary maiden at Bath most recently
runners.owner: The Lamb Inn Wantage Racing Team

runners.name: Ardad Steve
runners.spotlight: Showed clear promise with fourth of eight, amid traffic issues, at Yarmouth (6f, good) and is likely to improve; interesting contender for a trainer whose Woodcote record in the last three years reads 132.
runners.comment: Should build on Yarmouth effort; trainer has a solid recent Woodcote record; interesting

runners.name: Boiling Point
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on good/firmer ground; rallied gamely for Listed honours at Goodwood (1m2f) most recently and, assuming another gritty display is on the way, should remain competitive; top on his peak figure, achieved when defying top weight in last year's Cambridgeshire (1m1f).
runners.comment: Rallied gamely for Listed honours at Goodwood last time and should remain competitive
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Chancellor
runners.spotlight: Has taken his form to a new level, notably with a 1m Listed success, since switched to artificial surfaces; ran creditably in first-time cheekpieces (retained) on finals day at Newcastle most recently; the query is whether his very useful AW form can be transferred back to turf.
runners.comment: The query is whether his very useful AW form can be transferred back to turf

runners.name: Seagulls Eleven
runners.spotlight: Appears versatile as regards ground; Group 3 winner at last year's Glorious Goodwood and better than ever in two French races this term, making all at Longchamp (1m Listed) latest; reasonably solid in current form returned to home soil, and further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Better than ever in two French races this term; reasonably solid in current form
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Persica
runners.spotlight: Versatile as regards ground; gelded prior to below-par effort in sole start this term; smart on his day and is 2-2 at Epsom with wins at this meeting for the last two years; beat Ice Max (who received 3lb) by just under 1l in this race 12 months ago and warrants respect back here.
runners.comment: Record of 2-2 at Epsom includes a success in this race last year; respected back here

runners.name: Royal Playwright
runners.spotlight: Respectable fourth in this contest last year but showed better form, including success in conditions race and third-place finishes at Listed/Group level, over 1m2f/1m3f later in 2025; doesn't seem to be crying out for this drop back in trip on reappearance.
runners.comment: Respectable fourth in this contest last year; showed better form over 1m2f/1m3f later on

runners.name: Ice Max
runners.spotlight: Very useful performer whose good-ground form includes a close second in this race last year and respectable sixth to stablemate Boiling Point at Goodwood latest start; has registered the majority of wins on softer, notably in Group 2 in 2024 and Listed grade in 2025 (both 1m); major player.
runners.comment: Close second on good ground in this race last year; most wins on softer; major player
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Skukuza
runners.spotlight: Form dipped last time when he raced too freely; otherwise a broadly consistent sort who may rebound, provided he settles better; has Group form and can't be dismissed from a ratings perspective but may be more interesting when back at the Curragh where he's 2-2 (only 1-15 elsewhere).
runners.comment: May be more interesting when back at the Curragh where he's 2-2 (only 1-15 elsewhere)

runners.name: Qirat
runners.spotlight: Not totally solid on his form since 150-1 success when pacemaker in last year's Sussex Stakes (1m, good); below par in Listed grade in sole run this season; however, record of 3-3 at Goodwood bodes well for this first visit to Epsom, which is a fairly similar track; return to best is plausible.
runners.comment: Record of 3-3 at Goodwood (including Sussex win) bodes well for this first visit to Epsom

runners.name: Respond
runners.spotlight: Appeared to act on good to soft last term (unraced on soft); has gone from strength to strength this year, first on AW but most strikingly when returned to turf for a dominant, front-running display as 10-11 favourite in 11-runner race at Chester (1m2f, good); 9lb higher today but highly respected.
runners.comment: Going from strength to strength this year, dominant at Chester (1m2f, good) latest; up 9lb

runners.name: King's Code
runners.spotlight: Below form in this race in 2024 and also last April on his only other visit here; had an excellent autumn last year on AW and won again in January, but he's struggled to make an impact in more recent AW starts and finished last at York on return to turf; return to 1m2f would suit if he were in better form but this may be a tough turf mark.
runners.comment: Struggled to make an impact in recent AW starts and finished last on York return to turf

runners.name: Beylerbeyi
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to soft, much more to prove on soft; won the Cesarewitch at Newmarket (2m2f, good to firm; 10lb higher today) last October and had some very solid AW form over 1m2f-2m this year, prior to tailing off at 50-1 in the Group 3 Ormonde at Chester four weeks ago; he's not on a bad mark but plenty needs to go right if one of his late rushes is to prevail in this.
runners.comment: Not on a bad mark but plenty needs to go right if one of his late rushes is to prevail

runners.name: Ammes
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to soft, unraced on soft; fit from AW and hurdling when good second of seven at Redcar (1m2f) five weeks ago, keeping on strongly; up 2lb and into a better race but needs a second look.
runners.comment: Good second of seven at Redcar (1m2f) five weeks ago, keeping on strongly; up 2lb

runners.name: Sallaal
runners.spotlight: Has run respectably on soft; hooded on last two starts, with a back-to-form second of six when stepped up to 1m2f for C&D handicap (good) on 2026 reappearance; raised 5lb since and probably not straightforward but that was a promising effort here from out the back (ran on well with too much to do) and he has to be in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Upped to 1m2f for a C&D 2nd (good) on return, running on well with too much to do; up 5lb

runners.name: Have Secret
runners.spotlight: 6yo who has a career-high mark on the strength of his win at Pontefract (1m2f, good; acts on soft/heavy) in April; creditable fifth at York next time but the chief disincentive is probably that he ran poorly in this race last year on his only visit to Epsom.
runners.comment: Career-high mark after April win; ran poorly in this last year on his only visit to Epsom

runners.name: Quai De Bethune
runners.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good; London Gold Cup third at Newbury last May was a promising start to handicap life and he built on that with a last-gasp win from out the back at Royal Ascot (again 1m2f on good to firm); unraced since, having left Andrew Balding for 775,000gns that July and been gelded in September; 7lb higher than at Royal Ascot but he's had just six races.
runners.comment: Last-gasp win at Royal Ascot (1m2f, good to firm); left A Balding for 775,000gns that July

runners.name: Tycoon
runners.spotlight: Unraced on softer than good; failed to make a significant impact in his first four handicaps but did so at Newmarket (1m1f, good to firm) on latest outing, running on well into a close third of 12 off today's mark; return to 1m2f could help and he needs a second look.
runners.comment: Ran on well into a close third at Newmarket (1m1f, good to firm) last time

runners.name: Let's Dream
runners.spotlight: Won a soft-ground maiden in 2023 but peak efforts have been on good in the last 13 months; unraced here but scored twice over 1m2f at the similar Chester track, including on his only start this season; all three handicap wins were in tight finishes, but he's gone up just 3lb since latest and is likely to be seriously involved if he goes on the ground.
runners.comment: Peak efforts have been on good going in the last 13 months; won at Chester on reappearance

runners.name: Spoken Truth
runners.spotlight: Rather disappointing for Dermot Weld in Ireland after winning a soft-ground maiden; has still not added to that 2024 win but he's been highly competitive in 1m2f handicaps on all three starts for new connections, runner-up to an unexposed rival in the Zetland Gold Cup at Redcar (good to firm) 11 days ago; each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Acts on soft; highly competitive in 1m2f handicaps on all three starts for new connections

runners.name: Great David
runners.spotlight: Won on soft as 2yo; reappearance win last April saw him pitched afterwards into Listed and Group events, in which he was initially on the premises but his handicap mark shot up as a result; ninth of 12 to Respond at Chester wasn't a greatly auspicious return to handicap company on reappearance this season and connections now reach for headgear.
runners.comment: Ninth of 12 at Chester wasn't a greatly auspicious return to handicap; first headgear

runners.name: Liberty Lane
runners.spotlight: Usually races prominently (didn't when trying 1m4f last time); a mostly solid 2025 included 1m2f Listed wins in May and November; has started 2026 in downbeat fashion but today's ground could prompt something better, as his top three ratings have been registered on softer than good; below-form favourite in this race two years ago but was denied a clear run; needs to be back in peak form to have any chance.
runners.comment: 2026 has started in downbeat fashion but today's ground could prompt something better
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Alpha Crucis
runners.spotlight: All four of his wins were on soft or heavy ground, three at Goodwood; runner-up on his only two visits here, including at this meeting (good to soft) last year and he's 1lb lower today; that was over 8.5f and his creditable fifth on reappearance ten weeks ago was in the 1m Lincoln at Doncaster, but he stays 1m2f; strong each-way claims.
runners.comment: Stays 1m2f, has proved himself here and goes well in the mud; strong each-way claims

runners.name: Hand Of God
runners.spotlight: Won on good to soft in 2024, unraced on soft; lightly raced for his age but led in the final 1f of 11-runner race at Newbury (1m2f, good) three weeks ago and scored by 2l going away; back up 5lb; how he'll take to this new track and possible soft ground are greater worries but he makes appeal otherwise.
runners.comment: Won by 2l going away at Newbury (1m2f, good) three weeks ago; unraced here or on soft

runners.name: Auld Toon Loon
runners.spotlight: Failed to figure from the widest draw behind Respond at Chester (went well there previously) on reappearance but a close third of six when favourite at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) 11 days ago; with a good soft-ground record too, he could be a major player.
runners.comment: Close third at Windsor 11 days ago; with a good soft-ground record, he could be a player

runners.name: Bolster
runners.spotlight: Four wins in 2024 included this race on good to soft, with the other three on soft; performed near his best only a couple of times last season, which has been followed by gelding and wind operations; also now wears first-time headgear; some warning signals and he's not slipped far in the weights.
runners.comment: Won this 2024; a lesser 2025 was followed by gelding/wind operations; first headgear too
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Dhahabi
runners.spotlight: Ended his time with Charlie Appleby on a low note (March 2024) and no revival in his four races at Meydan for another yard the following winter; has switched stables again and needs to be back to his best after 483 days off.
runners.comment: Has switched stables again and needs to be back to his best after an absence of 483 days

runners.name: Legacy Link
runners.spotlight: All four starts on good to firm/good ground; rallied for fourth in a hot running of the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket last October on her final 2yo run, and she reappeared with a hard-fought win in the Group 3 Musidora at York (10.2f), asserting close home; there is every chance that she will stay 1m4f and her pedigree provides optimism that she will be effective on slow ground; dam is a sister to Frankel; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Asserted close home in the Group 3 Musidora at York and she's firmly in calculations

runners.name: Venetian Lace
runners.spotlight: 0-6 since debut win on AW last June and unproven on slow ground; however, she was a fine second in a red-hot running of the Group 1 Fillies' Mile at Newmarket last October and reappeared with an excellent third in the 1,000 Guineas there (1m) last month; made the running on her last two starts; by Derby winner Masar and rallied in the closing stages last time, so it's possible that this sizeable step up in trip will suit; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Rallied for 3rd in 1,000 Guineas and it's possible this sizeable step up in trip will suit
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Amelia Earhart
runners.spotlight: Built upon previous promise (including on soft ground) when easily winning a maiden at Leopardstown (1m, yielding) last October on her fifth start, and reappeared with 2l win in Listed Cheshire Oaks at Chester (11.3f, good) where she wore a hood and blinkers for the first time (retained today); she's followed the same route on her last two races as Minnie Hauk, who won this for Ballydoyle 12 months ago, and Ryan Moore rides; major player.
runners.comment: Won the Listed Cheshire Oaks by 2l and is following same route as 2025 winner Minnie Hauk
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Thundering On
runners.spotlight: By Frankel and dam won the 1m2f Group 1 Pretty Polly for connections; she's improved with each of her four starts and comfortably won the Group 3 Salsabil Stakes at Navan (1m2f, good to yielding) in April when upped in trip; has shown promise on heavy ground; powered clear in the final furlong last time, providing hope that she will stay 1m4f, and she's one to consider.
runners.comment: Powered clear in 1m2f Group 3 at Navan in April when upped in trip; she's one to consider

runners.name: A La Prochaine
runners.spotlight: She has an attractive German pedigree and was bought for 550,000gns as a yearling; won at Newbury (1m, soft) last October on sole 2yo start and beaten about 3l by Amelia Earhart when third in the Listed Cheshire Oaks at Chester (11.3f, good) on last month's reappearance; she still looked a work in progress last time (slowly away and outpaced turning for home) and could have lots more to offer; slow ground might aid her cause; interesting.
runners.comment: Still looked a work in progress when third to Amelia Earhart in Cheshire Oaks; interesting

runners.name: Cameo
runners.spotlight: 7f maiden winner (soft to heavy) who took a big step forward with a convincing win in the Listed Oaks Trial at Lingfield (11.6f, good to firm) last month on her sixth start, which was her first run beyond 1m; Ryan Moore was on board last time and rides stablemate Amelia Earhart this afternoon, but she was certainly impressive at Lingfield and is unexposed at the trip; chance.
runners.comment: Impressive in Lingfield trial; has a chance even though Ryan Moore rides Amelia Earhart
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: K Sarra
runners.spotlight: Sister to connections' 10.2f/1m4f Group 2 winner Pride Of Arras; comfortable win at Southwell (1m, AW) last November on sole 2yo start and the drop to 7f was against her in the Fred Darling at Newbury (good) on her return; left that run well behind when staying on for third in the Group 3 Musidora at York (10.2f, good) last month, beaten just over 2l by Legacy Link; looks set to relish this step up in trip and she could be a serious force.
runners.comment: Stayed on for third in the Musidora and could be a serious force over this longer trip

runners.name: Sugar Island
runners.spotlight: Third in Group 2 May Hill at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last September and readily made all in Group 3 Staffordstown Stud Stakes at the Curragh (1m, heavy) in October; she could improve for last month's comeback run in the Listed Cheshire Oaks at Chester (11.3f, good) but has work to do with stablemate Amelia Earhart and A La Prochaine on that form; others preferred.
runners.comment: Made all in 2yo Group 3 last October; work to do with two of these on Chester form
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: On Message
runners.spotlight: Won Wolverhampton novice (7f, AW) last October on third start and followed up on handicap/seasonal debut here (8.5f, good) in April; kept on strongly for 1l third in Listed Height Of Fashion Stakes at Goodwood (1m2f, good) 13 days ago; she has plenty to find on form but the step up in trip could prompt further improvement and she might not be a forlorn hope each-way.
runners.comment: Strong finish for Listed third over 1m2f at Goodwood; might not be a forlorn hope each-way

runners.name: Asmen Warrior
runners.spotlight: Won a Salisbury maiden (1m2f, good to firm) last June when sporting first-time cheekpieces for previous yard; yet to score for current stable but he's been placed on six of those seven starts and it was a narrow defeat when second of ten at Windsor (1m, good to firm; visored) under Silvestre De Sousa latest; soft going would ask a question but he's well drawn to go forward and newly fitted blinkers might eke out more.
runners.comment: Soft conditions would ask a question but otherwise could go well in new headgear

runners.name: Ozat
runners.spotlight: Ex-French gelding who won four times there, the last two coming over 8.5f (AW) and 1m1f (good) at Chantilly last spring; changed hands for 75,000euros in November; 80-1 when 19th of 24 at Newbury (1m, good) in April on his British debut and he's probably one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Ex-French; 80-1 when down the field (1m, good) on his British debut

runners.name: Mister Winston
runners.spotlight: Unraced on ground softer than good; Chester maiden (7.5f) winner who made all on Newmarket's July course last August; had been gelded when successfully repeating those tactics on his comeback at Newmarket (also 1m) in April but below that form back there at the Guineas meeting the following month; not dismissed, with Oisin Murphy sticking with him.
runners.comment: Made all on return; not so good latest; unraced on softer than good but Murphy keeps faith

runners.name: Bragbor
runners.spotlight: He made his debut only as a 4yo but it was a winning one at Newcastle (1m); subsequently useful form on AW included another narrow win at that venue (1m2f) in August for George Scott and he went mighty close over latter C&D on stable debut in November; niggled along at various stages when seventh on his turf debut (10.5f) last month and some guesswork is involved as to whether this test will bring out the best in him.
runners.comment: Useful on AW but only seventh on turf debut last time; plenty of guesswork involved

runners.name: Mr Swivell
runners.spotlight: Proved progressive last year having joined this yard, winning three times (good to firm/AW) including when making all at Wolverhampton (8.5f) in July; also ran really well when second of 17 at Ascot (1m, soft; off current mark) in September but he was beaten a long way on his final start of that term and again at Lingfield on his reappearance four weeks ago; needs to put those efforts behind him but that's not out of the question.
runners.comment: Can go forward; has to put poor return behind him but that's not impossible

runners.name: Walsingham
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner who landed a third success at Pontefract (1m, good) on his penultimate start, having attracted support; also effective on a softer surface than that but it was this mark he raced from when well held at York three weeks ago and this demands a career best.
runners.comment: It was this mark he raced from when well held at York three weeks ago; career best needed

runners.name: Final Night
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner who is 0-6 on turf but his 40-1 second of 13 at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) was a good effort in May and he backed it up when third to Sterling Knight over that C&D (good) a fortnight later; sole start on good to soft (1m2f) last year was inconclusive but he'll do well to defy this career-high mark.
runners.comment: Good efforts on turf last two starts; career-high mark to overcome though

runners.name: Man Of La Mancha
runners.spotlight: Gelded before winning at Windsor (good to firm) last May and finished that season with further wins at Sandown (also 1m, soft) and Newmarket (1m1f, good to firm); on the premises when he suffered chance-ending interference at Newbury last time and this tactically versatile sort is well drawn to sit handy; interesting.
runners.comment: Hampered latest; tactically versatile sort who is well drawn to sit handy; interesting

runners.name: Celeborn
runners.spotlight: Won on good to soft as a juvenile and reappeared with good-ground C&D success at last year's Spring meeting; that was his only start in 2025 and he made a respectable comeback despite hanging when fifth of 11 at Newbury (1m2f) three weeks ago; drops back down in trip with cheekpieces added and his best days are almost certainly ahead of him; player.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fair return and drops back in trip in cheekpieces; unexposed; he's a player
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Principality
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner of a Glorious Goodwood handicap (1m) last July and he had dipped below that winning mark when scoring at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last time; 2lb nudge shouldn't stop him being competitive.
runners.comment: Heavy-ground winner who won last time; 2lb nudge shouldn't stop him being competitive

runners.name: Stem
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin win (7f, heavy) on his sole start at two promised plenty and, after being campaigned over 1m2f and 1m4f, he recorded a clear career best when second of 15 over 1m at Newbury (good to soft) on his final start at three; subsequently gelded and his reappearance when beaten less than 4l at Newbury in April was perfectly respectable; unexposed and he's respected.
runners.comment: Good second (1m, good to soft) on final start at three; respectable return; unexposed

runners.name: Popmaster
runners.spotlight: Seven wins include three on good to soft/soft; also won on AW at Kempton (1m) in February but only midfield in valuable events at Newcastle (AW) and Ascot (good) on his last two starts; however, latter effort was better than it looks having been poorly drawn.
runners.comment: Last effort, from poor draw, was better than it looks

runners.name: Jimmy Speaking
runners.spotlight: Acts on soft ground; better than ever this season, winning at Doncaster (good to soft) and following up at Southwell (again 7f, AW); solid second to Sterling Knight (1m, good) last time but he'll need more from the same mark.
runners.comment: Better than ever this season but he'll need more from this mark

runners.name: Sterling Knight
runners.spotlight: Versatile regarding ground and he was winning his 12th race when beating Jimmy Speaking and Final Night at Newmarket (1m, good) three weeks ago; that was a comfortable win and he has the assistance of Ryan Moore but this demands more from him (has never won off a mark this high).
runners.comment: Won 12th race when scoring last time; has never won off a mark this high, though

runners.name: Nizam
runners.spotlight: He has a similar profile to stablemate Ozat, boasting good form in France but 80-1 when down the field in that same Newbury race on his British debut; others look more likely.
runners.comment: Similar profile to stablemate Ozat; others look more likely

runners.name: Hot Cash
runners.spotlight: Acts on soft going; picked up a maiden here (7f, good) in July 2024 on his debut; fair run of form last year came to a crashing halt when virtually tailed off over C&D (good to soft) in September; subsequently gelded and he was back to near his best when second of 13 at Thirsk (1m, good) last time; far from dismissed.
runners.comment: Course winner who was back near his best when second last time; far from dismissed

runners.name: Thunder Roar
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground specialist who landed a fifth success when scoring at Ayr (1m) in September; yet to get going in three starts this year but he's edging down the weights and the booking of Colin Keane catches the eye; requires monitoring in the market with every drop of rain in his favour.
runners.comment: Soft-ground specialist; down the weights; Colin Keane booking is interesting; watch market

runners.name: Mirsky
runners.spotlight: 7yo who was 6lb lower when landing this race (good to soft) last season; this year's running is more competitive but his success in the Thirsk Hunt Cup (1m, good to firm) last month was close to a career best and he had a possible excuse when failing to back that up at York; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Won this last year off 6lb lower; won penultimate start and excuse latest; not ruled out

runners.name: Stellar Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Bit disappointing in similar event at Newmarket last time but otherwise a broadly progressive colt and can't be written off; ready winner of useful handicap at Newbury (7f, good) in his only other start this term.
runners.comment: Bit disappointing last time but otherwise a broadly progressive colt

runners.name: Awaken
runners.spotlight: Ran well in Group events at Royal Ascot (6f) and Newmarket last season; posted a duck egg in the Nell Gwyn in sole run this term but looks a possible danger on these terms, provided she takes well to first-time hood.
runners.comment: Possible danger on these terms, provided she takes well to first-time hood

runners.name: Ardisia
runners.spotlight: Picked up a lot of prize-money during a busy 2yo season but isn't totally solid on 2026 efforts and has probably already reached his ceiling anyway; seems unlikely defy a 3lb penalty and top weight.
runners.comment: Not totally solid on 2026 form and has probably already reached his ceiling anyway

runners.name: Ten Carat Harry
runners.spotlight: Productive gelding, mostly over 6f and on AW; prevailed by a neck in Newmarket handicap (good to firm) back on turf last time, taking record to 7-12; has bits of 7f form, including at Listed level; in the mix.
runners.comment: Productive, mostly over 6f; has bits of 7f form, including at Listed level

runners.name: Ellusive Butterfly
runners.spotlight: AW scorer; 0-5 on turf but ran well in this year's Fred Darling and looked better than the bare form (met traffic issues) in York handicap most recently; this task seems to demand a personal best, however.
runners.comment: Has some encouraging turf form but this task demands a personal best

runners.name: Assaranca
runners.spotlight: Won well at Lingfield (7f, good to firm) most recently but that was only a handicap off a mark of 70 and everything panned out perfectly for her; has the worst chance at the weights back up in class.
runners.comment: Won well last time but has the worst chance at the weights back up in class

runners.name: Greek Mythology
runners.spotlight: Sire landed the Woodcote on this card in 2019 en route to even better things; this is a thrice-raced Irish colt who has progressive 7f form, most recently 1l third in Listed race at Naas (good); strong credentials.
runners.comment: Thrice-raced Irish colt who has progressive 7f form; sire won on this card in 2019

runners.name: Postmodern
runners.spotlight: Has two useful pieces of 6f form, namely an emphatic domestic win on debut and rallying second in Meydan handicap last time; gelded since; ready for a crack at 7f; possibilities if taking well to first-time hood.
runners.comment: Has two useful pieces of 6f form; ready for a crack at 7f; hood is fitted

runners.name: Northern Champion
runners.spotlight: Record is 3-6, most of those starts in non-domestic races; came up short in Group 1 last time when bidding for Meydan hat-trick; bit to prove back on home soil but very hard to dismiss on ratings.
runners.comment: Came up short in Group 1 last time when bidding for Meydan hat-tirck

runners.name: Dance In The Storm
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who can be difficult to settle but ran well in some quality handicaps last summer (including on good to soft; unraced on softer) and reappeared with a win at Chester (7f, good) last month; prevailed despite a wide draw and she could still have more left in the tank.
runners.comment: Won on reappearance at Chester despite wide draw; could still have more left in the tank

runners.name: Arctic Thunder
runners.spotlight: Not at the top of his game on either run this year but well backed at Chester last time, when the tight track may not have been ideal; on a handy mark on last season's best form and and he's not discounted.
runners.comment: Chester may not have suited last time; he's on a handy mark on last season's best form

runners.name: Pietro
runners.spotlight: Progressive during last season's 3yo campaign and continued the good work when winning at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in March on his reappearance; another good run when third to Dance In The Storm at Chester (7f, good) last month; versatile ground-wise and might not be far away.
runners.comment: 4yo who has continued the good work this season and might not be far away

runners.name: Son
runners.spotlight: 0-17 since winning in June 2023 on his second start and he was below par at Newmarket (7f, good) three weeks ago; however, prior to that he was runner-up on his first two starts for this yard (7f, good) and has an each-way shout if he's back on song; has shown promise on good to soft ground.
runners.comment: On a long losing run and below par last time, but made good start for new yard previously

runners.name: Zarathos
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 18th attempt at Windsor (1m, good to firm) last month and followed up in big field at York (7f, good) eight days later; latest 7lb rise may not stop this thriving 4yo if he handles the softer ground here.
runners.comment: Took a long time to get off the mark but has won his last two and is clearly thriving
runners.owner: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar

runners.name: Monarch's Gold
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo who went beyond 6f for the first time when going close at Chester (7.5f, good) last month; has also shown promise on good to soft ground; unexposed at this sort of trip and could be involved.
runners.comment: Went close over 7.5f at Chester last month on first run beyond 6f; could be involved

runners.name: Sunny Smile
runners.spotlight: Won 2yo maiden at Hamilton (6f, good) last July on third outing and runner-up there (6f, good to soft) in September on second nursery start; that latest form was franked by the winner and every chance he'll continue to improve, but this is a tough starting point to his 3yo year; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Promising 2yo last year but this is a tough starting point to his 3yo year

runners.name: The Angel King
runners.spotlight: Two-time French winner (7f, very soft/good to soft) who took a step back in the right direction with close sixth of 13 at Chester (7.5f, good) last month on his second stable start; the forecast slower ground could be a positive today and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction when sixth at Chester; forecast slower ground could be a plus

runners.name: A War Eagle
runners.spotlight: Made all at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) in April on second stable start; respectable efforts when fifth on his next two outings but he needs something extra today and is unproven on slow ground.
runners.comment: Made all at Beverley on second stable start; fifth on next two outings but needs extra

runners.name: Colombier
runners.spotlight: Won four times on slow ground in France (7f-1m2f); returned from long absence with heavy defeat on last month's stable debut at Ascot (1m, good to firm) where he pulled hard; that run may have got the freshness out of him and it will be interesting to see if he's again strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Pulled hard when well beaten on stable debut; interesting to see if his backers return

runners.name: Veblen Good
runners.spotlight: Considerable promise during light career for Karl Burke and better than ever when getting up close home in small field on his stable/seasonal debut at Pontefract (6f, good); there can be optimism that he will be fine back up to 7f and on slower ground, and he could have more to offer; contender.
runners.comment: Got up close home over 6f on stable debut and could have more to offer; contender

runners.name: Musical Angel
runners.spotlight: Beat her five rivals to make all over C&D (good to soft) last September on final run for Andrew Balding; didn't run badly on stable/seasonal debut at Kempton (6f, AW) in April and could improve for that run; this race is more competitive than she's used to but she's not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: This is more competitive than she's used to but this C&D winner is not ruled out each-way

runners.name: Rhoscolyn
runners.spotlight: Down the field in the Thirsk Hunt Cup (1m, good to firm) last month but promising reappearance there (7f, good) previously; won this race in 2021 (good to soft) and 2024 (good to soft) and was third last year (good to soft); this week's rain has been in his favour and he's on a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: Won this in 2021 and 2024 and this week's rain has been in his favour; dangerous mark

runners.name: Crimson Spirit
runners.spotlight: Two C&D wins, the latest when completing a hat-trick last July (good to firm), and he won at Goodwood (7f, good) a fortnight ago; could be bang there if handling the forecast slow ground (mixed messages in his form).
runners.comment: Two C&D wins and on the scoresheet at Goodwood a fortnight ago; could be bang there

runners.name: Mister Bluebird
runners.spotlight: 8yo who has been as good as ever this spring, short-headed at Musselburgh (7f, good) then second of 14 to Crimson Spirit at Goodwood (7f, good); last season's two wins came on soft ground; effective here; respected.
runners.comment: 8yo who has been as good as ever this spring, runner-up on last two starts; respected

runners.name: Aurora Majesty
runners.spotlight: Disappointing last October on final 3yo start but the cheekpieces adopted that day are dispensed with and he's been gelded since; won over C&D (good) last July before very good efforts in defeat at Goodwood (7f, good to soft) and over C&D (good); retains potential and could be involved.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who twice ran really well over C&D last summer; could be involved

runners.name: Cascade Hall
runners.spotlight: Soon in a clear lead and perhaps stole a four-runner race at Ayr (1m7f, good to firm) on seasonal debut, belatedly opening his account; however, he backed up that performance with a clear second at Carlisle (1m6f, soft) a fortnight later and remains well handicapped on some of last year's form.
runners.comment: Nicked Ayr race from the front last month but backed up that run with a creditable second

runners.name: Elemental Eye
runners.spotlight: Two wins last season, the latter gained by 10l here (1m6f, soft; also suited by much quicker ground) in October; not in same form on either outing since returned to the Flat in March (1m4f/2m; cheekpieces refitted last time) but still considered.
runners.comment: Yet to strike form this year but was a wide-margin course winner in October; a possible

runners.name: Velvet Whisper
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with two slow-ground wins from the front (1m2f/1m4f); not yet fully exposed but has been safely held twice on good ground this year (1m4f/1m2f) and is not bred for this new trip.
runners.comment: Ended last season in excellent form on slow ground; safely held twice this year; new trip
runners.owner: C Wright, D Kilburn, A Dick And P Snook

runners.name: Kisiyra
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden; seen only once in 2025, when fourth of six at Thirsk (1m4f, good) on stable debut a year ago; back from another layoff when weakening badly with a first-time tongue-tie at Hamilton (1m5f) on Thursday.
runners.comment: Another layoff before weakening badly with a tongue-tie at Hamilton (1m5f) on Thursday

runners.name: Simple Star
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on both Flat starts this year (1m4f/2m), and just a respectable fifth over hurdles 12 days ago; now 2lb lower than when second here (2m2f, good) last summer but needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Well held on both Flat starts this year; just a respectable fifth over hurdles last time

runners.name: Spit Spot
runners.spotlight: Won two 3m hurdles last autumn but has lost her way over jumps this year and was well beaten here (1m4f) when back on the Flat a fortnight ago, after a wind op; this longer trip ought to suit better but it still takes a leap of faith to support her.
runners.comment: Lost her way over hurdles this year and struggled on recent Flat return

runners.name: Star Of Markinch
runners.spotlight: 0-44 under all codes (including three points); gave pretty good account when fourth of nine here (1m4f, good) two starts ago and will be fine over today's trip but can't be recommended from 10lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: 0-44 under all codes (including three points); 10lb wrong; can't be recommended

races.race_name: Menzies Meander EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive)

runners.name: Time For The Moon
runners.spotlight: Well-bred colt who was close third of four in slowly run contest at Haydock (7f, good to soft; 11-10f) a fortnight ago; that form is tricky to evaluate but, if his pedigree is a guide, a stronger pace over this trip ought to show him in a better light.
runners.comment: Well-bred colt; close third of four in slowly run race on debut at Haydock

runners.name: Tribal Appearance
runners.spotlight: Second of five on last month's debut at Bath (5.7f, firm) and not beaten far when last of four (just behind Time For The Moon) in another slowly run contest at Haydock (7f, good to soft) a fortnight ago; no surprise if he's on the premises again here.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on either outing (5.7f/7f), albeit both those races were slowly run

runners.name: Heldobeldo
runners.spotlight: Out of a half-sister to an Italian Listed 2yo winner; made promising debut when close second of ten at Kempton (6f, AW) last month and has big role to play if suited by this different trip-and-ground combination.
runners.comment: Made promising debut when close second of ten at Kempton (6f, AW) last month; respected

runners.name: Odegaard
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to fair middle-distance handicapper Lethal Touch and dual 1m winner Vondelpark; dam unraced half-sister to three useful winners; already gelded; stable not known for 2yo winners.
runners.comment: Half-brother to fair 1m2f handicapper and dual 1m winner; yard not known for 2yo winners

runners.name: Mr Minz
runners.spotlight: 70,000euros yearling; second foal; closely related to 6f 2yo winner French Affair (RPR 78); dam a modest four-race maiden (64); stable has very modest strike-rate with first-time-out 2yos.
runners.comment: Closely related to a 6f 2yo winner; would be rare first-time-out 2yo winner for stable

runners.name: Amantha
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten over 5f on first two starts but her recent sixth at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) was much more encouraging; further progress will almost certainly be required here, though.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten over 5f on first two starts but recent 7.4f run was much more encouraging

runners.name: Sandy Craic
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after seven starts but was placed twice (6f/7f) before her very respectable fifth of 20 at York (7f, good) a fortnight ago; big player in this easier race if first-time cheekpieces have any positive effect.
runners.comment: Respectable fifth of 20 at York a fortnight ago; big player in this easier race

runners.name: Maximum Velocity
runners.spotlight: Made all to open account at Redcar (7f, good) last month, his eighth start, but recent 5lb rise demands a bit more from him here and his tendency to race too freely remains a nagging concern.
runners.comment: Made all at Redcar recently but his tendency to race too freely remains a nagging concern

runners.name: The Anthony Gover
runners.spotlight: Just a respectable sixth when dropped to 7f and fitted with blinkers (retained) for recent handicap debut at Wolverhampton (AW) but remains unexposed and a recent 2lb drop helps.
runners.comment: Just a respectable sixth when dropped to 7f for handicap debut but remains unexposed

runners.name: Born A Star
runners.spotlight: Won C&D (good) maiden last summer but has disappointed in all three handicaps this season (7f-8.6f) and his tendency to race too freely remains a worry; needs to get career back on track.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner last summer but disappointing in all three handicaps this year

runners.name: Stoic Poet
runners.spotlight: Two good runs this season, latterly when keeping-on second in Haydock handicap (7f, soft) a fortnight ago; ought to make his presence felt again here, and significant rain will increase his appeal.
runners.comment: Good second on soft ground last month; significant rain will make him of interest

runners.name: Yorkshire Queen
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; made quite promising seasonal debut when fourth at Southwell (7f, AW) last month but raced too freely and was disappointing when upped to 1m at Haydock (good to soft) a fortnight ago; drops back to 7f with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Raced too freely and disappointed when upped to 1m; quickly drops back to 7f

runners.name: Highland Shah
runners.spotlight: In the frame all three starts, most recently when fourth of 11 (behind Sandy Craic) on last month's seasonal debut at Catterick (7f, good; first-time hood); should have some improvement to come in handicaps; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Encouraging fourth in Catterick novice on seasonal debut; open to improvement in handicaps

runners.name: Walk On Walk On
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; posted by far his best of three runs for new stable this season when dropped back to 7f and tried in blinkers (retained) at Redcar (good) last month, finishing fourth; up in grade here and needs to build on that effort.
runners.comment: Ran quite well in new blinkers last month; needs to build on that effort here

runners.name: Wise Crack
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; bettered inauspicious seasonal/stable debut when fifth of eight at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) last month and, with this return to 7f likely to suit, could plausibly take another step forward here.
runners.comment: 0-6 but latest run was much better than stable debut, and this return to 7f will suit

runners.name: Homestrait
runners.spotlight: Dual 2yo winner (once over C&D); more exposed than some of these but ran game race from the front when close second in first-time cheekpieces at Catterick (7f, good) a fortnight ago; contender if headgear continues to aid.
runners.comment: More exposed than some of these but ran well in defeat in new cheekpieces last month

runners.name: Dingwall
runners.spotlight: Won consecutive AW races in November (9.4f/8.6f) and has been placed several times since, including a close second off today's mark at Ayr (1m1f, good to firm) in early May; needs to get back on track after two below-par runs, though.
runners.comment: Placed several times since his two AW wins in November; below form lately, though

runners.name: Amidst The Chaos
runners.spotlight: Gained both wins last August, the latter over C&D (good); denied clear run before making the frame at Ripon (1m, good) and Beverley (8.4f, good to firm) on last two appearances; can be dangerous if his luck changes.
runners.comment: C&D winner; denied clear run when making frame last two outings; dangerous if luck changes

runners.name: Starliner
runners.spotlight: Looked unlucky when third at Hamilton (8.3f, good to firm) nine days ago, where he was persistently denied a clear run, and remains just 2lb higher than when scoring at Ayr (1m, good to firm) in early May; high on the list.
runners.comment: Looked unlucky at Hamilton recently and is still only 2lb higher than for win in early May
runners.owner: David Orr & Miss L A Perratt

runners.name: Detective
runners.spotlight: 10yo who gained the latest of his eight career wins over this C&D (good; wore blinkers) last summer; soundly beaten on both outings this year but has blinkers refitted today and it won't be a shock if he stages a revival.
runners.comment: C&D winner last summer; yet to strike form this year but has blinkers refitted today

runners.name: Union Island
runners.spotlight: Two wins this year, the latter when coming from off the pace to prevail by a nose at Leicester (1m, good to firm) last month; still on a workable mark but his record is very mixed, and he didn't fire on latest outing.
runners.comment: Two wins this year, the latter last month, but his record is very mixed; hard to predict

runners.name: Poet's Dawn
runners.spotlight: Veteran who exploited a reduced mark when winning from the front at Ripon (1m2f, good) last month; held under 4lb penalty soon afterwards, though, and effectively another 1lb higher here; younger rivals appeal more.
runners.comment: Veteran; won off reduced mark last month but held since; younger rivals preferred

runners.name: Mayor Of Maghera
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on some of his old Flat form for Phillip Makin but was disappointing during four-race spell over hurdles for an Irish stable this year and has quite a bit to prove on debut for Mike Smith.
runners.comment: Disappointing over hurdles this year and has quite a bit to prove on stable debut

runners.name: Motawaared
runners.spotlight: Did not get seriously competitive when seventh on first two starts this season (1m4f/1m1f) and is now 0-8 overall; worth a glance in the betting but others have much more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Did not get seriously competitive when seventh on first two starts this season (1m4f/1m1f)

runners.name: Tiva
runners.spotlight: Built upon satisfactory seasonal debut when second of six in refitted tongue-tie/visor at Hamilton (5f, good to firm) nine days ago; still on a fairly tough mark but can make her presence felt again.
runners.comment: Remains on a fairly tough mark but posted sound effort when second at Hamilton recently

runners.name: Lion's House
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner; went close on Tapeta at Newcastle (5f) last month but finished well down the field either side on turf and only fourth of six at Hamilton on Thursday; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner; only fourth of six at Hamilton on Thursday; not the percentage call

runners.name: Little Mi Mi
runners.spotlight: Resurgent mare who has collected three wins this year, two on Newcastle AW and the latest at Ayr (5f, good to firm) last month; they were Class 6 races, though, and she was soundly beaten when recently upped to Class 3.
runners.comment: Resurgent mare who has collected three Class 6 wins this year; well held in recent Class 3
runners.owner: N Irving, F Steele & I Jardine

runners.name: Right And Exact
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who led close home to take 17-runner Thirsk handicap (5f, good) three weeks ago and is now 2-5; probably still has more to offer and commands respect.
runners.comment: Led close home to take 17-runner Thirsk handicap last month and is now 2-5

runners.name: I'm Next
runners.spotlight: Showed career-best form when winning comfortably at Beverley (5f, good) in April and again ran well when fourth of 12 in quite valuable C&D (good) contest three days later; will beaten in good-quality York handicap since but back in calmer waters here and goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Good winner at Beverley in April and ran well in quite valuable C&D contest next time

runners.name: Miss Brazen
runners.spotlight: Made winning seasonal debut under Lewis Chalkley at Nottingham (5f, good) in April and ran at least as well when close third at Beverley (5f, good to firm) last month; up in grade today but evidently in good nick.
runners.comment: Scored on seasonal debut and ran at least as well when close third next time; up in grade

runners.name: Woohoo
runners.spotlight: Gained two of his five wins over C&D, the latter on good ground in April; didn't run badly in a big field at Thirsk (5f, good) on Sunday but current mark demands a career-best performance.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; in good form but current mark demands a career-best effort

runners.name: Curious Rover
runners.spotlight: Gained last season's win over C&D (good); slightly disappointing in first-time blinkers (retained) on AW a week ago but now 2lb lower than when fourth of 17 behind Right And Exact at Thirsk (5f, good) two starts ago.
runners.comment: Slightly disappointing on AW last week but previous turf run gives him each-way claims
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Lope Y Linda
runners.spotlight: Scored off basement mark at Catterick (7f, good) a fortnight ago, opening her account at the ninth attempt; should cope with this return to 1m and further progress is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Scored off basement mark at Catterick (7f) last month; further progress possible

runners.name: Mereside Princess
runners.spotlight: Came good at 13th attempt, making all to fend off warm favourite at Carlisle (7.8f, soft) last month; should be fine on the forecast faster ground here and recent 4lb rise does not look excessive.
runners.comment: Off mark on 13th start, making all at Carlisle (7.8f, soft) in May; 4lb rise not excessive

runners.name: Royal Baroness
runners.spotlight: Struggled on all three 2yo starts but made quite promising seasonal/handicap debut at Carlisle (7.8f, soft) in May, keeping on from the rear for fourth behind Mereside Princess; contender if that run brought her on.
runners.comment: Kept on for fourth behind Mereside Princes on recent seasonal/handicap debut

runners.name: Longevity
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on both 2yo starts and probably didn't achieve huge amount when well-held third of five in Beverley maiden (7.4f, good to firm) won easily by 2-13 favourite last month; open to improvement in handicaps, though.
runners.comment: Didn't achieve much when well-held third last time but open to improvement in handicaps

runners.name: Saxon Gem
runners.spotlight: Found things happening too quickly before eventually keeping on for fairly respectable sixth of nine on handicap debut at Carlisle (6f, good) a week ago; could plausibly be seen in a better light over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Found things happening too quickly when sixth over 6f onn handicap debut; up in trip here

runners.name: Bobbys Pride
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all four 2yo starts, most recently when last of nine over C&D (good to soft) on nursery debut in September; needs to have made good progress during 264-day break.
runners.comment: Last of nine over C&D on nursery debut in September and not seen since

races.race_name: Hartshead Dos Champions Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Circle Of Trust
runners.spotlight: 0-6 and disappointing in handicaps on both starts this season (7f/6f, good to firm/good); however, she was runner-up in a nursery at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) last September and it's possible the return to slow ground will trigger a revival; he's a player if so.
runners.comment: Disappointing on both starts this season but he's a player on his slow-ground 2yo form
runners.owner: Mrs R G Hillen And Partner

runners.name: Eagle Bay
runners.spotlight: Inexperience evident when third of ten at 6-1 at Catterick (7f, good) last October on sole run for Ed Walker; a leading contender if tapping into that promise on this stable and seasonal debut; from a family his new trainer has done very well with.
runners.comment: Promise on sole 2yo start and new trainer has done very well with his family

runners.name: Soda
runners.spotlight: Green when third of six on debut at Catterick (7f, good) in April; the drop to 6f looked against her at Ripon (6f, good) next time and she might have an each-way shout back up in trip.
runners.comment: Green when third over 7f on debut; 6f looked against her latest; might have each-way shout

runners.name: Ma Leila
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth on debut at Kempton (1m, AW) last October; failed to back that up at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and absent since; however, she may be able to bounce back with a bold show following a break.
runners.comment: Promising 4th on debut last October; failed to back it up in December but not written off

runners.name: Ice Show
runners.spotlight: Weak in the market and soundly beaten in fifth this spring on her first two starts (1m/1m1f, AW/good to firm); drops in trip; improvement needed.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in fifth this spring on first two starts and improvement is needed

runners.name: Kyrenia
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to the stable's top 7f/1m handicapper Kynren; no match for the winner when 20-1 for recent debut at Ripon (6f, good) but kept on into third and could be sharper for that outing; she's one to consider.
runners.comment: Half-sister to stable's Kynren; kept on for 3rd on debut and could be sharper for that run

runners.name: Revolutionelle
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to 5.5f-7.5f winner Golden Pharaoh (RPR 83); dam placed 5f/6f (73), out of unraced half-sister to US Horse of the Year Havre De Grace; market check advised on debut but there's a suspicion this 4yo will be best watched.
runners.comment: Market check advised on debut but there's a suspicion this 4yo will be best watched

runners.name: Dolly Golightly
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on 2yo debut at Redcar (5f, good) last April and same story at Catterick (6f, good) a fortnight ago on her sole run since.
runners.comment: Well beaten on both starts, with a long absence in between

runners.name: Rake Lane
runners.spotlight: 37,000gns foal, 20,000gns yearling; second foal; brother to 7f AW/1m2f turf winner Titainium (RPR 77); dam unraced half-sister to winners Juan Elcano (1m2f Listed) and Nkosikazi (1m2f AW Group 3); makes belated debut but in a modest race and no surprise to see a good run for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Makes belated debut but in modest race and no surprise to see a good run for in-form yard

runners.name: Brash
runners.spotlight: Very slowly away when seventh of ten at 50-1 on recent debut at Doncaster (6f, good); however, in the circumstances he did reasonably well and there's potential in his pedigree; could be a contender if breaking on terms.
runners.comment: Blew the start on debut at Doncaster and did pretty well in the circumstances; chance

races.race_name: Hartshead Dos Champions Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Big Shot Veto
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who has been placed in both turf attempts, latest at Catterick (7f, good) on seasonal debut; leading contender in this field, assuming he takes well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Placed in both turf attempts; leading contender, assuming he takes well to headgear

runners.name: Indefensible
runners.spotlight: Proving inconsistent but is one of the main players on best form, namely placed efforts at Newcastle (6f, AW) last summer and Beverley (7.5f, good to soft) on penultimate start.
runners.comment: Proving inconsistent but is one of the main players on best form (two placed efforts)

runners.name: You Will See
runners.spotlight: Trailed home last of five at Musselburgh, failing to back up his Southwell AW effort; something to prove in another turf event.
runners.comment: Last of five at Musselburgh, failing to back up his Southwell AW effort

runners.name: Burrows Silver
runners.spotlight: Silverwave half-sister to AW winners Burrows Seeside (6f; RPR 76) and Allez Burrows (French 6.5f; 76); dam French 5f turf/6.5f AW 2yo scorer (95); sole newcomer in the field; market helpful.
runners.comment: Silverwave half-sister to two AW winners; sole newcomer in the field; market helpful
runners.owner: Mrs C Casterton & Mrs J Morgan

runners.name: Tip Foster
runners.spotlight: Stablemate of Sand Sea; showed ability with third of nine, best of the newcomers, in Pontefract contest (6f) two months ago; pedigree suggests he'll stay 7f; possibilities.
runners.comment: Third of nine, best of the newcomers, in Pontefract contest two months ago; possibilities

runners.name: Ziggy Bay
runners.spotlight: Brings the worst form, having recorded a meagre RPR in 6f contest at Ripon.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form, having recorded a meagre RPR in 6f contest

runners.name: Leelawadee
runners.spotlight: Shaped quite encouragingly at Pontefract last time but has modest RPRs and may be more interesting in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Has modest RPRs; may be more interesting in handicaps shortly

runners.name: Moving Rubber
runners.spotlight: Never better than midfield in 7f maiden at Doncaster, recording a modest RPR, but may improve with the experience under her belt.
runners.comment: Recorded a modest RPR at Doncaster but may improve with the experience under her belt

runners.name: Secretinthesky
runners.spotlight: Holds weak claims on her Newcastle AW efforts last November; change of surface needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Weak claims on her Newcastle AW efforts
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho

runners.name: Sand Sea
runners.spotlight: Sole 4yo in this field; showed promise in Catterick maiden (6f) just over a year ago, while leaving the impression this extra furlong will suit; the market may offer further guidance.
runners.comment: Showed promise at Catterick just over a year ago; market may offer further guidance

runners.name: Keep The Gold
runners.spotlight: Maiden (0-7); showed plenty of speed but run out of it late at Ayr (5f, good to firm; 13-2) recently; that was his first run since last June, so he's entitled to strip fitter here; runner-up twice since introduced to cheekpieces; possible.
runners.comment: Maiden (0-7); plenty of speed but run out of it late at Ayr (5f, good to firm) latest.

runners.name: Miss Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Won this last year (first-time visor) and quickly added to that with further wins at Catterick (7f), Newcastle (6f), and back here (6f), rising steadily in the weights; she's a likely pace angle, only worn down inside final furlong at Catterick (6f; 17-2) 25 days ago.
runners.comment: Won this last year (first-time visor); pace angle; worn down late at Catterick (6f) latest

runners.name: Beerwah
runners.spotlight: Won at Redcar (5f, good to firm; beating Zuffolo and Kyber Crystal, from 5lb lower) on penultimate start but wasn't in the same form at Hamilton subsequently, possibly inconvenienced by the draw; won on soft last year, so unlikely to mind whatever the weather does.
runners.comment: Won at Redcar (5f; 5lb lower) penultimate; not in the same form at Hamilton subsequently.

runners.name: Ninety Nine
runners.spotlight: 2-29; her best form is on soft, even heavy, ground; runner-up at Catterick (5f, soft) last October; hasn't offered much in four starts since, finishing seventh (two places behind Kyber Crystal) at odds of 50-1 at Carlisle latest.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Catterick (5f, soft) last October; hasn't offered much in four starts since.

runners.name: Irish Dancer
runners.spotlight: Consistent this year (form figures 22731); picked off the weakening pacesetter to score at Catterick (5f, good; 2-1 favourite) latest; slightly more required from this 3lb higher mark but does have a C&D win to his name; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Picked off weakening pacesetter to score at Catterick (5f, good; 2-1 fav); now 3lb higher.

runners.name: Without Flaw
runners.spotlight: Two of her three wins have come at Beverley (5f, good to firm/soft) and she has dropped back below her last winning mark; ran well on seasonal reappearance at Wetherby (5.5f; third) but had no in-running luck at Beverley subsequently; isn't a forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Ran well on seasonal reappearance at Wetherby (5.5f; third); no in-running luck latest.

runners.name: Harry Mole
runners.spotlight: Low key (peak RPR 55); hasn't that many miles on the clock (0-5) but failed to figure at Carlisle (5f, good; 22-1, fifth of ten, beaten 7l) latest; is easing slowly in the weights.
runners.comment: Hasn't many miles on the clock (0-5) but failed to figure at Carlisle (5f; 22-1) latest.

runners.name: Aurora's Doublesix
runners.spotlight: Lowly rated 15-race maiden; hasn't beaten a rival in her last three appearances (all 5f) so she's virtually impossible to consider.
runners.comment: Lowly rated 15-race maiden; she's virtually impossible to consider.

runners.name: Invincible Crown
runners.spotlight: Yet to win on turf (0-14) but has won three on AW at Wolverhampton (5f; marks between 55 and 71); had every chance, and no excuses when fourth of eight at Musselburgh (5f, good; from 2lb higher) a fortnight ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: Yet to win on turf (0-14); no excuses, fourth of eight at Musselburgh (5f, good) latest.
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing VIII & Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Toptime
runners.spotlight: Made all at Nottingham (5f, good; 3-1 favourite; from 2lb lower) last August, posting his seventh win; disappointing on soft at Pontefract next time, and curled up late when last in action at Southwell (5f; fourth) 217 days ago; positively drawn for one who likes to attack.
runners.comment: Curled up late when last in action at Southwell (5f) 217 days ago; is positively drawn.

runners.name: Kyber Crystal
runners.spotlight: 5-55; outran her double-figure odds when third to Beerwah and Zuffolo on seasonal reappearance at Redcar (5f, good); she's failed to build on that since, only fifth (runner-up a winner since) back there ten days ago; is due to ease a further 2lb in the weights.
runners.comment: Third to Beerwah & Zuffolo on seasonal reappearance at Redcar; has failed to build on that

runners.name: Zuffolo
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark at Newcastle (5f; 7-1; first-time visor) last September but mostly disappointing since until chasing home Beerwah at Redcar (5f, good to firm; 16-1) five weeks ago; is 5lb better off with that rival here; one of two for trainer Michael Dods.
runners.comment: Chased home Beerwah at Redcar (5f, good to firm; 16-1) five weeks ago; now 5lb better off.
runners.owner: R R D Saunders And Partner

runners.name: Sherlock
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough at Newcastle (5f) in February and fairly consistent since; couldn't master Yes I'm Mali over C&D (runner-up; 18-1) on penultimate start and boxed on from further back (fourth) at Beverley subsequently; a possible.
runners.comment: Couldn't quite master Yes I'm Mali over C&D (runner-up) on penultimate start; a possible.

runners.name: Fan Mail
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner (6f); fitted with cheekpieces and just held by the Michael Dods-trained Call Me Betty at Carlisle (6f, good to soft) 18 days ago; raised 1lb since but merits consideration with sterner headgear (blinkers) entering the equation.
runners.comment: Just held at Carlisle (6f, good to soft) 18 days ago; blinkers now replace cheekpieces.
runners.owner: L Bond/Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi/Ptr

runners.name: East Tyrone
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner (2-29); placed six times on AW since narrow win at Wolverhampton (6f; 18-1) in early January; failed to figure from a wide draw at Catterick (7f) latest so needs to bounce back; more appeal than stablemate Kitaab.
runners.comment: Placed six times on AW since narrow win at Wolverhampton (6f; 18-1) in early January.
runners.owner: Alan And David Fairhurst And P Kirby

runners.name: Elvetham
runners.spotlight: Justified market support when scoring in added blinkers at Lingfield (6f AW, 5-1; from 5lb lower) in April; similar efforts (on RPRs) since, having to settle for minor honours at Kempton and Windsor; has a shout from a handy high draw.
runners.comment: Scored in added blinkers at Lingfield (6f AW) in April; similar efforts (on RPRs) since.

runners.name: Bibendum
runners.spotlight: Took until the 21st attempt to get off the mark at Redcar (6f, good to firm) last August; acquitted himself well enough on AW at Newcastle over the winter, justifying favouritism there (5f) last November; monitor only for now though, returning to action off a four-month break.
runners.comment: Acquitted himself well enough on AW at Newcastle over the winter; monitor only off a break

runners.name: Woodhay Whisper
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Jack Jones, both times over 6f at Wolverhampton; hung right and weakened when last in action at Southwell (5f) ten weeks ago; changed hands for 3,600gns since; best watched on stable debut.
runners.comment: Formerly with Jack Jones; changed hands for 3,600gns; best watched on stable debut.

runners.name: Annie Edson Taylor
runners.spotlight: Has shown her best form at Hamilton since joining Phil Makin, winning in added blinkers there (similar mark) last September; did well to finish fourth (met trouble) on seasonal reappearance at Newcastle but took backward step there next time; is capable if fully firing.
runners.comment: Fourth (met trouble) on seasonal reappearance at Newcastle; backward step there next time.

runners.name: Teddy Shaw
runners.spotlight: Struggled to make his mark, often slowly away, in three 6f AW maidens at Newcastle; switches to turf for handicap debut off a ten-week absence; plenty to prove for the time being.
runners.comment: Struggled to make mark in three 6f AW maidens at Newcastle; set for handicap & turf debut.

runners.name: Taygar
runners.spotlight: 4-30; rated 5lb lower than when last successful at Ayr (6f, good to soft) last July; has been knocking on the door lately, runner-up on last two starts, including under Paul Mulrennan at Ayr; should have a positive role to play.
runners.comment: Knocking on the door lately, twice runner-up, including under Paul Mulrennan; player.

runners.name: Kitaab
runners.spotlight: No win since October 2024 (Newcastle); weakened quickly on turf return at Doncaster (7f) three weeks ago; the only real glimpse of life this year was when a never-nearer fourth at Southwell (5f; 25-1) in February; stablemate of East Tyrone.
runners.comment: No win since Oct 2024 (Newcastle); weakened quickly on recent turf return at Doncaster.

runners.name: Imperial Guard
runners.spotlight: Yet to win for this trainer (0-11) and readily outpaced on recent seasonal reappearance at Carlisle (5f, good; eighth of ten); visor added - has won blinkers in the past - seeking a revival on first venture into a Class 6 handicap.
runners.comment: Yet to win for this trainer (0-11); readily outpaced on seasonal reappearance; visor on.

races.race_name: Ruby Lodge Care Home In Thirsk Handicap

runners.name: King Of Berkshire
runners.spotlight: Knuckled down to score at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm; 5-6 favourite) on penultimate start and in the mix from this revised mark at Windsor (11.5f; third) subsequently; few concerns about 1m4f around this sharp track, so this looks a good opportunity to add to his tally.
runners.comment: Redcar winner (1m2f); in the mix at Windsor since and this looks a good opportunity.

runners.name: Forever Perfect
runners.spotlight: Productive on AW this spring (form figures 3122); career best when runner-up to the progressive Kakirra (won twice since) at Wolverhampton (1m4f; from 2lb lower) four weeks ago; needs to prove herself conclusively on turf but this sharp left-handed track looks suitable.
runners.comment: Career best when second to progressive Kakirra (won twice since) at Wolverhampton latest.

runners.name: Myrrh
runners.spotlight: Carried persistently left when awarded the race at Hamilton (8.5f, maiden) last September; twice placed since, including when third on handicap debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) in April; latest Redcar fourth was broadly similar on RPRs; new trip but considered.
runners.comment: Hamilton maiden winner (8.5f); twice placed since, including at Doncaster (1m2f) in April.

runners.name: Fareenar
