runners.spotlight: Placed in a Redcar novice (1m, good; 16-1) last October but has gone backwards since introduced to a tongue-tie and sent handicapping; 2lb lower than when sixth of nine at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) latest; new trip.
runners.comment: Has gone backwards since introduced to tongue-tie and sent handicapping

runners.name: Rumbustious
runners.spotlight: Stayed on into third in a Chelmsford maiden (1m2f, AW) in March and again lacked the necessary gears when fourth of 11 at Haydock (1m2f, good to soft; 20-1) a fortnight ago on second handicap start; still unexposed and this extra two furlongs is worth a go.
runners.comment: Lacked the necessary gears when fourth of 11 at Haydock (1m2f; 20-1) latest; new trip.

runners.name: Wave Power
runners.spotlight: Initial promise when a never-nearer fourth to Myrrh at Hamilton (8.5f, soft) last September; gelded over the winter and struggled to get competitive on handicap debut at Southwell (1m3f, AW; 40-1) five weeks ago, beaten over 15l; others are more obvious candidates.
runners.comment: Struggled to get competitive on handicap debut at Southwell (1m3f; 40-1) five weeks ago.

races.race_name: David Ritchie Is 50! Happy Birthday! Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Military Girl
runners.spotlight: Built on her reappearance when beating her five rivals at Ayr (5f, good to firm) 16 days ago; won a maiden on good to soft in 2024 and has claims back up 5lb.
runners.comment: Built on her return when winning at Ayr last time (good to firm); claims back up 5lb

runners.name: Star Marian
runners.spotlight: Made all in a maiden at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) in March but she's been well held in two handicaps since; better needed back on turf.
runners.comment: Made all in a maiden at Wolverhampton in March but well held in two AW handicaps since

runners.name: Data Fata Secutus
runners.spotlight: Placed at Musselburgh (5f, good) and Redcar (5f, good to firm) in two of her four starts on turf this season; not so good in the other two, however, and she weakened tamely when a visor was added at Wetherby on Thursday.
runners.comment: 0-10; placed twice on turf as 3yo but weakened tamely when visored at Wetherby on Thursday

runners.name: Cheerleader
runners.spotlight: Won her first two starts for this yard at Redcar (good to firm) and Catterick (good to soft) last May, but she was last of eight at the latter track when last seen 291 days ago; has since had wind surgery.
runners.comment: Won twice last May but didn't beat a rival when last seen 291 days ago; wind operation

runners.name: Fille Unique
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the eighth attempt when winning at Musselburgh (5f, good) in April and went close off this mark at Redcar (5f, good to firm) 11 days ago; 2lb well in and has solid claims.
runners.comment: Off the mark when winning at Musselburgh in April and went close last time; 2lb well in

runners.name: Dragon Spin
runners.spotlight: Posted some solid efforts in handicaps prior to getting off the mark at Nottingham (5f, good) 27 days ago, making all; now 6lb higher but she's likely to give another good account.
runners.comment: Made all at Nottingham last month and now 6lb higher; can give another good account

runners.name: Pull The Rug
runners.spotlight: Improved when winning a handicap at Leicester (6f, good to soft) in September but pulled too hard at Newcastle next time; beat only one rival home at Catterick (6f, good) on her reappearance but she raced wide on that occasion and may well come on for it; drop back to 5f should be fine.
runners.comment: 1-7; raced wide at Catterick latest and may well come from that reappearance; back to 5f.

runners.name: Tanjen
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been on the AW (2-3 in that sphere); did best of the group of four at Goodwood (5f, good) on her reappearance but failed to back that up at Beverley (5f, good to firm) 24 days ago; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner; solid effort on reappearance but failed to back that up last time

runners.name: Startling
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been on the AW and she's yet to finish in the first three in four races on turf; pulled up after hitting the gates on her final start for David O'Meara and needs to take a step forward from last month's stable debut.
runners.comment: All four wins have been on the AW; down the field on her stable debut at Redcar last month

runners.name: J Street
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 13 starts came in a maiden at Catterick (5f, soft) in July 2024; shaped well when sixth of 17 at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) in March and ran another solid race on the AW at Southwell 15 days ago; contender.
runners.comment: 1-13; shaped well at Doncaster in March and another solid effort on the AW last time

runners.name: Thunder Star
runners.spotlight: Has won five times on turf on a variety of ground; did best of her group when ninth of 17 at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) on her return but failed to beat a rival home at Yarmouth 55 days ago; blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Has won five times on turf; failed to beat a rival at Yarmouth latest; switch of headgear

runners.name: Solid Bond
runners.spotlight: Placed twice at novice level, including on soft ground over this C&D on her debut, but wasn't able to progress in handicaps last year; has had a wind operation since last seen 221 days ago and she's best watched unless the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Placed twice at novice level but didn't progress in handicaps; wind operation since

races.race_name: Next Meeting Tuesday 16th June Featuring Royal Ascot Handicap

runners.name: Brave Traveller
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f nursery at Newcastle in December and went close there last time over 5f on his first start since being gelded; steps back up in trip and returns to turf; can make his presence felt.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle in December and went close there last time after being gelded; contender

runners.name: Showtown
runners.spotlight: Down the field on his handicap debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) last month but ran a bit better when fourth of 11 at Haydock (6f, soft) 15 days ago; better still needed.
runners.comment: Ran better at Haydock last time than he did on his handicap debut; better is still needed

runners.name: Different Opinions
runners.spotlight: Promising second on his debut at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in March but he's not progressed in three starts since, including over this trip at Ripon in April; makes his handicap debut with a visor now applied.
runners.comment: Promising second on his debut in March but hasn't progressed; visor now added

runners.name: Von Trotter
runners.spotlight: Second in his first three starts (6f/5f, AW) but finished down the field in a competitive race at York (5f, good) on his handicap and turf debut three weeks ago; steps back up in trip and drops in grade.
runners.comment: Second in his first three starts (AW) but well beaten on handicap debut

runners.name: Empress Olivia
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been over 6f at Ripon, the most recent in April on good to soft off 4lb lower than today; led a smaller group of five when 11th at Ascot (6f, good) 28 days ago but looks capable of bouncing back.
runners.comment: Both wins over 6f at Ripon; possibly wasn't helped by track position at Ascot last time

runners.name: Daizen
runners.spotlight: Sole win from nine starts came in a maiden at Ayr (6f, soft) in September; below that level since but he shaped a bit better than the bare result at Haydock (6f, soft) 15 days ago; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Sole win came in a soft-ground maiden at Ayr; a bit better than the bare result last time

runners.name: Real Jack
runners.spotlight: 0-5 but runner-up in a novice at Newcastle (5f, AW) in November; midfield finish on handicap debut at Southwell (6f, AW) in April and ran to a similar level there (7f) four weeks ago; drops back in trip on this return to turf.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Newcastle in November; ran to a similar level in both starts this year (AW)

runners.name: Logi Bear
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 12 starts came for Richard Hannon at Newmarket (6f, good) last May; struggled on his last few starts for that stable and he's not threatened in his two outings since joining this yard; dropped another 3lb.
runners.comment: Sole win came at Newmarket last May; not threatened in two starts since joining stable

runners.name: Hvar
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the third attempt when winning a novice at Wolverhampton (6f) in October on his final start for Ralph Beckett, but he's struggled in three handicaps since joining this stable; others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Won a 6f novice on the AW for Ralph Beckett; struggled in handicaps for this stable

runners.name: Uncertainty
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who was runner-up at Lingfield (7f, AW) on her final outing for Richard Hannon; however, she's struggled in three starts for this yard (1m/6f) and has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: 0-9; struggled in three starts since joining this stable (1m/6f) and has a bit to prove

runners.name: Napolian
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but he did finish in the first three in each of his first three starts, including over C&D in August; below his best at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) when last seen 253 days ago and has since been gelded; handicap debut.
runners.comment: 0-4; below his best at Pontefract when last seen 253 days ago and has since been gelded

runners.name: Bye Law
runners.spotlight: Won Ayr maiden and Redcar novice (6f, good/good to firm) last summer but he hasn't matched that level in handicaps since; never-dangerous sixth of 14 at Beverley (5f, good to soft) last month but well held over 7f at York 13 days ago; drops back to 6f with a place squeak.
runners.comment: Won a Ayr maiden and Redcar novice last summer but hasn't matched that level since

runners.name: Perineighs
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden but she posted a solid effort when third of nine on her handicap debut at Nottingham (5f, soft) in October; off since but should be suited by the step back up in trip.
runners.comment: Solid effort when third on her handicap debut at Nottingham (5f) in October; back up to 6f

runners.name: Dancing Destiny
runners.spotlight: Was backed on debut and finished well to win over 7f at Roscommon; 20-1, outpaced and outclassed in 6f Group 3 for fillies at Naas.
runners.comment: Finished well to win over 7f at Roscommon; outclassed in 6f Group 3 at Naas

runners.name: Florida Bay
runners.spotlight: Was stable second-string when hit the line well over this trip at Leopardstown, after having to be switched twice; looks like he'd get a mile already.
runners.comment: Stable second-string when hit the line well over this trip at Leopardstown

runners.name: Telegraph Road
runners.spotlight: Homebred from a good family; 16s on debut at Leopardstown, finished around 3l off third Florida Bay, not losing any ground on that rival up the straight; looks capable of winning races.
runners.comment: 16s on debut at Leopardstown, finished around 3l off third Florida Bay over this trip

runners.name: Apollo's Comet
runners.spotlight: Mehmas filly has Group 2 entry; E48,000 F, 47,000gns Y; first foal; dam 2m1f winner (RPR 85), half-sister to winners Keeper Of Time (7f Group 3) and Bold Impact (7f 2yo), out of Italian Listed-placed 5.5f-1m winner.
runners.comment: Mehmas filly has Group 2 entry; E48,000 F, 47,000gns Y; dam 2m1f winner (RPR 85)
runners.owner: OMEGA Racing & Seamus McGowan & Niall McInerney

runners.name: Blitogue
runners.spotlight: Space Traveller colt; dam unraced half-sister to winners Fresnel (Group-placed 1m) and Fortunino (1m7f Listed), out of unraced close relative of 1m4f/1m6f Group 3 winner Profound Beauty, half-sister to useful 7f-1m2f Flat/2m hurdle winner; led before fading in recent barrier trial over 7f.
runners.comment: Space Traveller colt from family of 1m4f/1m6f Group 3 winner Profound Beauty
runners.owner: Mrs F Cumiskey & W R Crawford

runners.name: My Antonio
runners.spotlight: Kodi Bear colt; E18,000 Y (n/s 14,000gns 2yo); half-brother to winners Kingdom Of Riches (7f 2yo; RPR 91), Sirius White (5f-1m AW; 65) and Phaidra (French 1m/1m1f); dam unraced half-sister to 5f Listed winner.
runners.comment: Kodi Bear half-brother to three winners; dam unraced half-sister to 5f Listed winner

runners.name: Always Magic
runners.spotlight: Mohaather colt cost £72,000 at the breeze-ups; closely related to 6f AW winner Cajetan (RPR 103), half-brother to winners Liberation Bay (6f 2yo/Swedish 1m inc AW; 76) and Supercontango (5f AW; 65); dam unraced half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner; yard had 2yo run well in a Group race lately; showed up well in recent barrier trial over 6f.
runners.comment: Mohaather colt cost £72,000 at the breeze-ups; showed up well in recent barrier trial

runners.name: Tina Tereshkova
runners.spotlight: Space Traveller filly; 80-1 when stood in the gates at Listowel on Saturday and never involved; forgiven that but unlikely to be competitive here.
runners.comment: Sent off 80-1 when stood in the gates at Listowel on Saturday and never involved

runners.name: Mudyrk
runners.spotlight: Bought for 100,000gns as a yearling; 13-2 for first start, early speed but weakened tamely over 6f at Navan; likely better than that, but a watching brief looks best here.
runners.comment: 13-2 for first start, early speed but weakened tamely over 6f at Navan

races.race_name: #5 General Admission At Fairyhouse On June 12th Maiden

runners.name: Suspicious Mindz
runners.spotlight: Best run was when third to pair of solid stakes-level performers in 7.5f Listed contest last year; beaten fair and square in 1m maidens this year, looking awkward under pressure when odds-on last time.
runners.comment: Third in strong Listed contest at two, but beaten fair and square in 1m maidens this year

runners.name: Zenford
runners.spotlight: All runs over 7f and placed every time; line came too soon when just denied in Curragh handicap off 75 last month.
runners.comment: All runs over 7f, placed each time; line came too soon when just denied in Curragh h'cap

runners.name: Al Haarith
runners.spotlight: Smart level of form in three runs at the Curragh last year, notably only losing second late on in 5-runner Group 2 Beresford Stakes won easily by recent Prix du Jockey Club runner-up Hawk Mountain; missed intended comeback (due to testing ground) last month, and worth noting he wasn't declared to run in a tongue-tie then; even if his mark proves to be somewhat inflated, should be winning this.
runners.comment: Smart level of form in three Curragh runs last year, notably second in Group 2 over 1m

runners.name: Solana Beach
runners.spotlight: Promising debut over 7f at Gowran; bit keen when not as good over 1m at Naas; place claims.
runners.comment: Promising debut over 7f at Gowran; bit keen when not as good over 1m at Naas; place shout

runners.name: Krasimir
runners.spotlight: Placed all three starts last year, including over 6f here on debut and over this trip (led) on final outing in September; has been gelded.
runners.comment: Placed all three runs last year, including over 6f here and over 7f (led) on latest start

runners.name: Oro Blanco
runners.spotlight: RPRs of 81 for both starts, firstly over 7f and then 1m; second-string but solid place claims, nonetheless.
runners.comment: RPRs of 81 both starts, first over 7f  and then 1m; second-string but solid place claims

runners.name: Green Amoro
runners.spotlight: Dark Angel colt; E140,000 yearling; first foal out of Que Amoro, a Listed sprint winner (five wins in all) who was runner-up to Battaash in 2020 Nunthorpe; second in a recent 7f barrier trial at Naas.
runners.comment: Dark Angel colt cost E140,000 as a yearling; second in a recent 7f barrier trial at Naas

runners.name: Capall Cuain
runners.spotlight: Homebred by Kessaar; dam 23-race maiden (runner-up 10.6f AW; RPR 78), out of maiden half-sister to winners Permission Slip (Listed-placed 1m4f) and Tidal Watch (useful 2m-2m2f hurdle).
runners.comment: Homebred by Kessaar; dam 23-race maiden (runner-up 10.6f AW; RPR 78)

runners.name: Elusive Path
runners.spotlight: Brother to winners Elusive Heights (1m; RPR 104) and Erratic Path (9.4f; 90), half-brother to winners Unterberg (1m1f-1m4f inc AW; 88) and Marmolata (7f; 69); dam 7f winner (inc 2yo/Group 3; 104), sister to 6f winner Bilderbeg; second-string on jockey bookings.
runners.comment: Homebred gelding; brother to winners Elusive Heights (1m; RPR 104) and Erratic Path (9.4f)

runners.name: Coney Major
runners.spotlight: Homebred was bought back for 10,000 euros in February; half-brother to Swedish 7f winner Lawyer Up; dam 1m winner (inc AW; RPR 102), half-sister to 1m2f/1m4f winner Dame Lucy, out of half-sister to Irish 6f 2yo Listed/US 1m1f Grade 3 winner Coney Kitty.
runners.comment: Homebred was bought back for 10,000 euros in February; half-brother to Swedish 7f winner

runners.name: Thumama Sands
runners.spotlight: Footstepsinthesand newcomer; dam 7.3f winner (RPR 77), half-sister to winners Vange (useful 6f 2yo) and Ronnie Baird (1m), is granddaughter of Irish Oaks winner Melodist.
runners.comment: Footstepsinthesand newcomer; dam 7.3f winner from family of of Irish Oaks winner Melodist

runners.name: Sands Castle
runners.spotlight: Ran well in 1m maiden first time out this year; too keen in 10.5f Sligo handicap last time.
runners.comment: Ran well in 1m maiden first time out this year; too keen in 10.5f Sligo handicap latest

runners.name: Ocean Echo
runners.spotlight: Much better on latest of three starts when second over this trip at Cork, around 5l ahead of the third who is now rated 60.
runners.comment: Second over this trip at Cork, around 5l ahead of third who is now rated 60.
runners.owner: R Scahill, S Begley, J & C Bourke

runners.name: Motta Alta
runners.spotlight: 50s, showed ability when beaten 11l over 1m at Naas, but best watched for now.
runners.comment: 50s, showed ability when beaten 11l over 1m at Naas, but best watched for now

runners.name: Due In June
runners.spotlight: Beaten 11l both starts; handicap mark after this run.
runners.comment: Beaten 11l both starts; handicap mark after this run

runners.name: Roman Sands
runners.spotlight: Big price when down the field over 1m at Gowran.
runners.comment: Big price when down the field over 1m at Gowran

runners.name: Hope N Pray
runners.spotlight: Huge prices and no show so far.
runners.comment: Huge prices and no show so far

races.race_name: Fiver Fridays At Fairyhouse Rated Race

runners.name: Ocean's Breath
runners.spotlight: Has 11lb pull for three-quarters of a length with Kindergarten from last year's debut clash at Navan; beaten at odds-on next time but he was a backward juvenile; off the mark by 3l on AW in April and runner-up now rated 82; nothing went right when favourite for premier handicap at Naas, got warm beforehand and rider decided to follow his draw up the stands' side but all the action was far side; tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Ready winner over 6f on AW; raced alone and on wrong side when fav for Naas handicap

runners.name: Morehampton
runners.spotlight: 7f winner at two and fair fifth in Listed contest over C&D; hasn't settled over 7f in both runs this term and has faded; drop back in trip a good move and will have more to offer if they can get him to race on an even keel.
runners.comment: 7f winner at two and fair fifth in Listed contest over C&D; well held both runs this term

runners.name: Substance
runners.spotlight: Was dropped out on handicap debut over 7.5f at Roscommon and luckily they went hard and he came through to easily beat a subsequent winner; only 5-1 at Curragh next time, never landed a blow over 7f after getting out of his ground at halfway; will need a good pace to aim at, so a hostage to fortune in that respect.
runners.comment: Ready winner on handicap bow over this trip at Roscommon; never involved at Curragh since

runners.name: County Carlow
runners.spotlight: Promise on AW (found 1m too far on second start) before making all in first-time cheekpieces at Sligo (6f, soft); had returning Substance well back that day; will need to have improved again to take this.
runners.comment: Promise on AW before making all in first-time cheekpieces at Sligo (6f, soft)

runners.name: Kindergarten
runners.spotlight: That bit sharper on their respective debuts when beating Ocean's Breath over 6f at Navan in October; fourth to the classy Charles Darwin in Listed race back there in April; didn't finish off in Group 3 at Naas since; comes off the bridle fairly early in her races.
runners.comment: Beat Ocean's Breath over 6f at two; fourth in Navan Listed held in Group 3 at Naas

runners.name: Luna Mia
runners.spotlight: That bit more exposed than the rest after eight starts at two; ran in some hot contests before getting off the mark in 6f Naas maiden in September and good second in Birdcatcher Nursery back there when last seen the following month; trainer feels she's best on easy ground; ran well first time out last term.
runners.comment: Had eight starts at two; won Naas maiden over 6f and good second in Birdcatcher back there

runners.name: Breaking Dawn
runners.spotlight: Solid runs at two and dropped back to 6f to win on heavy ground at the Curragh in March (form not worked out); only 10-3 for Madrid Handicap at Naas a week later, but it must have come too soon as he weakened to finish last; freshened up since and headgear left off.
runners.comment: Solid form, including 6f maiden win, before bombing out when just 10-3 for Madrid Handicap

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Race

runners.name: Gazelle d'Or
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner; scored in the Joe McGrath handicap at the Curragh (5f, yld-sft) last September off 83; excellent 1.5l second of 10 in listed heat at Naas on latest (5f, good), splitting 110 and 104 rated rivals; has won twice over 6f; dangerous to dismiss.
runners.comment: Five-time winner, excellent second in 5f listed heat at Naas latest, dangerous to dismiss

runners.name: Tango Flare
runners.spotlight: Won the first two renewals of this race in 2023 and 2024 and was runner up behind the talented King Cuan last year; won the Bold Lad (6f, yielding) last September and was then runner up in listed company at Naas; below best in two handicap starts this term but could bounce back.
runners.comment: Form figures of 112 in this race, below best in two runs this term but could bounce back

runners.name: Howd'yadoit
runners.spotlight: Last year's Ballyhane Stakes winner got off the mark for this season when landing a Navan handicap (5f, yielding) against older rivals off a mark of 91; now rated 101 and this 3yo ran well to be fourth in a listed race over this C&D last September (Alibah third); the return to 6f is fine and should go well.
runners.comment: Won the Ballyhane last year, landed 5f Navan h'cap latest, has C&D form, go well again

runners.name: Alibah
runners.spotlight: 3yo filly; won on debut at Gowran (7f, good) last September and close third in Listed race over this C&D (yielding) the following month (Howd'yadoit fourth); run may have come too soon when soundly beaten in Curragh Group 3 six days later; 18-1 and no show on seasonal return in Gowran handicap (7f, soft); needs to leave that behind.
runners.comment: Gowran maiden winner and listed placed over C&D at two, must leave seasonal return behind

runners.name: Tina's Indian
runners.spotlight: Won a conditions race on heavy ground at the Curragh (7f) last October; out of the frame in conditions race at Gowran on seasonal return (7f, soft) and again when down the field in 6f Curragh handicap latest (6f, good); not well treated by race conditions here and cheekpieces now reached for.
runners.comment: Out of frame both starts this season, not well treated by race conditions, cheekpieces on
runners.owner: C F Harrington & James John Hughes

runners.name: Little Queenie
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner including three on turf; won off 87 at Navan last August (6f, yielding); well-beaten when last seen at Dundalk in February; has gone well fresh before so worth a market check on return.
runners.comment: Eight-time winner, off since below par effort at Dundalk in Feb', has gone well fresh

runners.name: She's Too Kool
runners.spotlight: 0-5 but has ability and rated 85; far from disgraced when a 3.75l fourth of five in a Curragh Group 3 when last seen last June but that form hasn't worked out; hooded for seasonal return and probably best watched.
runners.comment: 0-5 and rated 85, form of Gr' 3 Curragh fourth hasn't worked out, hooded, best watched

runners.name: Jampa Ling
runners.spotlight: Was awarded AW maiden in February after winner was disqualified on a technicality; held in handicaps since and blinkers didn't work in 3yo contest over C&D last week; coming down the ratings quickly.
runners.comment: Held in handicaps and blinkers didn't work over C&D; coming down the ratings quickly

runners.name: Reponse Finale
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best run was when third over 6f at Cork in September, when just behind Sovereign Banter; blew the start from wide draw here last week, late headway up the rail but never threatened; low draw might be more of a hindrance if she does that again.
runners.comment: Blew the start from wide draw here latest, late headway up the rail but never threatened

runners.name: Sovereign Banter
runners.spotlight: Consistent over 6f on AW over the winter and into April; gelded and has had a break; twice runner-up on turf last year, but nasty draw here.
runners.comment: Consistent over 6f on AW over the winter and into April; twice runner-up on turf

runners.name: Unfamiliar
runners.spotlight: Only beaten 5l in higher grade on handicap debut at Naas; 9-2 next time when weakened over 7f at Gowran and no better back there latest; hooded all starts, goes without here; drop back to 6f might help and not one to give up on.
runners.comment: Only beaten 5l in higher grade on handicap debut; drop back from 7f might help

runners.name: Skillman Ave
runners.spotlight: Winner over 5f and second over 6f last summer for Gavin Cromwell; yet to place for current yard and mark in freefall; wide draw too.
runners.comment: Winner over 5f last summer for Gavin Cromwell; yet to place for current yard and wide draw

runners.name: Glen Breeze
runners.spotlight: Ran okay against her elders at Bellewstown on handicap debut over 5f; money for her last twice but to no reward, too free in blinkers latest; Hayes up and she's capable of better from trap one.
runners.comment: Money for her last twice but to no reward, too free in blinkers latest; capable of better

runners.name: Whatswrongnow
runners.spotlight: Long-standing maiden but definitely has one in him; had a clear run at things when second over C&D  last week; slightly wider now but has enough early speed to get a position; cheekpieces tried for just second time.
runners.comment: Long-standing maiden but definitely has one in him;

runners.name: Subiaco
runners.spotlight: Has early speed and won on AW over 5f on handicap debut; good run next time but last of all latest two starts.
runners.comment: Has early speed and won on AW over 5f on handicap debut; good run next time; poor since

runners.name: Luminessa
runners.spotlight: Just fair AW form, beaten 7l in 5f handicap which has worked out okay on latest outing in April; worst of the draw.
runners.comment: Just fair AW form, beaten 7l in 5f handicap which has worked out okay;  worst of the draw
runners.owner: Scott Sterling & Darragh Cuffe & Conor Keohane

runners.name: Platino Bianco
runners.spotlight: Money for AW winner when she was fitted with first-time visor at Navan last month, but only ran okay when sixth of 17; another one whose mark is dropping rapidly.
runners.comment: Money for AW winner when she was fitted with first-time visor at Navan, but only ran okay

runners.name: Mini Cotai
runners.spotlight: Placed once in five AW handicap starts; ran well when fourth at Cork (5f, soft) when less than a length off runner-up Whatswrongnow; 7-1 at Down Royal a week ago (5f, good), never got involved.
runners.comment: Fourth at Cork (5f, soft) when less than a length off runner-up Whatswrongnow

runners.name: I'm Spartacus
runners.spotlight: All six wins on AW, 0-23 on turf but second here in 2024; from similar draw, had to weave his way through from fair bit back to be fourth here last week; dropped 3lb but will need the breaks.
runners.comment: From similar draw, weaved his way through from fair bit back to be fourth here last week

runners.name: Andosky
runners.spotlight: Second on handicap debut last year but nothing since; has come down 14lb after four unplaced efforts this term.
runners.comment: Second on handicap debut last year but nothing since; has come down 14lb this term

runners.name: Bel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; six-race maiden; bit better the last twice but still beaten 8l both times.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden; bit better the last twice but still beaten 8l both times; reserve
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Sunday Sovereign
runners.spotlight: Last win was in 2023; prominent racer ran well first time out last year when third over 7.5f at Roscommon; also third over same C&D in October.
runners.comment: Prominent racer ran well first time out last year when third over 7.5f at Roscommon

runners.name: Polar Bear
runners.spotlight: Three years since last win; running well this term but slow starts are killing him; wide draw not bad thing in that respect, because he can run home down the outer.
runners.comment: Three years since last win; running well this term but slow starts are killing him

runners.name: Verhoyen
runners.spotlight: Coming towards the end of a fine career and might have a last win in him from declining mark; goes well here; didn't ping the lids as he usually does when sixth here last week, but not far behind a couple of these rivals; break will be crucial.
runners.comment: Didn't ping the lids when sixth here last week, but not far behind a couple of these
runners.owner: Paul Cullen & Joseph E Keeling & M C Grassick

runners.name: Livingston Range
runners.spotlight: First reserve; nearly three years since latest of two wins; ran well on return at Navan and was second over C&D around this time last year.
runners.comment: Ran well on return at Navan and was second over C&D around this time last year; reserve

runners.name: Monastere
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; nothing but duck eggs so far and was last of 16 on recent return over 1m.
runners.comment: Nothing but duck eggs so far and was last of 16 on recent return over 1m; reserve

runners.name: La La Lucrative
runners.spotlight: After almost six months off, money came for him in the morning at Listowel last weekend and he bolted up over 7f; up 13lb and when he tried to back up a win last year after 11-day interval, he bombed; 'bounce factor' at play here too.
runners.comment: After almost six months off, bolted up over 7f at Listowel and now 13lb higher

runners.name: Oitentinha
runners.spotlight: Had okay maiden form for Danny McLoughlin and beaten 7l in 7f AW nursery; that was back in December 2024 and not seen since; tongue-tie on and she's stuck out wide.
runners.comment: Beaten 7l in 7f AW nursery December 2024 and not seen since; tongue-tie on and poor draw

runners.name: Pierre Grosse
runners.spotlight: Rated 80 after five runs; close third off that mark in Leopardstown handicap (1m2f, gd-yld) on seasonal return in April; 4.5l second in 10.5f rated race at Naas (good) on latest; big player back in maiden company.
runners.comment: Rated 80, solid runs both starts this term in h'cap and rated race, player back in maiden

runners.name: Antigua
runners.spotlight: Rated 84; poor form at two but shaped better when fourth in maidens at Leopardstown in two runs this season; 1m4f seemed to stretch him latest when weakening under 2f out under a positive ride; tongue tie and blinkers replace cheekpieces; should go well dropped back in trip.
runners.comment: Rated 84, 1m4f too far latest, drop in trip a plus, new headgear on, should go well

runners.name: Big Cypress
runners.spotlight: Well bred and has shaped as though in need of further in both starts this season, over 7f at Limerick (yielding) and 1m at Gowran (gd-yld); steps up in trip now and his official rating of 73 might underestimate him; big player.
runners.comment: Shaped like this sort of trip would suit in two runs over shorter this season, big player

runners.name: Noble Honour
runners.spotlight: Promise in Leopardstown barrier trial last August; went close on heavy ground in a Gowran maiden (1m) last September; strip fitter for 4.25l third of 13 on seasonal return at Roscommon (10.5f, gd-yld); place chance.
runners.comment: Promise in both maidens, strip fitter for Roscommon third latest, place shout

runners.name: Trustmore
runners.spotlight: Best work at finish when beaten 4.25l on debut in a 7f Curragh maiden (good) last August; kept on well again upped to 1m on seasonal return at Leopardstown in first-time cheekpieces (retained); not bred for this trip but it's worth a go on the evidence of those first two efforts.
runners.comment: Not bred for this trip but shaped like further would suit in both starts at 7f/1m, chance

runners.name: Elzaam Express
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten at big odds in two 10.5f Roscommon maidens; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at big odds in two Roscommon maidens over 10.5f, safe to rule out

runners.name: Noble Venture
runners.spotlight: Home-bred Dandy Man gelding; dam a half-sister to yard's Group 3 winner Lady Kaya (also by Dandy Man) and Lightship (Listed-placed 1m); worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Dandy Man gelding, dam half-sister to yard's Group 3 winner Lady Kaya, check market

runners.name: Shewillow
runners.spotlight: Tamayuz filly; half-sister to winners Reeking Havoc (6f; RPR 69) and Marmalade N Toast (8.4f; 64); dam unraced half-sister to French 9.5f Listed winner Rainbow Crossing, out of 7f Group 3 2yo winner; likely best watched.
runners.comment: Tamayuz filly, half-sister to two modest winners, likely best watched

runners.name: Giordano
runners.spotlight: Cotai Glory colt; E18,000 yearling; second foal; half-brother to 7.4f winner Rogue Invader (RPR 84); dam well held 7f/1m (53), out of 1m Group 3 winning half-sister to 10.5f Group 1 winner Casual Conquest; 6th of 10 in barrier trial at Naas last month; watching brief advised.
runners.comment: Cotai Glory colt, half-brother to 7.4f winner, 6th in barrier trial, best watched

runners.name: Hydream
runners.spotlight: No impact in maidens at Naas (8f, good) and Cork (7f, sft-hvy) last month; may need a bit more time.
runners.comment: No impact in maidens at Naas and Cork last month, may need more time

runners.name: Satisfy My Soul
runners.spotlight: Solid effort on debut at Dundalk in December (1m) when a 4.25l fifth behind recent Gowran Classic runner up Mo Mhuirin; didn't back that up when beaten 21l back there in December and off since; cheekpieces on; best watched.
runners.comment: Promise on debut at Dundalk but didn't build on it, off since Dec', cheekpieces on, watch

runners.name: Dun Aonghasa
runners.spotlight: Unlikely winner based on maiden efforts at Dundalk (6f) and Gowran (1m, gd-yld); will qualify for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Unlikely winner based on efforts at Dundalk and Gowran, handicaps beckon

runners.name: Tanzanite Diamond
runners.spotlight: Debut effort at Navan (1m2f, yielding) not devoid of promise; soundly beaten upped to 1m4f at Dundalk latest; will likely be seen to better effect when sent handicapping.
runners.comment: Okay debut effort at Navan, soundly beaten at Dundalk, do better when tackling h'caps

races.race_name: Racing Again Next Friday Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Onyeisi
runners.spotlight: AW winner for Charlie Johnston; poor on debut for this in form yard at Roscommon; beaten 14.5l behind Sweet Baby Zou at Gowran four days ago (9.5f, yielding) when making late gains from the rear; much more needed to turn that form around.
runners.comment: AW winner in Britain, well behind Sweet Baby Zou on latest, much more needed

runners.name: Amavasya
runners.spotlight: 0-10 on turf and yet to trouble the judge; ran on well over 1m5f (good) at Leopardstown on latest; 1lb  lower now but not sure this sharper test will suit on that evidence.
runners.comment: 0-10 on turf, stayed on well over 1m5f on latest, shorter trip a query on that evidence

runners.name: Master Garvey
runners.spotlight: Strike rate of 1-32 on turf; sole success came on heavy ground over 7f at Naas in 2024; beaten 7.5l when fifth of 16 at Gowran on latest; well handicapped but others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: 1-32 on turf, beaten 7.5l when fifth at Gowran latest, others arrive in better form
runners.owner: Patrick M O'Brien/Larry Murray/Richard W Walsh/Derek Cummins

runners.name: Thegooseiscooked
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark at Gowran (9.5f, good) last August off 3lb lower; some decent efforts in defeat subsequently; may have needed latest run at Gowran (1m) after a 206-day break and this trip more suitable, so not discounted.
runners.comment: Won off 3lb lower last August, may have needed latest after break, not discounted

runners.name: Hiltons Pass
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in maidens at 6f-1m in March and April; 33-1 and beaten 9l on handicap debut at Ballinrobe on latest; 4lb drop not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Poor form in maidens, eased 4lb for 9l defeat on h'cap bow latest, others appeal more

runners.name: Kilgharrahs Love
runners.spotlight: Poor form in 2025 and similar story in four runs this season; beaten 28l at Dundalk on latest; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Poor form last year and so far this season, heavy defeat on AW latest, hard to fancy

runners.name: Captain Ciano
runners.spotlight: Good second at Gowran last April (9.5f, soft) off 9lb higher but that is starting to look like a flash in the pan; failed to beat a rival when 29l behind the winner at Navan on latest; drops in grade but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Has failed to build on last year's Gowran second, drops in grade but hard to fancy

runners.name: Cuckaloo Hill
runners.spotlight: Maiden was runner up in a division of this race last season for Paul Nolan off 9lb higher; decent fourth for this yard on penultimate at Ballinrobe (9.5f, gd-yld); 1m4f and softer ground not ideal latest; could bounce back.
runners.comment: Runner up in this last year off 9lb higher, trip/ground excuses latest, could rebound

runners.name: She's Ideal
runners.spotlight: Maiden but she's been running consistently this season; early traffic problems when a 4.75l third of 16 over C&D eight days ago; eased 1lb and should be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: Maiden has been in good form this season, good run over C&D latest, 1lb lower now

runners.name: Tara Na Ri
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens at 6f-9f last autumn; no impact in first two handicaps at the Curragh (8f, heavy) and Roscommon (7.5f, gd-yld); eased 4lb and drops in grade but hard to fancy on form.
runners.comment: Poor in first two handicaps, eased 4lb for latest and down in grade but hard to fancy

runners.name: Coul Dore
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in maidens or on handicap debut at Ballinrobe on latest; dropped 2lb and hood goes on; may need a bit more time and experience.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on handicap bow latest, hood now goes on, may need more time

runners.name: Sweet Baby Zou
runners.spotlight: Promise on handicap debut at Roscommon (7.5f, gd-yld) and again at Down Royal on penultimate (7f, good); step up to 9.5f and first-time cheekpieces (retained) did the trick at Gowran four days ago; 7lb penalty may not stop her from following up (declared to run 7.30 Leopardstown Thursday).
runners.comment: Off the mark upped to 9.5f at Gowran, cheekpieces retained, 7lb penalty, more to come

runners.name: Ella's Gold
runners.spotlight: 0-5; went close in first two handicaps last October, over 1m at Naas (yld-sft) and 7f at Gowran (heavy); raised 3lb for that latest effort; lacks a recent run and could do with the forecast rain arriving.
runners.comment: Went close in first two handicaps last Oct', off since, rain would boost chance

runners.name: Barretstown
runners.spotlight: Four wins on AW; sole success on the grass came at Cork (1m2f, yld) in May 2024 off 5lb higher; recent form on AW not great and well beaten in this last year; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Sole win on turf in May 2024 off 5lb higher, recent AW form not great, others preferred

runners.name: Medieval Night
runners.spotlight: Regressive since runner up on handicap debut last August; slight step in right direction at Gowran on latest but more needed in new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Minor step in right direction at Gowran latest, more needed in new headgear

runners.name: Lust
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner but hasn't got his head in front since a listed win in 2022; badly out of sorts last season and can only be watched on seasonal reappearance.
runners.comment: No win since 2022, badly out of sorts last year, can only be watched after break

races.race_name: Racing Again Next Friday Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: In The Minus
runners.spotlight: 7f win at Limerick (good) last June off 5lb lower; decent fifth on seasonal return at Ballinrobe (9.5f, gd-yld); place chance on that run off 2lb lower, but poor over hurdles since.
runners.comment: Pleasing fifth on return at Ballinrobe, place chance on that, poor over hurdles since

runners.name: No Bother Aoife
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in maidens over 7f-1m at Dundalk last winter; kept on well enough over 1m at Gowran (gd-yld) on handicap debut after a break in first-time tongue tie (retained), beaten 6.75l into eighth; this trip should suit; could go well.
runners.comment: Kept on over 1m at Gowran on h'cap debut, this longer trip should suit, could go well

runners.name: Miss Americana
runners.spotlight: Narrowly denied on seasonal return at Gowran (1m, soft) off this mark; below best the last twice at Leopardstown (1m, gd-yld) and Gowran (7f, soft); step up in trip a plus (second over 9.5f at Clonmel last season); rain would boost chance.
runners.comment: Best efforts have come on soft ground at 1m-9.5f, rain would enhance claims

runners.name: Alto Sax
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D claimer in May 2024; sole win since on AW on return from a break in April (1m); creditable efforts returned to turf over 1m at Leopardstown and Gowran the last twice, staying on well; return to 1m2f a big plus; player.
runners.comment: C&D claimer winner, stayed on well over 1m last twice, return to 1m2f a big plus, player

runners.name: La Campanella
runners.spotlight: Has shown glimmers of ability at Dundalk; beaten 3l when seventh over 1m there when last seen in January off 3lb higher; might need this but worth a market check for local yard.
runners.comment: Glimmers of ability at Dundalk, off since January, might need this but check market

runners.name: Captain Con
runners.spotlight: Poor form in maidens at 7f-1m2f on varying ground; beaten 14l on handicap debut at Roscommon (1m4f, yielding); drops in trip and this is easier, but more needed off just 1lb lower.
runners.comment: Beaten 14l on handicap bow, eased 1lb and in grade, drops in trip, others preferred

runners.name: My Kurkum
runners.spotlight: On long losing run; best efforts for this yard have come over shorter than this; poor form so far in 2026; others hold more pressing claims.
runners.comment: On long losing run, best form for this yard over shorter, poor form so far in 2026

runners.name: Copper Craft
runners.spotlight: Very modest form on turf but has been knocking on the door at Dundalk; close third on return from break there in April (10.5f); beaten a neck there over 1m4f latest; just 1lb higher here, but has to prove effectiveness on turf.
runners.comment: Has gone close twice at Dundalk since return from break, yet to show much on the turf

runners.name: Swift Charm
runners.spotlight: Left previous form behind when a 2.75l third of 14 at Ballinrobe on latest (9.5f, gd-yld) at odds of 50-1 four weeks ago; the fourth and sixth from that race have won since; same mark here and shortlisted.
runners.comment: Best effort yet on latest, form has been boosted, same mark here, shortlisted

runners.name: Yquem
runners.spotlight: Promise in a couple of AW runs for John O'Donoghue but poor form in four runs on turf; off since 28l defeat at Newcastle in February; best watched on first run for new yard.
runners.comment: Promise on AW for former yard, turf form poor, best watched on debut for new stable

runners.name: Cleopatra's Needle
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose mare; shaped very nicely on last start in this sphere at Leopardstown in April (1m, gd-yld) when a 0.75l second of 20; sole win on level came at this trip and has won at this venue over hurdles; chance.
runners.comment: Fine second on last Flat start at Leopardstown, has won here over hurdles, chance

runners.name: Deuteronomy
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways at Cork 17 days ago (1m2f, sft-hvy); effective on better ground but raised 7lb and has never won off a mark this high; career best needed to follow up.
runners.comment: Up 7lb for Cork win on latest, has never won off a mark this high, career best needed

runners.name: Maker's Mark
runners.spotlight: 0-5 on turf and yet to show winning potential; beaten cumulative distance of 33l in first two handicaps at Bellewstown (1m, soft) and Dundalk (1m); new trip tried now.
runners.comment: 0-5 on turf, soundly beaten in first two h'caps at 1m, new trip tried now
runners.owner: Hugh J O'Brien & Colm O'Brien & Ms Denise McCourt

runners.name: Albatala
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden; 0-7 on the Flat; off since heavy defeat over hurdles at Kilbeggan in April 2025; not seen on the Flat since July 2024; best watched.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose,0-7 on the level, not seen on Flat since July 2024, best watched

runners.name: Green Icon
runners.spotlight: Yet to hit form since joining this yard; heavy defeat over 6f here on latest; steps up in trip but failed to fire when tried at this distance before.
runners.comment: No impact so far for this yard, well beaten over 6f here latest, steps back up in trip

runners.name: Rushford
runners.spotlight: No win since 2022; showed little on last two starts at Dundalk after return from massive layoff; thrown in on old form, but would prefer further than this and hard to fancy.
runners.comment: On long losing run, poor twice on AW after long layoff, this trip a query, hard to fancy

runners.name: Milverton
runners.spotlight: Placed in a Perth bumper in 2024 but yet to make much of an impact in handicaps on the Flat; 33-1 and beaten 19l when last seen at Dundalk in November; eased 5lb and yard flying, but hard to fancy on form.
runners.comment: Poor Flat form, beaten 19l on AW when last seen in Nov', yard in form but hard to fancy

races.race_name: Track Radio On Digital & DAB Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Platinum Prince
runners.spotlight: Needs a revival but fast ground wasn't ideal on his comeback run and seven career wins include this race 12 months ago off only 3lb lower; rarely looks straightforward but remains capable of winning races.
runners.comment: Seven career wins include this race 12 months ago off only 3lb lower

runners.name: Tilani
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who has only had four runs for Ian Williams and won the last two, 1m handicaps on good ground at Newbury and Yarmouth; had no penalty last time and now 6lb higher, but he's on a roll; has won on soft.
runners.comment: Had no penalty last time and now 6lb higher, but he's on a roll; has won on soft

runners.name: Pay Attention
runners.spotlight: Missed the break at Newbury (1m, good; in this first-time headgear combination) but still got to challenge Tilani and he's better off at the weights with that rival here; has missed three engagements in the last year on account of good to soft and soft ground.
runners.comment: Chance on his Newbury run last time but softening ground might not be favourable

runners.name: Oman
runners.spotlight: Versatile ground-wise and he wasn't far off the principals last time at Lingfield (1m2f); hard to call him well handicapped but entitled to have some say.
runners.comment: Versatile ground-wise and he wasn't far off the principals last time at Lingfield

runners.name: Bizou
runners.spotlight: Improved to win at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) and followed up from the front at Wolverhampton (9.4f), albeit by the skin of her teeth; only 2lb higher so remains on a competitive mark, but slow ground hasn't looked ideal previously.
runners.comment: Improving hat-trick seeker but slow ground hasn't looked ideal previously

runners.name: Somkiyr
runners.spotlight: Won in France at two; modest hurdler for current team and hasn't been finding life all that easier back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Modest hurdler and hasn't been finding life all that easier back on the Flat

runners.name: Perfect Scoundrel
runners.spotlight: Beaten between 6.5l and 11l in his three handicaps (1m2f-1m4f) and has only dropped 5lb; doesn't look a winner waiting to happen.
runners.comment: Beaten between 6.5l and 11l in his three handicaps (1m2f-1m4f) and has only dropped 5lb
runners.owner: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway

runners.name: Look Back Smiling
runners.spotlight: Lost his way last autumn and, after changing hands for 9,000gns, he was out the back on stable debut at Bath (1m, good; fine on soft); potentially well handicapped but has enough to prove.
runners.comment: After changing hands for 9,000gns he was out the back on stable debut at Bath

runners.name: Trooper
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner in Belgium/Germany and could be glad to see the back of hurdles having offered nothing in that scene; current wellbeing is an issue but goes well with ease underfoot and cheekpieces go on; he'd be interesting if market vibes did bode well.
runners.comment: Current wellbeing is an issue but goes well with ease underfoot and cheekpieces go on

races.race_name: Fitzdares Apprentice Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B-D)

runners.name: Matilda Joslyngage
runners.spotlight: Competitive in her last two maidens in Ireland, over 1m on good to yielding and good ground; taken out of two other races due to soft and yielding conditions, which is a bit of a concern; however, her form claims are right up there.
runners.comment: Bit to prove on this slower ground but her form claims are right up there

runners.name: Venetian Gold
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns breeze-up buy by St Mark's Basilica; did a lot wrong on her debut and was more with it when running out a length winner at Kempton last month; has to go well in this company even with her extra weight.
runners.comment: She was more with it when running out a length winner at Kempton last month

runners.name: Poddle Park
runners.spotlight: 33,000gns yearling; seventh foal; half-sister to four winners including Valpolicella (6f; RPR 83), Grand Cru Gaga (6f; 74) and Adelabella (1m; 71); dam unraced half-sister to Listed-placed 6f winner Chocoya; faces no easy starter.
runners.comment: 33,000gns yearling; seventh foal; half-sister to four winners; faces no easy starter

runners.name: Blue Birdie
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in two visits to Kempton for 1m3f novice races.
runners.comment: Tailed off in two visits to Kempton for 1m3f novice races

runners.name: Afraj
runners.spotlight: Penalised for winning a weak 7f maiden at Chelmsford in November but she defied market weakness with a narrow verdict and her inexperience was evident; certainly open to further improvement.
runners.comment: Penalised for winning a weak 7f maiden at Chelmsford but her inexperience was evident

runners.name: Living In Hope
runners.spotlight: Has brought up the rear in both her 1m races on the AW and looks one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Has brought up the rear in both her 1m races on the AW and looks one for handicaps

runners.name: Miss Guinevere
runners.spotlight: Daughter of Camelot who cost 65,000euros as a yearling; made a quiet start when ninth at Kempton (1m; 20-1) and she was weak in the market; the hood fitted there is removed.
runners.comment: Made a quiet start when ninth at Kempton (1m; 20-1) and she was weak in the market

races.race_name: Tommie Jakes Memorial Apprentice Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Insuspense
runners.spotlight: Forced to challenge widest of all last time at Windsor so forget that; she's a consistent maiden but whether she wants slowish ground is questionable (withdrawn the once on account of such conditions).
runners.comment: She's a consistent maiden but whether she wants slowish ground is questionable

runners.name: Rye
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden but hard to knock her consistency and she's handily drawn in ten; however, only once has she encountered ground slower than good and she trailed home last.
runners.comment: Consistent and well drawn but might not be helped by good to soft ground

runners.name: Magna
runners.spotlight: Has been on a roll on the AW and wasn't far away behind Midnight Call in her bid for a four-timer (fine at 5f and 6f); the glaring concern is that she has no worthwhile form on turf, albeit after just four attempts.
runners.comment: Improver on the AW but she has no worthwhile form on turf

runners.name: Moostar
runners.spotlight: Nursery winner over 6f on good to soft; more consistent than progressive in her recent defeats and mark continues to edge upwards; should still run well, provided the return of cheekpieces (replacing visor) doesn't trouble her as they've done nothing in the past.
runners.comment: Has been running well in defeat, though her form in cheekpieces is poor

runners.name: Land Of Magic
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 4-35; best run for a while when a front-running second at Lingfield (7f, good) and dropping back to 6f won't hinder, nor the slower ground; could have done with a higher draw ideally but should run well.
runners.comment: Strike-rate of 4-35; best run for a while when a front-running second at Lingfield

runners.name: Addarella
runners.spotlight: Both wins over this trip but on the AW and for Marco Botti; it was a good first run for this yard back at Wolverhampton but hasn't kicked on at all; ground conditions are another likely negative, together with stall 1.
runners.comment: Good first run for this yard back at Wolverhampton but hasn't kicked on at all

runners.name: Midnight Call
runners.spotlight: Has won two of her last three on the AW, taking overall record on synthetics to 6-14; last time she dictated from the front at Wolverhampton (4lb lower); seemed to handle turf fine in three previous attempts, though not on ground this slow.
runners.comment: Dictated from the front last time to make it 6-14 on the AW; unraced on slow turf

runners.name: Cameley Days
runners.spotlight: Has won three of her last six races and the defeat at Lingfield 17 days ago was no backward step (5f, good); has another career high mark to deal with (raised 3lb) but she's a 6f winner on good to soft and very much considered from stall 8.
runners.comment: In-form filly and latest defeat no backward step; has won over 6f on good to soft

runners.name: Twirler
runners.spotlight: Exposed six-time winner who did well to overcome trouble in running at Windsor one month ago (6f, good to firm; 4lb lower); unraced here but she's an Epsom winner on good to soft.
runners.comment: Did well to overcome trouble in running at Windsor a month ago; won on good to soft

runners.name: Thunderous Love
runners.spotlight: Back on her last winning mark and perhaps stretched by 7f last time when behind Land Of Magic; fine on all surfaces and considered, despite a low draw.
runners.comment: Back on her last winning mark and perhaps stretched by 7f last time; ground fine

races.race_name: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Betting Service Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Twilight Glow
runners.spotlight: His second on good to soft at Newbury last season (6f maiden) remains his best piece of form; gelded prior to this campaign and ran okay on his return (7f handicap) before struggling in a Class 4 over 6f at Ascot; one to consider in these calmer waters.
runners.comment: Best run was on good to soft; this is easier than a Class 4 at Ascot last time

runners.name: Miss Lady Grace
runners.spotlight: Her fifth start when winning a 7f maiden from the front at Wolverhampton in February; out the back in 6f/7f handicaps since then.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner in February but out the back in 6f/7f handicaps since then
runners.owner: R H J Martin And Mrs J Martin

runners.name: Ice Cube
runners.spotlight: Only 5-4 and 4-1 for his two 1m handicaps since being gelded and showed plenty of pace both times before weakening; dropping back in trip should be a big help.
runners.comment: Has shown plenty of pace in 1m handicaps and dropping back to 7f should help

runners.name: Fanjove
runners.spotlight: The winner of two 7f handicaps on the AW and has shown he can threaten off this mark; last run was disappointing but could bounce back, and no doubt a better horse now than when last seen on turf.
runners.comment: The winner of two 7f handicaps on the AW and has shown he can threaten off this mark
runners.owner: P C J Dalby & R D Schuster

runners.name: Chelio
runners.spotlight: Promising runs last season at 7f/1m; well held in two handicaps (7f/6f) this campaign, though was quite short in the betting for both and Windsor last time didn't go to plan; wouldn't rule out.
runners.comment: Short in the betting for both handicaps and Windsor last time didn't go to plan

runners.name: Perfect Location
runners.spotlight: 6f maiden winner on the AW and runner-up off this mark on her handicap debut next time (6f, good to soft); again runner-up at Kempton two runs ago but over 6f and didn't convince when upped to 7f latest.
runners.comment: Can win races off this mark but didn't convince when upped to 7f last time
runners.owner: Mildmay Racing Pl & Only Fillies Rc

runners.name: Ahead Of Fashion
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times, though only four ran when second at Wolverhampton last time (7f) and that form is unconvincing; others preferred.
runners.comment: Only four ran when second at Wolverhampton last time (7f) and that form is unconvincing

runners.name: Feisty Minx
runners.spotlight: Beaten just over a length into fourth of seven on handicap debut at Kempton (7f) and 1m seemed too far back there 16 days ago; not yet exposed, though this is her first time on the grass.
runners.comment: Close up over 7f on handicap debut and 1m seemed too far last time; first run on turf
runners.owner: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway

runners.name: Irish Fusilier
runners.spotlight: Made some progress in each of his runs last season (5f/6f) and was always likely to be seen to better effect once entering handicaps; this step up in trip should be a further plus; the market could be revealing after a lengthy break.
runners.comment: Made some progress in each of his runs last season (5f/6f); handicap debut

runners.name: Asteverdi
runners.spotlight: All three wins for Alice Haynes were on ground slower than good; had lost her way but the signs have been a tad more encouraging for James Owen and she's on a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: Signs have been a tad more encouraging for James Owen and she's on a dangerous mark

runners.name: Cristo
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been at Chelmsford over 1m2f; capable of winning on turf and had Silca Bay behind when going close at Salisbury last time (1m, good; effective on soft); more solid than most in here.
runners.comment: Runner-up on good ground at Salisbury and he's fine on soft; fairly solid claims

runners.name: Canopus
runners.spotlight: A 1m maiden winner for Ed Walker; no worthwhile form over hurdles or on the Flat (two handicaps) for this operation.
runners.comment: No worthwhile form over hurdles or on the Flat (two handicaps) for this operation

runners.name: Rising Force
runners.spotlight: He's 2-5 for this yard, the wins over 1m on good to soft and good to firm; a slow start was no help last time at Thirsk but he might be high in the weights now.
runners.comment: Slow start was no help last time at Thirsk but he might be high in the weights now

runners.name: Port Erin
runners.spotlight: Won three 1m handicaps (AW/good) last year and was fourth in an earlier race on this card 12 months ago when the ground was good to soft; the clear negative is complete no-shows in his last three starts.
runners.comment: Capable of going well but the clear negative is complete no-shows in his last three starts

runners.name: City Of God
runners.spotlight: Left Karl Burke after winning an AW seller in February (1m); nearer last than first in two 1m handicaps for current yard.
runners.comment: Won a seller in February; nearer last than first in two 1m handicaps for current yard

runners.name: Silca Bay
runners.spotlight: All three wins on the AW and ground this slow will be a first for him; however, his usual cheekpieces are back on after keeping on well for fourth at Salisbury (1m, good) and he enters the equation.
runners.comment: Usual cheekpieces are back on after keeping on well for fourth at Salisbury

runners.name: Ararat
runners.spotlight: A five-time winner but over shorter distances and the fact he often front-runs could count against him now trying 1m for the first time; well beaten over 6f on recent stable debut.
runners.comment: A five-time winner but over shorter distances; serious stamina concerns over this far

runners.name: Jakarta
runners.spotlight: Won on good and soft ground as a 2yo; nearly added a Newmarket 1m handicap during a light second season and fast ground (for which she's been withdrawn before) was perhaps not ideal when down the field 13 days ago; better expected.
runners.comment: Dual winner who enjoys cut underfoot; fast ground wasn't for her four days ago

races.race_name: Goodwood Horseracing Club Membership Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Dancing Tiger
runners.spotlight: 1m2f might be his trip but this far is worth exploring and he went close at Lingfield last time (good) despite starting slowly; very at home on soft ground and has won here; respected.
runners.comment: Good run last time after starting slowly; has won here and enjoys soft ground; respected

runners.name: Must Believe
runners.spotlight: Picked up for 25,000gns in February and emerged from midfield to make a successful debut for George Baker over 1m3f on the AW; impressed with his attitude that day as he was under the pump early enough; raised 4lb; 0-4 on turf and unraced on ground slower than good.
runners.comment: Brave winner on AW (1m3f; yard debut); 0-4 on turf and unraced on ground slower than good

runners.name: Adjuvant
runners.spotlight: Ages since he last won but has run well now and again during his losing streak and might be happier getting back on the grass; cheekpieces belatedly tried for the first time.
runners.comment: Ages since he last won but has run well now and again during his losing streak; new aids

runners.name: Salamanca City
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been at 1m2f and, although she has run well at 1m3f, this far might be a stretch; if she did last out then would have a chance in current form on ground she handles; Arron D'Arcy's claim eases her career-high mark.
runners.comment: If she can last out over this far then would have a chance on ground she handles

runners.name: Renesmee
runners.spotlight: Had a lot of racing but she arrives here in a fine run of form and kept on well for second in a Class 4 at Chester four weeks ago (good); effective on good to soft; chance.
runners.comment: In a fine run of form and kept on well for second in a Class 4 at Chester four weeks ago

runners.name: Tiger
runners.spotlight: 1m1f winner in France last March; might be finding his feet for new yard after a never-dangerous sixth (not beaten far) over 1m last time and this could be more his trip.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous sixth (not beaten far) over 1m last time and this could be more his trip

runners.name: Sixpack
runners.spotlight: Stays 1m6f and he battled on best to win over 1m4f at Doncaster last month to earn a 3lb rise; Mason Paetel can't claim this time but this 6yo will probably be bang there on ground he can deal with.
runners.comment: Stays 1m6f and he battled on best to win over 1m4f at Doncaster last month; up 3lb

runners.name: Love Is Golden
runners.spotlight: Quite useful on Flat up to 1m4f for the Johnstons and later won twice over hurdles for Gary Moore; however, it's difficult to know what to expect after such a mammoth spell on the sidelines.
runners.comment: Flat/hurdles winner but difficult to know what to expect after such a mammoth absence

runners.name: Arthur's Realm
runners.spotlight: Quite smart at his best for Ed Dunlop and he goes well on slow ground; however, essentially regressive in 2025 and new trainer has to turn him around.
runners.comment: Essentially regressive in 2025 and new trainer has to turn him around

runners.name: Whats New
runners.spotlight: Mercurial character has placed in several maiden hurdles that were stronger than this; 17.5l fourth at Bellewstown and 21l third at Killarney the most recent of his runs over obstacles; creditable sixth on Flat at Gowran last month; big chance on figures but not the most trustworthy individual.
runners.comment: Mercurial character has placed in several stronger contests than this; big chance

runners.name: Clanokre Lass
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; beaten 20l in what has turned out to be a strong Cork bumper on her debut (2m1f, good); needs to step forward.
runners.comment: Beaten 20l in strong Cork bumper; needs to step forward

runners.name: Open Approach
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros yearling, (25,000euros vendor 3yo); first foal; dam unraced sister to winners Adamantly Chosen (smart 2m-3m2f hurdle/chase) and Uncle Pat (useful 2m6f hurdle/3m7f chase), half-sister to useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Kylecrue; may be up to taking this on debut; check market.
runners.comment: Dam unraced sister to winners Adamantly Chosen and Uncle Pat; check market

runners.name: Earthwatch
runners.spotlight: Flat winner for William Haggas achieved a peak rating of 81 in that sphere; decent effort on second hurdles start at Killarney when fourth, 6.5l behind Whats New; needs to bridge that gap somehow.
runners.comment: Fourth on second hurdles start, 6.5l behind Whats New; needs to bridge that gao somehow

runners.name: Straightenyourhand
runners.spotlight: Moderate effort on Killarney debut but improved at Listowel when a 17.5l sixth of 17 (250-1); continued improvement needed.
runners.comment: Improved over moderate debut when sixth of 17 at Listowel (250-1); more needed

runners.name: The Chancie Grey
runners.spotlight: Capri filly; half-sister to winners Diamantiste (2m6f hurdle) and Mamaker (point); dam 2m6f-3m2f cross-country chase winner, sister to 1m1f-13.5f winner Dolman Gris and 2m1f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Ecolomaniac; others more likely.
runners.comment: Capri filly is a half-sister Diamantiste (2m6f, hurdle winner); others more likely

runners.name: The West A Yeats
runners.spotlight: Yeats filly; dam unraced half-sister to winners Mind The Crack (11.6f/1m4f) and Mackenberg (bumper and useful 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase), out of maiden half-sister to French 2,000 Guineas/French Derby winner Brametot; unlikely to make immediate impact.
runners.comment: Yeats filly; dam unraced half-sister to two winners; unlikely to make immediate impact

runners.name: Dahoma
runners.spotlight: Struggled in maiden hurdles and similar comments apply to his three outings in handicap hurdles; tongue tie goes on after being pulled up at Kilbeggan.
runners.comment: Struggled in maiden hurdles and similar comments apply to three h'caps; PU latest

runners.name: Johnny Rambo
runners.spotlight: Dam showed nothing in point/bumper, sister to useful 2m2f/2m4f hurdle winner Euxton Lane, out of unraced half-sister to 2m hurdle/chase winner Charlton Kings; not so appealing on paper.
runners.comment: Dam showed nothing in point/bumper, sister to hurdle winner Euxton Lane; unappealing

runners.name: Nuestro Caballo
runners.spotlight: Dam no sign of ability, sister to bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Sam's Choice, out of unraced half-sister to winners Lord Scoundrel (Galway Plate), Barnaslow (useful 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Northern Bound (useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase); best watched.
runners.comment: Dam showed no sign of ability but is a sister to winner Sam's Choice; best watched

runners.name: Mambo Du Large
runners.spotlight: Dam unraced close relation to French 2m2f hurdle winner Fadas, half-sister to French winners Pafadas (1m7f-2m2f hurdle), Winklas (2m2f/2m3f hurdle/chase), Prinkle (2m1f hurdle) and Frankle (1m2f-1m4f); pulled up in sole point-to-point; hard to fancy on that basis.
runners.comment: Pulled up in sole point-to-point; hard to fancy on that basis

runners.name: Mirfaq
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in two maidens; pulled up at Downpatrick latest.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential in two maidens; pulled up at Downpatrick latest

runners.name: Magna Gee Gee
runners.spotlight: Flat AW winner in Britain in 2024; middling runs on turf recently; makes his hurdling debut now.
runners.comment: AW winner in 2024; middling turf runs and makes hurdling debut now

runners.name: Laligurash
runners.spotlight: Unseated in both point-to-point starts; 67l behind on hurdling debut at Cork; got closer at Ballinrobe but others preferred.
runners.comment: Unseated in both points; 67l behind on hurdling debut; got closer latest; others preferred

runners.name: Stone Rulya
runners.spotlight: Ordinary Flat form and tailed off at triple-figure odds in three maiden hurdles; perhaps better ground and a tongue tie can help.
runners.comment: Ordinary Flat form and tailed off at triple-figure odds in three m'den hurdles

runners.name: William F Browne
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner for Ger Lyons in 2024; 22-1 and beaten 35l on hurdling bow at Sligo last month (2m2f, good).
runners.comment: Dual Flat winner for Ger Lyons in 2024; beaten 35l on hurdling bow last time

runners.name: Shakita
runners.spotlight: Basement rating on the Flat from six starts; weak maiden hurdles form too; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Basement rating on the Flat from six starts; weak maiden hurdles form too; hard to fancy

runners.name: Tingarran Express
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner, also placed here four times; battled well from the front to land a mares' handicap at Tramore last August (good) off 98; tailed off here on heavy back from a break in February and weak in the market here the last day when never dangerous; she's a player back in the 0-100 category now.
runners.comment: Good course form inc' two wins; she's a player back in the 0-100 category now

runners.name: Benzine
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form over hurdles; returned from a break with an excellent first run over fences at Limerick, finished second despite making a few slight errors; didn't maintain that improvement at Kilbeggan back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Positive first run over fences but continued ordinary form over hurdles last time

runners.name: Ishan
runners.spotlight: Three-time winning hurdler in 2023, showing a liking for a test of stamina in testing conditions; now 5lb below last winning mark but not much encouragement in his stable debut here last month (2m4f, good to yielding).
runners.comment: Three-time winning hurdler in 2023; not much encouragement in stable debut here

runners.name: Hatfield Hammer
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles at Wexford on return from a break in May last year (2m1f, good) and second in similar event after; yielding to soft ground unlikely to his liking when always behind at Thurles when last seen in Nov'; not discounted.
runners.comment: Won back from a break this time last year; not discounted

runners.name: Gino Bella
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner last November and was in fairly good form following that until weakening quickly and being pulled up at Kilbeggan last time (2m3f, good to yielding); took a 3.25l fourth over 2m here (soft) on penultimate; in the mix if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Point winner was in fairly good form following until PU latest; chance if bouncing back

runners.name: Mon Sheriffe
runners.spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles; ran well on testing ground at Navan in December but has failed to complete two of last three starts; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles and failed to complete two of last three starts; others preferred
runners.owner: T Leopold, P Smith, D P Connolly & C Connolly

runners.name: Marlpark
runners.spotlight: Has shown his best form here; won a C&D maiden hurdle (good to yielding) in 2024; ran respectably here (2m3f, soft) in March when an 8l second of 15; tailed off in two subsequent runs; fourth of nine at Limerick was a bit more encouraging when 2m4f was too far.
runners.comment: Shown best form here inc' C&D maiden win; some encouragement in fourth at Limerick latest

runners.name: Small Bucks
runners.spotlight: A 33-1 winner on good ground at Cork last summer; shaped nicely at Down Royal last month when staying on late (2m2f, good) and built on that when fourth of 13 at Downpatrick over the same trip four days later; this ground suits her and she's getting her act together.
runners.comment: 33-1 winner at Cork last summer; this ground suits her and she's getting her act together

runners.name: Dontdooddson
runners.spotlight: Veteran added a fourth win at Wexford in July (2m1f, good) off this mark; creditable efforts last twice but more needed to win.
runners.comment: Veteran added a fourth win in July; creditable efforts last twice but more needed to win
runners.owner: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club

runners.name: Urban War
runners.spotlight: Won twice on the Flat early in his career, modest Flat/hurdles form for this stable; fair run at a big price on the Flat at Leopardstown in April.
runners.comment: Modest Flat/hurdles form for this stable; fair run at big price on Flat in April

runners.name: Gentle Annie
runners.spotlight: Good effort in third maiden at Tramore when finishing within 2.75l of subsequent Listed hurdle winner Cousin Kate; ran disappointingly on handicap debut at Fairyhouse (2m5f, soft to heavy); maybe better ground can bring out her best.
runners.comment: Got to within 2.75l of progressive sort in final m'den; tailed off on h'cap bow

runners.name: Theanniversary Man
runners.spotlight: First reserve; back-to-back winner in November/December at Thurles (2m, good to yielding) and Cork (2m4f, soft to heavy); made a disappointing return at Tramore and perhaps best watched after that.
runners.comment: Reserve; back-to-back winner in Nov'/Dec' but made a disappointing return last time

runners.name: Keep It Together
runners.spotlight: Yet to trouble the judge in ten career starts; 100-1 when pulled up on handicap bow at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Yet to trouble the judge in ten starts; PU on h'cap bow at Punchestown

runners.name: Boogie Inthejungle
runners.spotlight: Regressive Flat maiden didn't show much in her opening hurdle runs but has started to show small signs of life recently; 12l sixth in a Limerick mares' maiden hurdle (2m, soft) before being beaten 10.5l at Wexford on handicap debut; more needed in first-time tongue tie.
runners.comment: Small signs of life recently but more needed in first-time tongue tie

runners.name: Max Meridius
runners.spotlight: Beaten 51l over C&D on heavy and 33l at Punchestown (2m, good to yielding) in last two handicap outings; others preferred.
runners.comment: Beaten 51l and 33l in last two h'cap outings; others preferred

runners.name: Frontier Rose
runners.spotlight: Modest form in bumpers and maiden hurdles for her previous trainer; not much better for new yard, tailed off on handicap debut at Downpatrick.
runners.comment: Modest form; tailed off on h'cap bow last time

runners.name: Timurshah
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 66-1 on final start for Philip Byrne for his handicap debut at Thurles (2m, good to yielding) in November and ran as his price suggested; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Ran as 66-1 price suggested on h'cap bow in Nov'; makes stable debut

runners.name: Liss Eile
runners.spotlight: Winless from 16 career starts, six over hurdles; bounced back after being pulled up at Tramore to take a close fourth back there next time (2m, good); needs to build on that but hard to trust on overall form.
runners.comment: 0-16, six over hurdles; decent fourth last time but hard to trust on overall form

runners.name: Getaway Henry
runners.spotlight: Shock winner at Wexford in 2024; best effort last season when runner-up at Thurles (2m, good to yielding) in October; pulled up over 2m4f at Limerick (soft) back from a break; others preferred.
runners.comment: Best effort last season when runner-up at Thurles; PU back from a break last time

runners.name: Sidiriya
runners.spotlight: Took third at Naas in March (2m, soft to heavy) before lesser efforts at Cork and Kilbeggan; favourite at Sligo (2m2f, good) last time and ran better than her distant third suggests after she missed the start; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Third last time can be rated higher after she missed the start; not dismissed

runners.name: Falcon Park
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner with his latest win being a C&D victory in January last year off 87 (soft) for Paul Hennessy; best effort for current yard was when runner-up behind a well handicapped rival here on heavy in February; hasn't matched that effort since.
runners.comment: C&D victor last year off 87; best effort for this yard when runner-up here in Feb'

runners.name: Must Meet Cecil
runners.spotlight: Five-time winning hurdler hasn't been at his best for some time; 17l behind in seventh at Kilbeggan last time; drop into 0-100 company for the first time may spark a revival.
runners.comment: Five-time winning hurdler but no sign of best for some time; drops into 0-100 company now

runners.name: Go To Town
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at Punchestown over 3m on handicap/stable debut; this trip may suit better but others preferred.
runners.comment: Tailed off at Punchestown over 3m on h'cap/stable debut; others preferred

runners.name: You Done Well
runners.spotlight: Is 1-13 over hurdles with the win coming at Limerick last year off 6lb higher (2m3f, good); some decent runs in defeat in claiming hurdles here and at Tramore, when a 12l runner-up (2m1f, good to yielding); makes stable debut.
runners.comment: Won off 6lb higher last year; some decent runs in claiming hurdles recently

runners.name: Kp's Ladyofknock
runners.spotlight: Is 1-12 over hurdles with the victory coming over C&D in December 2024 (yielding); returned from over a year off at Down Royal (2m4f, good) in April when probably beaten in second when falling at the final flight; attracted support in the market that day and it was a creditable run; interesting dropped back to winning trip.
