runners.comment: C&D winner; creditable run back from over a year off latest when fell at last; interesting

runners.name: Fedneys Park
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner for Mick Goff; ran her best race under rules when fourth of ten at Bellewstown (2m1f, yielding) on penultimate; didn't build on that when tailed off at Wexford dropped to this grade last time; needs to bring her best.
runners.comment: Point winner ran best race when fourth on penultimate; tailed off last time

runners.name: Magnificent Era
runners.spotlight: Best of three maiden runs came at Punchestown (2m1f, good to yielding) when 29l behind in a decent quality contest; decent opening mark if taking the 91 RPR he ran to that day literally; absence to overcome.
runners.comment: Best m'den run was when 29l behind in quality contest; absence to overcome

runners.name: Western Opera
runners.spotlight: Standout run in handicaps came when second in a 2m6f Ballinrobe contest last summer (good); pulled up at Wexford on return from an absence; this trip looks inadequate.
runners.comment: Standout run came over 2m6f at Ballinrobe last summer when runner-up; PU last time

runners.name: Crypto Crash
runners.spotlight: Second over 1m4f on fast ground for David Marnane; improved effort over hurdles upon encountering good ground at Down Royal (2m2f) last month when a 12l sixth; more needed in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Improved effort over hurdles upon encountering better ground latest; more needed

runners.name: I'm Flattered
runners.spotlight: Won off 1lb higher at Kilbeggan in August (2m, good to yielding); no major impact in five starts since; eighth of 18 at Leopardstown (2m3f) in March on soft to heavy ground; may improve for better ground here.
runners.comment: Won off 1lb higher last summer; eighth of 18 last time; may improve for better ground

runners.name: The Cheeky Pup
runners.spotlight: Didn't improve for switch to handicaps last time when tailed off at Punchestown over 3m (good); hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Didn't improve for switch to h'caps last time when tailed off; hard to fancy

runners.name: Rogue Sea
runners.spotlight: Flat winner; no sign of winning potential in four maiden hurdles at 2m-2m1f but may improve for switch to a handicap on stable bow; check market.
runners.comment: Flat winner; no sign of winning potential in four m'den hurdles; check market on h'cap bow

runners.name: Boltlander
runners.spotlight: Moderate maiden form for Gordon Elliott and tailed off on handicap/stable debut at Wexford; unlikely.
runners.comment: Moderate m'den form and tailed off on h'cap/stable debut; unlikely

runners.name: He Who Dares
runners.spotlight: First reserve; ordinary form so far in three runs on the Flat and five over hurdles; beaten 21l on seasonal return in a first-time hood six days ago.
runners.comment: Reserve; ordinary Flat/hurdles form so far; beaten 21l at Tramore on seasonal return

races.race_name: Talbot Hotel Clonmel (C & G) Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: In The Trenches
runners.spotlight: Maiden under all codes in 33 races including 14 over hurdles but placed lots of times; some good chase runs lately (not last time) and should go well if back to best hurdles form.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden but should go well if back to best hurdles form

runners.name: Market Ready
runners.spotlight: The best of five hurdle runs came when a 1l runner-up in a C&D handicap in April; not as good at Limerick since in more testing ground; can run well but has a bit to find with the best of these.
runners.comment: Best run came in a C&D handicap; run well but has a bit to find with the best of these

runners.name: Holy Joe
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten at Perth on debut; was making some headway from the rear when unseating at the second-last at Downpatrick two weks ago; mightn't be far away if none the worse for that mishap.
runners.comment: Unseated at Downpatrick two weks ago; mightn't be far away if none the worse for that

runners.name: Welcome Back
runners.spotlight: Third on debut in a Navan maiden hurdle in 2024 and won a point the following year; has had an interrupted career since and was well beaten in a 2m3f maiden hurdle here on return; has to return to his best; hood added.
runners.comment: Promising sort earlier in career; has to return to his best; hood added

runners.name: This Songisforyou
runners.spotlight: Useful bumper and Flat winner, his last win coming in a 1m2f Curragh premier handicap in 2024; some fine efforts in defeat in strong company last year but disappointing on final start; made mistakes when disappointing on second hurdle start in 2023; the one to beat if his jumping holds up.
runners.comment: Smart Flat sort; made mistakes last hurdles run in 2023; the one to beat if jumping well

runners.name: It's All Clear
runners.spotlight: Improved form in bumpers at Kilbeggan and here (2m) the last twice; tongue-tie goes on for hurdles debut and can be involved if his jumping holds up.
runners.comment: Decent bumper runs lately incl' here; can be involved if his jumping holds up
runners.owner: Paul M O'Loughlin & W D Gannon

runners.name: Care To Differ
runners.spotlight: Has become disappointing in points after finishing third on debut; cheekpieces on but best watched.
runners.comment: Became disappointing in points after finishing 3rd on debut; cheekpieces on; best watched

runners.name: Quest With Copper
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam placed in 2m/2m2f hurdles, out of unraced half-sister to winners Mephisto (smart 1m4f-1m6f Flat and 2m2f-2m7f hurdle) and Almaydan (1m6f/1m7f Flat and useful 2m-2m1f hurdle/chase); homebred is from the family of some decent sorts and could run well on debut.
runners.comment: Homebred is from the family of some decent sorts and could run well on debut

runners.name: Riverstown Boy
runners.spotlight: Has become disappointing in points after finishing runner-up on debut last year; can only be watched.
runners.comment: Became disappointing in points after finishing 2nd on debut last year; can only be watched

runners.name: Whoyougonnacall
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off in a bumper on debut last September and can only be watched.
runners.comment: Tailed-off in a bumper on debut last September and can only be watched

runners.name: China Grill
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two maiden hurdles including here last time and needs to find loads of improvement.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two maiden hurdles and needs to find loads of improvement

runners.name: It's Time To Leave
runners.spotlight: Jumping lacked fluency when well beaten in a 2m3f maiden hurdle here on recent debut; one to watch for now.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a 2m3f maiden hurdle here on recent debut; one to watch for now

runners.name: Secret Order
runners.spotlight: Can't be fancied on the basis of his two completed hurdle starts so best watched.
runners.comment: Can't be fancied on the basis of his two completed hurdle starts so best watched

runners.name: Thetwocharlies
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way on debut in a heavy-ground Thurles maiden hurdle; likely best watched today.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on debut in a heavy-ground Thurles maiden hurdle; likely best watched

runners.name: Slowdownbarney
runners.spotlight: AW Flat winner in UK has been well beaten in two hurdle races, the latest at Tramore last week; best wait for handicaps.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two hurdle races, the latest at Tramore last week; best wait for handicaps

runners.name: Farren Glory
runners.spotlight: Bumper/chase winner is a dual winner over hurdles, which were a C&D maiden and the Grade 1 Royal Bond at Fairyhouse in 2023; that was his last win in that sphere but some creditable runs in defeat in Graded contests last season; neck behind Thecompanysergeant at Ballinrobe last time (2m3f, good) but was in receipt of 5lb that day; likely suited by softer.
runners.comment: A neck behind Thecompanysergeant last time in receipt of weight; suited by softer

runners.name: King Of Kingsfield
runners.spotlight: Reverts to hurdles after a decent chasing campaign last season that returned three wins from seven starts; not at his best in a Newbury Grade 2 nor an Aintree Grade 1 in April; 142 hurdles rating is the best in this company and won't be troubled by the ground; in the mix.
runners.comment: Reverts to hurdles after a decent chasing campaign last season; ground okay; in the mix

runners.name: Mighty Tom
runners.spotlight: Three-time winning hurdler has clearly had his issues; unseen since tailed off in a Tipperary Grade 3 in October 2024; has the rating to be a big danger but questionable whether he can run to it after such a long time away.
runners.comment: Has the rating to be a big danger but questionable he can run to it after such an absence

runners.name: Crohan Lady
runners.spotlight: Point/maiden hurdle winner has it all to do on these terms.
runners.comment: Point/maiden hurdle winner has it all to do on these terms

runners.name: Thecompanysergeant
runners.spotlight: Dual bumper winner and winning chaser is 2-12 over hurdles and rated 135; bounced back to form when runner-up in a similar race at Ballinrobe last time (2m3f, good), with Farren Glory a neck behind in third; gave Farren Glory 5lb that day and they now race off levels, while claimer also takes off 7lb; big contender.
runners.comment: Bounced back to form when second last time, Farren Glory a neck behind; better off now

runners.name: Encanto Bruno
runners.spotlight: Career peak came in 2023 when winning a novice hurdle at Cheltenham October meeting on first run for Gavin Cromwell; well beaten when runner-up over fences in April and June last year; returned to John McConnell, with whom he started his career, last time at Musselburgh back over hurdles but was disappointing; hard to fancy on current form.
runners.comment: Career peak came in 2023 but hard to fancy on current form; 26l behind last time

runners.name: Le Diablo
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m1f handicap here in April coming back from a 194-day break; not as good twice since and was beaten 23l at Kilbeggan last time so questions now.
runners.comment: Won over 2m1f here on return in April; beaten 23l at Kilbeggan last time so questions now

runners.name: Stonecarthy
runners.spotlight: Reserve; maiden was beaten a long way on return/stable debut last time and cheekpieces need to work; 4lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Maiden was beaten a long way on return/stable debut last time and cheekpieces on; reserve
runners.owner: Dean Brennan & Nigel Brennan & Ms Alana McGuinness

runners.name: Churchwarden
runners.spotlight: Flat maiden won a Kilbeggan handicap hurdle in 2024 when with John McConnell but pulled-up next two hurdle runs; never involved on debut for this yard back on the Flat last time but can do better.
runners.comment: Never involved on debut for this yard back on the Flat last time but can do better

runners.name: Hidalgo Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Yet to win over hurdles though placed lots of times; best on a sound surface so won't want too much rain; could run well.
runners.comment: Maiden is best on a sound surface so won't want too much rain; could run well

runners.name: Coded Welder
runners.spotlight: Has run well in defeat in maiden hurdles; put two lesser handicap runs on heavy ground behind him when second at Cork on return/stable debut; up 3lb for that but still looks a big player.
runners.comment: Second at Cork on return/stable debut; up 3lb for that but still looks a big player

runners.name: The Monkey Pole
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in maiden hurdles at Wexford and Tramore last summer but hasn't been near that level in a bumper and two hurdle runs since; questions to answer.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two maiden hurdles last sunner but hasn't been near that level since

runners.name: Inthenickoftime
runners.spotlight: Was mixing hurdles and fences last year, winning a Galway chase in October before a hurdles second at Tramore; up 3lb for that run so will need to be ready on return to defy that.
runners.comment: Second at Tramore when last seen over hurdles; up 3lb for that so will need to be ready

runners.name: Ned In The Park
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in a C&D handicap in April and ran a solid race in defeat back here next time; below form over 2m at Ballinrobe since but can do better back here over this trip.
runners.comment: C&D winner; below form over 2m at Ballinrobe since but can do better back over this trip

runners.name: Mister Vic
runners.spotlight: Won a Ballinrobe handicap by 8l last July and has been well beaten off much higher marks twice since so looks best watched for now.
runners.comment: Won a Ballinrobe handicap by 8l last July; well beaten off much higher marks twice since

runners.name: Speakasyoufind
runners.spotlight: Yet to show winning potential in maiden and handicap hurdles and was beaten a long way over C&D last time in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Was beaten a long way over C&D last time in first-time cheekpieces

runners.name: Stumblin In
runners.spotlight: Won a Tramore handicap over 2m5f in 2025 but was beaten 50l at the same track over 2m on return; has to leave that run well behind.
runners.comment: Beaten 50l at Tramore over 2m on return; has to leave that run well behind

runners.name: Takarengo
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner is just 1-32 over hurdles; fourth at Wexford in April but has been below that twice since and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Fourth at Wexford in April but has been below that twice since and others make more appeal

runners.name: Global Skies
runners.spotlight: Flat winner ran his best race over hurdles when just denied over C&D last time, seemingly appreciating the livelier ground; is up 4lb but doesn't have to find much more to go one better.
runners.comment: Just denied over C&D last time; up 4lb but doesn't have to find much more to go one better

runners.name: Portarlington
runners.spotlight: Has won thrice over hurdles and once over fences; however, has been below form since his latest score at Downpatrick last year so has to get back to best.
runners.comment: Below form since his latest score at Downpatrick last year so has to get back to best

runners.name: Penny Jar
runners.spotlight: The last of his four hurdle wins came at Gowran in October 2024 off 108; hasn't repeated that form since and was pulled-up last time.
runners.comment: Last win came at Gowran in October 2024; out of form since and was pulled-up last time

runners.name: Boujee
runners.spotlight: Won a Down Royal bumper on debut in 2022 before going missing for nearly three years; best effort in four maiden hurdles when third at Ballinrobe last August; has to be ready on return.
runners.comment: Best effort in four maiden hurdles when third last August; has to be ready on return

runners.name: In For The Night
runners.spotlight: Won handicaps at Fairyhouse and Naas for Des McDonogh over a similar trip to this; lost his form after that latest score and has to return to best on debut for this yard.
runners.comment: Lost his form after that last win and has to return to best on debut for this yard

runners.name: Zolpharine
runners.spotlight: Was a comfortable winner over fences at Limerick and hurdles at Tramore on last two starts; is now 10lb higher in this sphere but is in such good form that he has to be feared; 7lb claims helps too.
runners.comment: Won over fences at Limerick and hurdles at Tramore on last two starts; up 10lb but feared

runners.name: Love Like This
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; second runner for yard; a maiden after 43 races in all codes; just denied at Sligo last month but below that level twice since.
runners.comment: Maiden after 43 races; just denied at Sligo but below that level twice since; reserve

runners.name: Three County Star
runners.spotlight: Not a bad run in a Cork maiden in April but well beaten in a handicap there since so others preferred.
runners.comment: Not a bad run in a Cork maiden in April but well beaten in a handicap there since
runners.owner: John P Coleman & M J Caffrey

runners.name: Sallap
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; sole win came over slightly further at this track last October; was in the lead when falling at Thurles next time off 1lb lower; however, was beaten a long way on return at Cork and has to do much better.
runners.comment: In fine form last autumn and unlucky not to have won twice; beaten a long way on return

runners.name: Last Note
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a bumper and maiden hurdle in UK; beaten a long way at Wexford on stable debut/return and has to improve hugely on that.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way at Wexford on stable debut/return and has to improve hugely on that

runners.name: Spellacy's Cross
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; dual chase winner is 0-17 over hurdles but posted some decent efforts lately and should go well with a repeat.
runners.comment: Is 0-17 over hurdles but some decent efforts lately; should go well with a repeat; reserve
runners.owner: James M Barcoe & J F O'Neill

runners.name: Cullig
runners.spotlight: Won a Cork handicap over 3m by 20l last year and initially ran well off revised marks; however, has been pulled-up in four of her last five starts so questions to answer.
runners.comment: Has been pulled-up in four of her last five starts so questions to answer
runners.owner: Miss Patricia Coghlan & Diana Edwina Oakes & Guy Culliford

runners.name: It's Time Again
runners.spotlight: All three hurdle wins have come at Ballinrobe, the last one in 2025; however, was pulled-up there 10 days ago and hard to fancy on that run.
runners.comment: Was pulled-up at Ballinrobe 10 days ago and hard to fancy on that run

runners.name: Queen Kulina
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 11 hurdle starts but some decent runs in defeat; fell at the 10th on return at Punchestown last time; a squeak if at her best.
runners.comment: Fell at the 10th on return at Punchestown last time; a squeak if at her best

runners.name: Miss Donna
runners.spotlight: Not bad runs over 2m the last twice but will have to find major improvement for step up in trip.
runners.comment: Not bad runs over 2m the last twice but has to find major improvement for step up in trip

runners.name: Fire Coral
runners.spotlight: Won handicaps at Downpatrick (2m6f) and Down Royal (3m) last August; not as good off this mark at Listowel when last seen and will have to be at her best after an absence.
runners.comment: Below form off this mark at Listowel when last seen and will have to be at her best

runners.name: Lily Milan
runners.spotlight: First reserve; point winner has run well in defeat in bumpers but hurdles form has been mostly below that and chedekpieces need to work.
runners.comment: Hurdles form has been disappointing and cheekpieces need to work; reserve

runners.name: Oogum Boogum
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark when winning a Punchestown handicap but didn't jump as well when third at Wexford since off this mark; both those were over 3m but this trip should be fine too; a player if holding his form.
runners.comment: Won at Punchestown and third at Wexford over 3m; a player if holding his form

runners.name: Where Are You Now
runners.spotlight: Went closest to scoring when just denied in a Punchestown handicap over 2m in January (well-backed); raced keenly over 3m on return at the same track last month; can do better down slightly in trip.
runners.comment: Just denied over 2m but raced keenly over 3m last time; can be a player today

runners.name: Rizedda
runners.spotlight: Best maiden run was when fourth at Tramore last August but was well beaten in a Listowel handicap next time; needs her best on return but yard is in form.
runners.comment: Well beaten in a Listowel handicap last seen; needs her best on return but yard is in form

runners.name: Nashville Notions
runners.spotlight: Won a Sligo maiden hurdle last summer but hasn't been near that level in handicaps since, though there was a bit more spark at Wexford last time.
runners.comment: Disappointing in handicaps though there was a bit more spark at Wexford last time

runners.name: Thewaybackhome
runners.spotlight: Point runner-up was well beaten in three maidens but a better effort over 2m3f here on handicap debut last time; there should be more to come and can be involved if staying this trip.
runners.comment: Third over 2m3f here on handicap debut last time; there should be more to come

runners.name: Whoswhointhezoo
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three maidens and on handicap debut last time; blinkers need to have a transformative effect.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three maidens and on handicap debut last time; blinkers need to work

runners.name: Appian Way
runners.spotlight: One of his two hurdle wins came at this track; has been soundly beaten on recent starts so others preferred.
runners.comment: One of his two hurdle wins came at this track; has been soundly beaten on recent starts

races.race_name: Larkhall Construction Handicap (Bath Summer Stayers' Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Lusaka
runners.spotlight: His six wins include two over C&D; no match for No More Bolero when second here (2m1f, good to firm) in April, although he is 5lb better off with that rival; fourth of 12 at Ascot (2m, good) four weeks ago and holds place claims.
runners.comment: His six wins include two over C&D; in reasonable form at present and holds place claims

runners.name: Curran
runners.spotlight: All four wins when trained by Charlie Johnston; sold to join current yard for 6,000gns last October; had wind surgery prior to his stable debut when he was tailed off at Newbury (1m4f, good) three weeks ago; stepping back up in trip will be more suitable but difficult to fancy at present.
runners.comment: Had wind op prior to his stable debut when he was tailed off at Newbury three weeks ago

runners.name: Kotari
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since recording a three-timer in 2024; last of six on return to action at Salisbury (1m4f, good) last month when favourite; has dropped to a career-low mark and first-time visor goes on; may be worth another chance.
runners.comment: Last of six on return to action at Salisbury last month when favourite

runners.name: Taritino
runners.spotlight: Has won twice over hurdles since his last run on the Flat last October; won this equivalent race two years ago when making all; must come into consideration.
runners.comment: Won this equivalent race two years ago when making all; must come into consideration

runners.name: No More Bolero
runners.spotlight: Scored over hurdles last November and recorded his first win on the Flat in Britain, at this track (2m1f, good to firm) in April off 4lb lower; good second at Ripon subsequently but finished last of six back there (2m, good) after slipping on the bend on the first circuit last month; swaps a visor for blinkers and may be worth forgiving latest run.
runners.comment: Won here in April off 4lb lower; second at Ripon subsequently but finished last since

runners.name: Golden Garden
runners.spotlight: Sole success came on AW at Wolverhampton last September when trained by Jamie Osborne; flattered by his finishing position when fourth of ten at Goodwood (1m6f, good) last month and finished last of six subsequently at Yarmouth (1m6f, good); cheekpieces are introduced for the first time and needs more.
runners.comment: Fourth at Goodwood last month and finished last of six subsequently at Yarmouth

runners.name: Percy Jones
runners.spotlight: All four wins on AW at Wolverhampton; 0-22 on turf and beaten 24l on reappearance at Newbury (1m4f, good) three weeks ago; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-22 on turf and beaten 24l on reappearance at Newbury three weeks ago

runners.name: Abando
runners.spotlight: Ex-German, beaten less than 10l in the German Derby last year; nowhere near that form in two starts for Marco Botti and similar story on his three outings for this yard; gelded before his front-running fourth of ten at Nottingham (1m2f) on Wednesday on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Ex-German; five starts in Britain; gelded before fourth on Wednesday on handicap debut

races.race_name: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (For Horses In Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Bill The Bull
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty of speed when fourth in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, good) in March, and filled the same position in a Listed race at Sandown (5f, good) eight days ago; sets the standard on that form and has leading claims.
runners.comment: In the frame on all three starts and is the form horse in the race.

runners.name: Graceful George
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability in both starts to date; drops back in trip with cheekpieces added and holds place claims.
runners.comment: Has shown ability in both starts to date; drops back in trip with cheekpieces added.

runners.name: Invincible Isaac
runners.spotlight: Best run when fourth of five on second start at Nottingham (5f, good) in April; has been gelded since his last run and cheekpieces are now added; may do better when switched to nurseries.
runners.comment: Best run when fourth on second start at Nottingham in April.

runners.name: Katie's Cavalier
runners.spotlight: Fifth of seven on debut at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) last month; makes turf debut and may do better over longer trips.
runners.comment: Fifth on debut at Wolverhampton last month; makes turf debut.

runners.name: Golden Oasis
runners.spotlight: 4,500gns yearling; second foal; by Oasis Dream; dam unraced half-sister to winners Toro Dorado (1m-1m2f) and Uncharted (1m 2yo), out of 11.4f winning half-sister to UAE 1m2f Group 1 winner Matterhorn; could be a longer-term prospect and he's probably best watched on debut.
runners.comment: 4,500gns yearling; could be a longer-term prospect and he's probably best watched on debut

runners.name: Texas Crown
runners.spotlight: 3,000gns yearling; first foal; by Mayson; dam unraced half-sister to useful 6f winner Aplomb, out of 5f winning half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Alhebayeb; makes debut but yard not renowned for 2yo winners.
runners.comment: Makes debut but yard not renowned for 2yo winners.

runners.name: Lucky Larry
runners.spotlight: First foal; by Land Force; dam placed 7f/1m AW (RPR 71), half-sister to useful 5f/6f 2yo winner Serried Ranks, out of Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner; yard still searching for their first 2yo winner this year and may need more time.
runners.comment: Yard still searching for their first 2yo winner this year and may need more time.

runners.name: South West
runners.spotlight: 12,000gns yearling; fourth foal; by Supremacy; half-brother to US winners Straight And Level (6.5f turf), Finery (1m/8.5f turf) and Brother Rice (multiple 6f dirt); dam US 5f turf winner, half-sister to Breeders Cup Dirt Mile winner Furthest Land; yard is firing in the winners at present and worth a check in the market on this debut.
runners.comment: Yard is firing in the winners at present and worth a check in the market on this debut.

runners.name: Holi Scarlett
runners.spotlight: Has shown just modest form in a couple of Newmarket novices last month; drops back in trip and may do better in time.
runners.comment: Has shown just modest form in a couple of Newmarket novices last month.

runners.name: Madentine
runners.spotlight: Always behind after being slowly away when ninth of ten at Kempton (6f, AW) on her debut 16 days ago; drops back in trip and should know more on this turf debut; not out of this.
runners.comment: Always behind after being slowly away when ninth at Kempton on her debut 16 days ago.

runners.name: Temple Court
runners.spotlight: Always behind after being slowly away when beating only one home on his debut at Newmarket (6f, good) three weeks ago; has been gelded since and drops back in trip; may have more to offer.
runners.comment: Beat only one home on his debut at Newmarket three weeks ago.

runners.name: Taseem
runners.spotlight: 80-1 outsider of six, ran as the market suggested when finishing a remote last at Yarmouth three weeks ago; gelded since; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Ran as the market suggested he would when a remote last at Yarmouth three weeks ago.

races.race_name: Hampton By Hilton Bath City Handicap (Bath Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Rogue Rebellion
runners.spotlight: Won on Polytrack (5f) for James Owen in January and was close second to Miss Magic Dragon over C&D (good) last month, on second start for Ronald Harris; needs to bounce back from a duff effort three weeks ago but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: Well beaten on latest outing but was close second over C&D two starts ago; a possible

runners.name: Apex Star
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; placed off 2lb higher at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last month but weakened tamely when dropped to 6f for turf debut at Salisbury (good) nine days later and still needs to prove himself on grass.
runners.comment: Placed over 7f on AW last month but weakened tamely when dropped to 6f for turf debut

runners.name: Tickettothestars
runners.spotlight: Opened account in big field at Nottingham (6f, good) in April and posted another good effort when second of 13 at Lingfield (6f, good) ten days ago; back in action before 1lb rise takes effect; likely contender.
runners.comment: Big-field winner in April and 1lb ahead of the handicapper after recent Lingfield second

runners.name: Forever Glamorous
runners.spotlight: Lost out only by a neck when second on seasonal/stable debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month and was also involved in a close finish when third of five at Chepstow (5f, good to soft) a fortnight ago; ought to be in the shake-up again.
runners.comment: Involved in close finishes on both starts for new stable this season (6f/5f)

runners.name: Luna Beaux
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f AW winner this year; kept on for very respectable fourth in 0-70 contest over C&D (good to firm) a fortnight ago and is 1lb lower today; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner this year; made the frame in higher-grade C&D race two weeks ago; a player

runners.name: Gal
runners.spotlight: Well down the field in three Irish maidens last year (6f-7.5f); could be considered only if lively in the betting before this stable/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well down the field in three Irish maidens last year; makes stable/handicap debut

runners.name: Miss Magic Dragon
runners.spotlight: Built upon encouraging seasonal/handicap debut when beating Rogue Rebellion by a neck over C&D (good) last month; both subsequent 6f runs were perfectly respectable and the return to this track will suit; respected.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D last month and both subsequent 6f runs were perfectly respectable

runners.name: Stolen
runners.spotlight: Raced too freely and looked an awkward ride before dropping out tamely on recent handicap debut at Windsor (6f, good); cheekpieces removed after one use; others look much less risky.
runners.comment: Raced too freely and looked awkward ride before dropping out tamely on handicap debut

runners.name: Snow Day
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in last place when a big-priced outsider for first three races, all over 7f on AW; miraculous improvement required on this handicap/turf debut.
runners.comment: Well beaten in last place when big-priced outsider for first three runs, all over 7f on AW

runners.name: Mortubo
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed after four starts but kept on well from the rear to take fourth on seasonal/handicap debut at Lingfield (6f, AW) in April and has plenty of scope to build on that performance; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Kept on for fourth on seasonal/handicap debut (6f, AW) and probably still has potential

runners.name: Geemann
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only four starts but absent since finishing well down the field over C&D (good to firm) on nursery debut in September; gelded in May.
runners.comment: Absent since well down the field over C&D on nursery debut in September

runners.name: The Flying Seagull
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in a Chester maiden in June 2024; often attracts support but flatters to deceive on many occasions; visor returns after being left off last time, and he's down to a career-low mark, but 1-16 win record is off-putting.
runners.comment: Sole success came in a Chester maiden in June 2024; 1-16 win record is off-putting
runners.owner: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four

runners.name: Glamour Show
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in an AW novice at Kempton in March last year when trained by Eve Johnson Houghton; has made the frame on all three AW starts for current trainer this year and won't be far away if she can replicate that form back on turf.
runners.comment: Has made the frame on all three AW starts for current trainer this year

runners.name: Emporess
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D on heavy in October 2024 and recorded her second win, at Windsor (6f, soft), last September; below that form on her two subsequent starts last year and watching brief advised on this reappearance unless the ground turns in her favour.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D in October 2024 and recorded her second win, at Windsor last September

runners.name: Lequinto
runners.spotlight: Returned to winning ways on the switch to turf, recording his seventh course win at Windsor (5f, good) last month; 3lb higher but still enters calculations away from his favourite track.
runners.comment: Recorded his seventh course win at Windsor last month but still enters calculations

runners.name: Lazzar
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been on AW over 6f; below form back on turf on his last two starts and blinkers worn for the first time, at Chepstow 15 days ago, are persevered with; best to wait until he returns to AW.
runners.comment: All four wins have been on AW over 6f; below form back on turf on his last two starts

runners.name: Strike
runners.spotlight: Last of four wins came in July 2024, but has been runner-up twice over C&D this year, latest on firm ten days ago; likes to hear his feet rattle and if the ground is in his favour then he would come into this.
runners.comment: Last of four wins came in July 2024, but has been runner-up twice over C&D this year
runners.owner: S Emmet And Miss R Emmet

runners.name: Tiger Tulip
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been over 6f; hasn't fired on either of her starts this year but well handicapped if she can return to form.
runners.comment: Hasn't fired in her two starts this year but well handicapped if she can return to form

runners.name: Savannah Smiles
runners.spotlight: Has seven wins but has lost her form since winning here (5f, heavy) last September; has has wind surgery since her last race but has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Has seven wins but has lost her form since winning here last September

runners.name: Jax Edge
runners.spotlight: Two of her six turf wins have been here, one over this distance; hasn't really finished off her races this year but is now 3lb below her last winning mark so cannot be discounted.
runners.comment: Hasn't really finished off her races this year but is now 3lb below her last winning mark

runners.name: Manhattan Mirage
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden who was below form in five runs last year; his mark continues to drop but is best watched until showing positive signs.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden who was below form in five runs last year

runners.name: Wedgewood
runners.spotlight: Unraced on slower than good; front-runner who won here over 5f in 2024; won on the AW at Lingfield in March and has posted two good efforts since, including when beaten 1l in a 5f handicap here 23 days ago; comes into this in better form than most.
runners.comment: Posted two good efforts since winning on the AW at Lingfield in March; has solid claims

runners.name: Porfin
runners.spotlight: Latest of his 11 wins came at Lingfield (6f, AW) in January and he went close at Wolverhampton next time; fair effort over 6f at Lingfield 36 days ago but this slight drop back in trip probably isn't ideal.
runners.comment: Has won 11 times; fair effort at Lingfield over 6f latest but drop back in trip a worry

runners.name: Dion Baker
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who ended a lengthy losing run when making all in a handicap at Brighton (7f, good to firm) a week ago; drops in class but this is the shortest trip he has tackled, which is a concern (all five wins over 7f).
runners.comment: Made all in a handicap at Brighton (7f) a week ago; this drop in trip is a concern

runners.name: Magnificent Mel
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden but she was second of 17 at Nottingham (5f, good) in April; however, she was well held over this C&D 32 days ago and needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Ran well at Nottingham in April but well held over this C&D latest; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Payforanother Daay
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but he was a close-up third in a classified event at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) last August; fifth of seven here over 5f on his reappearance and may come on for that; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: 0-11; fifth of seven here over 5f on his reappearance and now has a tongue-tie added

runners.name: Rokuni
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 27 starts came in a Brighton maiden when trained by Eve Johnson Houghton in 2024; posted some fair efforts for his previous yard last year but he was well beaten over 7f on his stable debut 11 days ago; headgear returns.
runners.comment: 1-27 (Brighton maiden in 2024); some fair efforts last year but well beaten on yard debut

runners.name: Coco Hill
runners.spotlight: Winless since her 2yo days and she has beaten only one rival home in each of her last three starts, including over this C&D two weeks ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Winless since her 2yo days and well beaten in recent starts; others are preferred.

runners.name: Jackie Brown
runners.spotlight: Dual winner in Ireland (6f; good/AW) and she was third at Dundalk on two occasions this winter; however, she was well beaten there 128 days ago and has since changed stables; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Dual winner in Ireland but well beaten when last seen 128 days ago; makes her yard debut

runners.name: Symbol Of Hope
runners.spotlight: Seven of his eight wins have been at this track, the most recent over C&D in August 2024; mostly struggled since and he beat only one rival home here 61 days ago; first start since rejoining Grace Harris.
runners.comment: Seven of his eight wins have been here but struggled recently; back with Grace Harris

runners.name: Ken Brulee
runners.spotlight: Had few realistic chances and ran quite encouragingly in his second handicap, on the AW last December; well beaten on reappearance but he scooted up the favoured rail to win at Lingfield (6f, good; first-time cheekpieces) ten days ago; carries a penalty for that but his incoming 6lb rise will force him out of this grade in future; good form chance.
runners.comment: Few realistic chances; well drawn when off the mark at Lingfield recently; due 6lb rise

runners.name: Spirited Dancer
runners.spotlight: Won C&D maiden as a 2yo but she's had some disappointments since, despite falling like a stone in the weights; down in grade on second run back from a break, which offers some hope.
runners.comment: Won C&D maiden as 2yo; had some disappointments since; now down in grade

runners.name: Sisters In The Sky
runners.spotlight: Last three turf wins came on this track, on extremes of going; went quiet on the AW earlier in the year but he came from a long way back to take a late third over C&D in May and has better claims than most.
runners.comment: Last 3 turf wins on this track; late headway into 3rd over C&D latest; good claims

runners.name: Little Miss Magic
runners.spotlight: Sole win over 5f here in 2024; mostly in indifferent form of late but ran better when seventh over C&D (good to firm; behind Sisters In The Sky and Coast) recently when meeting with trouble; possible contender.
runners.comment: Sole win over 5f here; indifferent form of late but met with trouble back here latest

runners.name: I Was Thinking
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who has kept on quite nicely in 7f and 6f events on Wolverhampton AW either side of a failure over 1m at Lingfield; not impossible on turf debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; minor promise over 7f and 6f on AW either side of a 1m failure; turf debut

runners.name: Coast
runners.spotlight: Multiple wins on AW and turf; has had some moderate results this year but she shaped better at Brighton (about 5f, good to firm) last week, not getting the best of runs but close up in third of nine; should be involved.
runners.comment: Had some moderate results in 2026 but better at Brighton latest and can be involved

runners.name: Port Hedland
runners.spotlight: Has 5f form here; broke duck at Brighton (about 5f, good to firm) in May 2025; probably in better form than form figures suggest, having been eased after suffering interference here last month and not faring badly against an in-form stablemate at Brighton since; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Has course form; probably in better form than form figures suggest; not ruled out

runners.name: Chiedozie
runners.spotlight: Maiden who had form in better races than this earlier in his career but he was vulnerable in AW classified races early in 2025 and has been well beaten in two starts on sand since returning from an absence.
runners.comment: Maiden; vulnerable in AW classified races early in 2025; well held since back from absence

runners.name: Addmann
runners.spotlight: Just five runs so far but has not shown much, including in two course handicaps; gelded since last seen in October; needs much more.
runners.comment: Just five runs so far but not shown much, including in two course handicaps; needs more

runners.name: Mali Star
runners.spotlight: Seven races, best when beaten under a length in 1m handicaps here (firm/good) on last two occasions; not bred for 1m2f but has now been shaping as if worth a crack at it; also now tries headgear but he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Beaten under a length in two 1m handicaps here; worth a crack at 1m2f; also tries headgear

runners.name: Atalanta Mist
runners.spotlight: Third in 1m/1m2f handicaps on firm ground here on last two starts; different ground today but she has far more solid claims than most of these.
runners.comment: Third in 1m/1m2f handicaps on firm ground here on last two starts; different ground today

runners.name: Elora Prince
runners.spotlight: 0-11 in Ireland for Kieran Cotter, tried at up to 1m and twice blinkered in April; probably best watched on debut for new connections unless he is strong in the market.
runners.comment: 0-11 in Ireland, tried at up to 1m and twice blinkered in April; probably best watched

runners.name: Rajbello
runners.spotlight: Blinkers on when switched to handicaps this spring (9.4f-1m4f) but he's not made a serious impact; 5lb lower than his opening mark but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Blinkers on for handicaps this spring (9.4f-1m4f) but he's not made a serious impact

runners.name: Skyolaire
runners.spotlight: There has been a whiff of ability in three of her five starts, including in 1m and 1m2f handicaps here (firm) on last two outings; needs to find extra and cheekpieces are added.
runners.comment: A whiff of ability, including in 1m and 1m2f handicaps here; cheekpieces are added

runners.name: Emerald Coast
runners.spotlight: Debut was apparently much her best form in five-race 2yo career; needs to turn things around dramatically on this seasonal debut with first-time headgear and new trip, and some stamina can be found in her pedigree.
runners.comment: Needs to turn things around dramatically on this return with first headgear and new trip

runners.name: Three Builders
runners.spotlight: Has a mixed pedigree for stamina; gelded and hooded when well beaten on handicap/seasonal debut at Yarmouth (1m) in April, at just 11-2 but his claims are far from solid.
runners.comment: Gelded and hooded when well beaten on handicap/seasonal debut (1m) in April, at just 11-2

runners.name: Shirakawa
runners.spotlight: No impact in her two maidens, a novice or when 18-1 in a recent Windsor handicap, but she does have some ability and perhaps racing at about 1m3f on last three starts has stretched her stamina.
runners.comment: No impact but perhaps racing at about 1m3f on last three starts has stretched her stamina

runners.name: Castlekeely
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in three 6f novice runs for Eve Johnson Houghton last year and the same in three 7f handicaps this term for new yard; his two highest ratings came on AW but the latest did offer some hope for this 3f longer trip.
runners.comment: His top two ratings came on AW but the latest did offer some hope for this 3f longer trip

runners.name: New Objective
runners.spotlight: Trained as 2yo by Jack Morland and showed some ability but well beaten from a wide draw on the round 1m at Doncaster on seasonal/yard debut three weeks ago; 40-1 that day and his pedigree gives more hope for this trip.
runners.comment: 40-1, well beaten from wide draw on seasonal/yard debut last month; pedigree hope for 1m2f

runners.name: Ethereal Charm
runners.spotlight: Modest at best as 2yo (best effort on second start) but tenth of 12 on handicap/seasonal debut at Kempton (AW; 80-1); having dropped back to 7f that day, she now tries a new trip.
runners.comment: Tenth of 12 on handicap/seasonal debut (80-1) when back to 7f; now tries a new trip

runners.name: Maryland Star
runners.spotlight: Needs improvement but this season's AW performances at Wolverhampton in 9.4f maiden and 1m4f handicap at least give her something to build on.
runners.comment: This season's AW performances in 9.4f maiden and 1m4f handicap give something to build on

runners.name: A Daughters Love
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over 5f-7f in Ireland and always behind in an AW classified over 8.6f on debut for new yard three days ago.
runners.comment: Well beaten in Ireland and always behind in an AW classified for new yard three days ago

runners.name: Us Officer
runners.spotlight: Tailed off with cheekpieces enlisted for nursery debut last August but visored for a close fourth of five at Chepstow (1m, soft; made most) the following month; gelded since; the dam won at up to 1m2f and this seasonal debutant needs a second look.
runners.comment: Competitive in small field on final 2yo start (1m, soft); gelded; needs a second look

races.race_name: Thank You To Bath University Handicap

runners.name: Moonlit Cloud
runners.spotlight: 2-3 at Bath, scoring over C&D on her last two visits, but she's not shown her face here since May 2024; has another career-low mark and beaten just under 3l last time, so she brings some interest.
runners.comment: 2-3 at Bath but has not visited since May 2024; has another career-low mark; some interest

runners.name: Man Is King
runners.spotlight: Gained his first turf win when scoring over 1m here (good) last month and followed up over C&D (firm; made all, from Galactic Glow) nine days later; going for a quickfire hat-trick did not work out over shorter at Lingfield 17 days ago but he should be of renewed interest back here at 1m2f; big shout if the ground is not soft.
runners.comment: Two wins here before a quickfire hat-trick bid did not work out over shorter at Lingfield

runners.name: Douglas Dc
runners.spotlight: Still without a win on the Flat (0-23) and only one win over hurdles, making it 1-52 overall; had Flat turf placings in 2024 and 2025 but always behind at Windsor four weeks ago when having his first run of 2026.
runners.comment: Still without a win on the Flat (0-23) and only one win over hurdles, making 1-52 overall

runners.name: Nuptown Girl
runners.spotlight: 0-13 but she did achieve some minor honours over 1m-1m4f on AW this year, which gives her a chance if the form is transferred to turf.
runners.comment: 0-13 but she did achieve some minor honours over 1m-1m4f on AW this year

runners.name: Galactic Glow
runners.spotlight: Four wins and five seconds from his 20 races at Bath; last two outings (Bath and Windsor) reinforce his each-way claims but all of his wins have been on good to firm or firm ground and recent rain is a concern.
runners.comment: Four wins and five seconds from his 20 races at Bath; recent rain is a concern

runners.name: City Escape
runners.spotlight: No win since last March but thereabouts on most occasions since, including over C&D and on AW three days ago; could make her presence felt.
runners.comment: No win since last March but thereabouts on most occasions since, including over C&D

runners.name: Bobby Dassler
runners.spotlight: 2-44 overall, with his latest win at this track (1m, heavy) last October; kept to Bath for his five races this season and has shown enough over 1m3f/1m2f on last three outings to figure in serious calculations.
runners.comment: Showed enough over 1m3f/1m2f here on last three starts to figure in serious calculations

runners.name: Revich
runners.spotlight: All seven wins have been on good ground or softer; latest was in 2023 and although he has plenty of form (this year even) to give him a chance, having a second stab at 1m2f on latest outing didn't go well enough.
runners.comment: All wins on good ground or softer, latest in 2023; second go at 1m2f didn't go well enough

runners.name: Crimson Road
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m2f scorer in 2024 for the Crisfords; however, failed to fire for Susan Corbett last year and well held this term on both starts for new yard.
runners.comment: Failed to fire last year and well held this term on both starts for new yard

runners.name: Four Fifty
runners.spotlight: Won debut at Newcastle (7f, AW) last April; fourth in the Leger Legends race at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last September but yet to make much of an impact in handicaps, so he can't be the percentage call on this first start since leaving Craig Lidster; tongue tied first time.
runners.comment: Yet to make much of an impact in handicaps; tongue-tie on this first start for new yard

races.race_name: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Racecourse Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Sweet Kiss
runners.spotlight: Won three times under Jake Dickson last August/September (1m2f, good to firm-good to soft); no impact in two runs this season but she caught the eye with her late headway at Carlisle (1m3f, good) last Friday; has claims if she can build on that and she won't mind what the weather does.
runners.comment: Three wins last season and she was an eye-catcher at Carlisle last week; interesting

runners.name: Muhib
runners.spotlight: Some creditable efforts this spring including when third at Catterick (1m4f, good) last month; hard to rule out another good run but he didn't really fire at Pontefract last time and his overall record is now 0-23.
runners.comment: Some good efforts this spring but he was well held last time and is now 0-23

runners.name: Letmeseethecolts
runners.spotlight: Hit a personal best with her clearcut win at Pontefract (1m4f, good) last April but she's had only two runs since and has struggled in both of them; has some questions to answer and she needs a major revival back up in trip.
runners.comment: Easy win at Pontefract last April but she's been out of sorts in both subsequent runs

runners.name: Division Day
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m6f Flat winner who has finished runner-up in novice/maiden hurdles (2m/2m4f) in his last two runs; returns after four months off but he's only 1lb higher than for his last win and it would be a positive if the forecast rain arrives; needs a close look back on the Flat.
runners.comment: Runner-up over hurdles in last two starts and he needs a close look back on the Flat

runners.name: The Ubermensch
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but he's finished runner-up in two of his four handicaps and he kept on well when fourth at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) last month; looks worth a try at this new trip and he's in the mix with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 0-7 but he still has potential and looks interesting at this new trip; cheekpieces added

runners.name: It's Only Fun
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a reduced mark when battling well to score at 20-1 at Southwell (1m3f, AW; cheekpieces reapplied) two weeks ago; back up 4lb in the weights but he remains well treated on old form and his first win was on turf; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Cashed in on a reduced mark when scoring at Southwell (1m3f) last month; respected

runners.name: Animato
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner but last two were over 2m at Catterick in May 2024; well handicapped on old form but has mixed record since switched to hurdling in his last seven starts; returns after a break but his tactical speed will be tested back in this sphere and others are preferred.
runners.comment: His last win was over 2m in May 2024; has work to do back on the Flat after a break

races.race_name: Support Weston Park Cancer Charity Today Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: My Mate Roger
runners.spotlight: 75,000euros breeze-up 2yo; finished runner-up in his three starts since December including a near-miss when upped to 1m2f at Windsor (good to firm) last month; sets useful standard on that form and his RPRs have been progressive so far; strong contender.
runners.comment: Runner-up in all three starts and sets useful standard on his close call at Windsor latest

runners.name: Harmonics
runners.spotlight: Made a low-key start at Southwell (1m, AW) in April but he left that form well behind when beating 11 rivals on his return visit (7-2) two weeks ago; this is tougher under a penalty but he has a useful pedigree and is open to more progress on this step up in trip; respected.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell last month and he's open to more progress upped in trip; respected

runners.name: Cliff Danger
runners.spotlight: Didn't show a great deal on Wetherby debut (1m) in April but, after a gelding operation, he shaped with promise when fourth at Nottingham (1m2f, good to soft) last month; has each-way claims if he can take another step forward and he's in the mix.
runners.comment: Showed promise over 1m2f at Nottingham and has each-way claims if he can build on that

runners.name: Hawksworth
runners.spotlight: 23,000euros yearling; first foal; dam placed 7f-8.6f 2yo (RPR 79), out of unraced sister to Group-placed 1m4f winner Sayida, half-sister to Derby winner Masar; already been gelded and this looks a tough starting point.
runners.comment: 23,000euros yearling; already been gelded and this looks a tough starting point

runners.name: Ingemar
runners.spotlight: 42,000euros yearling; ninth foal; gelded half-brother to 1m Group 3 winner Indian Blessing and two useful French midde-distance winners; dam useful 1m2f/1m4f winner; has good target to aim at on debut and market should guide.
runners.comment: 42,000euros yearling; has good target to aim at on debut and market should guide

runners.name: Annahita
runners.spotlight: Modest form when down the field at Kempton (1m, AW) in October and she was beaten 16l on her Lingfield reappearance (11-2) last month; still early days but she has a lot to find here and handicaps will be more suitable.
runners.comment: Down the field in both runs and handicaps look the way forward after this
runners.owner: Dr Jamal Ahmadzadeh & Mrs D Swinburn

runners.name: I Got Rhythm
runners.spotlight: 80-1 and was always in rear throughout on his Newmarket debut (1m, good) last month; was beaten around 16l in that race and he's probably a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: 80-1 and was always in rear throughout on his Newmarket debut (1m, good) last month

runners.name: Le Morbihan
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros yearling; dam unraced sister to 7f/8.6f 2yo winner Pomelo, half-sister to Group 1 scorers Bluestocking (1m2f/1m4f including Arc) and Qirat (1m Sussex Stakes); 22-1 at Beverley (1m2f, good) in April but he ran well for a long way and was beaten only about 4l in fourth of eight; this is a tougher assignment but that was an encouraging start and he should benefit from the experience.
runners.comment: Showed promise on Beverley debut and he should benefit from that experience

runners.name: West Sleekburn Lad
runners.spotlight: Missed the break and was always behind when last of ten on his Catterick debut (7f, good; 66-1) in October; best watched on return.
runners.comment: 66-1 and he failed to beat a rival on Catterick debut in October; best watched on return

races.race_name: Hits Radio Proudly Supports WPCC Novice Stakes Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ruler Of Time
runners.spotlight: RPR of 87 when runner-up in three of his four starts including a close second in a big-field handicap at Meydan (1m, good) in January; just about sets the standard on his return and he should have more to offer at this trip; key player.
runners.comment: RPR of 87 when runner-up in three of his four starts; key player on his return

runners.name: Seet
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five winners including Kalpana (8.6f-1m4f including Group 1; RPR 123); shaped with promise on Newbury debut (7f, good) in April and she found plenty of progress when scoring in good style on the step up to 8.6f at Wolverhampton (100-30) last month; has a penalty back on turf but she looks a smart prospect and is strongly respected.
runners.comment: Well-bred filly who won in good style at Wolverhampton last month; respected under penalty

runners.name: Upper East
runners.spotlight: 300,000gns yearling; second foal; dam 5.7f/6f winner (RPR 89), half-sister to smart 1m-1m6f winner Micro Manage and Listed winners Magnanimous (1m), Thornbrook (1m) and Seisai (7.4f 2yo), out of close relative of 1m4f Group 1 winner Mont Ormel; has useful target to aim on debut but he needs watching in market.
runners.comment: 300,000gns yearling; has useful target to aim at on debut but he needs checking in market

runners.name: Master Of Delphi
runners.spotlight: $100,000 yearling, 150,000euros breeze-up 2yo; seventh foal; gelded half-brother to winners Desert Peace (7f-1m2f AW/dirt including 2yo; RPR 98), Cantata (6.5f 2yo/8.5f dirt) and Mission Beach (5f-6.5f turf/dirt including 2yo); dam US 4.5f dirt 2yo winner (including stakes); this looks a tough starting point but interesting to see how he figures in market.
runners.comment: 150,000euros breeze-up 2yo; has useful standard to aim at on debut and market should guide

runners.name: Impierious
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 1m-1m4f winner Mashhoor (including Group 3; RPR 115) and he's a half-brother to seven winners; beaten 8l when third in the Wood Ditton at Newmarket (1m, good) in April but he looked inexperienced and that was a promising effort behind an impressive winner; should know more this time and he's a likely improver on his second start.
runners.comment: Promising third in the Wood Ditton and he should know more this time; in the mix
runners.owner: Hasmonean Racing, S Hope & S Barrow

runners.name: Forever Endless
runners.spotlight: 80-1 and he finished a remote fifth of six on Nottingham debut (8.3f, good) last month; opposable.
runners.comment: 80-1 and he finished a remote fifth of six on Nottingham debut (8.3f, good) last month

runners.name: Peel Park
runners.spotlight: Won Yarmouth maiden (6f, good to firm) last July but he's found things tough in four handicaps since; dropped another 3lb but he was beaten 11l at Ascot last month and he needs a transformation back in trip; blinkers added.
runners.comment: No joy in four handicaps and he needs a transformation back in trip; blinkers added

runners.name: French Affair
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 11 starts but he hit a personal best when going close in a 20-runner handicap at York (7f, good) last month; the first three pulled clear in that race and he's strongly respected off only 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Record of 1-11 but he went close in a 20-runner handicap at York last time; respected

runners.name: King's Cavalry
runners.spotlight: Kingman colt who made it third-time lucky when landing the odds in good style at Leicester (7f, good to firm) last month; looks on a workable mark now switched to a handicap and this related colt should have more to offer; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Easy odds-on win at Leicester and he's an interesting contender on handicap debut

runners.name: Toastmaster
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but he was consistent for the Crisfords and made a good start for new yard when close third in big-field handicap at York (7f, good) last month; ties in closely with French Affair on that form and he's in the mix.
runners.comment: 0-8 but was a close third in big-field handicap at York on recent stable debut; in the mix
runners.owner: Follow The Majer Partners & S Herrington

runners.name: Thunder Call
runners.spotlight: Well-bred colt who is closely related to 7f 2yo/1m Group 3 winner Skardu (RPR 114); left his initial form well behind when runner-up here (6f, heavy) in November and he found more improvement with an emphatic win on his Kempton reappearance (6f, AW) last month; now goes back up in trip but he looks a useful prospect and is a big player for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Easy win in Kempton maiden (6f) last month and he's a big player again on handicap debut

runners.name: Rogue Messiah
runners.spotlight: Unexposed gelding who won two of his four starts for Jack Jones, including on handicap debut at Lingfield (6f, AW) in April; up 5lb but he's bred to stay this extra furlong and is open to more progress on his first run for James Owen; respected.
runners.comment: Record of 2-4 for Jack Jones and he's open to more progress upped to 7f for new yard

runners.name: King's Trust
runners.spotlight: Beat one who is now on a mark of 95 off level weights at Southwell last October (7f); blotted his copybook by playing up before breaking out of the stalls on intended seasonal/handicap debut in 1m contest at Ascot last month; looked more relaxed at Kempton last week but he never got involved from a wide draw; still early days but he needs to raise his game back on turf.
runners.comment: Southwell winner but he's blotted his copybook since; needs to raise his game back on turf
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

runners.name: Sanaam
runners.spotlight: Forged clear in a Southwell maiden (7f, Tapeta) towards the end of last year but he was disappointing on handicap debut at Wetherby (7f, good to firm; 4-1) in April; has bit to prove again after that 8l defeat and his standout form was on his sole AW run.
runners.comment: Disappointing on handicap debut at Wetherby and his standout form was on sole AW start

runners.name: Illy's Roo
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come over 7f and latest was at Southwell in March; only 2lb higher than for that win but he's finished down the field in last three runs and he's the most exposed runner in this line-up; passed over.
runners.comment: Three wins at 7f but he's been well held in last three runs and is exposed after 12 starts

runners.name: Night Patrol
runners.spotlight: Won a Newbury novice (7f, good to soft) for Richard Hannon last September but that was a shock at 125-1 and he's not gone on from that in two handicaps since; still lightly raced but he was out the back on recent stable debut at Beverley (first run since gelded) and others are preferred.
runners.comment: 125-1 winner at Newbury last September but he's not gone on from that in two runs since

runners.name: Blakefell
runners.spotlight: Returned after wind surgery with a Musselburgh maiden win (7f, good) two weeks ago, but he didn't need to improve to take that prize; still unexposed but this is a deeper race on handicap debut and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Won after wind surgery at Musselburgh but this is tougher on handicap debut; needs more
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow

runners.name: Valpovo
runners.spotlight: Had three runs in France as a 2yo and he won at La Teste De Buch (9.5f, heavy) on his second start; changed hands for 40,000gns in October; returns after 223 days off but he has a striking pedigree and needs watching in market on handicap/stable debut.
runners.comment: Won in France on the second of three 2yo runs; needs close look on handicap/stable debut

runners.name: Cavolo Nero
runners.spotlight: Won twice as a 3yo and latest was at Goodwood (1m, good); didn't shine at Haydock (10.4f) when last seen in April but that was behind a subsequent winner and he was runner-up at Kempton (1m, AW) in March; has claims if he can recapture that form and he's not ruled out back in trip; new headgear.
runners.comment: His last win was over 1m and he's on a workable mark; dangerous in new headgear

runners.name: Intrusively
runners.spotlight: His sole win was in a maiden in August 2024 but he turned things around with a close fourth at Nottingham (8.3f, good) last month; didn't get the best of runs in that race and has claims if he can back that up.
runners.comment: Back to form with eye-catching fourth at Nottingham last month; interesting contender

runners.name: Talis Evolvere
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner but he's 1-20 on turf and has been well held over this trip in last two runs; down the list.
runners.comment: Five-time AW winner but he's 1-20 on turf and has been well held in his last three runs

runners.name: Miletus
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 14 starts came in a soft-ground novice at Windsor (1m) in May 2024 when trained by Charlie Hills; four runs for new yard this year and he's finished runner-up twice, including over 7f here (good to soft) on his penultimate run; has possibilities back up in trip and it would be a positive if the forecast rain arrives; hood worn last time is removed.
runners.comment: Some creditable efforts for new yard this season and he has claims back up in trip

runners.name: Canvas
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won twice over C&D last summer and he defied this mark when beating 15 rivals at York last September; reappeared with a creditable fourth at Newmarket last month and he's a big player if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who reappeared with a good fourth at Newmarket last month; respected

runners.name: Sailthisshipalone
runners.spotlight: Recorded his third win when scoring on stable debut at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) in April and he ran to a similar level when a creditable fourth on his return visit three weeks later; should go well again and he seems fairly versatile ground-wise; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: Won at Pontefract on his penultimate run and was a good fourth there latest; respected

runners.name: Bowood
runners.spotlight: Two clearcut wins at Newcastle (1m, AW) last autumn but he wasn't at his best on Tapeta during the winter; has scored over C&D in the past but he's never won a Class 4 and this is competitive on his return; others preferred.
runners.comment: All of his wins have been in lower grades and this is competitive on return; down the list

runners.name: Rumba Numba
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 4yo who won a Wolverhampton maiden (8.6f, AW) last May and she didn't get much luck when fourth on handicap debut at Glorious Goodwood (1m, good) last summer; now returns after 311 days off but she still has potential and is an interesting contender off the same mark as last time.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly who still has potential and needs a close look on her comeback

runners.name: Mr Mistoffelees
runners.spotlight: On dangerous mark but he's finished down the field in three runs this term and is now 0-12 in Flat turf events; others preferred.
runners.comment: Both of his wins have been on AW and he's been well held in three runs this season

runners.name: Tilted Kilt
runners.spotlight: Belatedly doubled his tally when beating five rivals on his Southwell reappearance (1m) in April; still looks feasibly treated but he's hard to predict and was never involved when a well-held seventh at Ascot last month.
runners.comment: Reappeared with a win at Southwell (1m) but he was down the field at Ascot last month

runners.name: Cadarn
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row at Redcar (1m, good to firm) last spring for George Boughey; now 2lb lower than for his last success but he's been disappointing since including for new yard this spring; this is a big drop back in grade but he reared at the start at York last time and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Two wins at Redcar last spring but he's been disappointing since; needs a major revival

runners.name: It'sneverjustone
runners.spotlight: All of his 14 runs have been on good or slower and he won back-to-back handicaps at Ffos Las (7.3f) and Galway (7f) last summer; still 3lb higher than for last success but he's finished a creditable fourth in both runs this season and he looks worth another try at this trip; dangerous.
runners.comment: Two solid efforts this season and he looks worth another try at this trip; shortlisted
runners.owner: Ian O'Connor & The Fox And Hounds

runners.name: City Of Kings
runners.spotlight: Won Ascot maiden (7f, good) for Charlie Appleby in 2022 but he has been absent, gelded and sold for 140,000AED since well held in conditions event at Newmarket the following spring; this is only his fourth run but he returns after a 1,144 days off and has plenty to prove for another new yard.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 6yo but he has plenty to prove after a mammoth absence; new yard

races.race_name: Join Us To Walk As One Handicap

runners.name: American Style
runners.spotlight: Goes well fresh and he scored on his seasonal/handicap debut last April; again ran well when close third off this mark on his comeback (also Ripon, 6f) this term; he didn't back it up when suffering a heavy defeat at York last time, albeit from a poor draw, but this represents a drop back in grade so he's not dismissed.
runners.comment: Heavy defeat latest but this is a drop back in grade so he's not completely dismissed

runners.name: Betties Bay
runners.spotlight: Rampant mare who completed a four-timer when coming back from a break to score at Catterick (6f, good) last month; that made it 4-4 for this yard and she's still competitively weighted on form for her previous stable; impossible to ignore.
runners.comment: Rampant mare who completed a four-timer latest, going 4-4 for yard; impossible to ignore

runners.name: Papa Cocktail
runners.spotlight: Exposed sprinter who wins in his turn these days but his C&D record reads 312; that latest visit was just one month ago and he competes from the same mark as when far from disgraced in midfield at York a fortnight ago; each-way contender.
runners.comment: C&D record reads 312; same mark as when far from disgraced at York latest; e-w shout

runners.name: Photosynthesis
runners.spotlight: Off the mark for this stable at Newmarket in September; lively in the betting for both starts this spring and he built on a midfield finish at Newbury when fourth of ten at Ascot (again 6f, good) four weeks ago; respected from the same mark.
runners.comment: Solid fourth (6f, good) last time and he's respected from the same mark

runners.name: Novello Lad
runners.spotlight: Track regular who added a second C&D win to his record on good to soft three weeks ago; handicapper handed him an 8lb rise but that looked a career best and he can never be discounted at this venue.
runners.comment: Second C&D win last time; 8lb rise sets a task but he can never be discounted here

runners.name: Alondra
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) in February on just her second start for this yard and defied a penalty over that C&D four days later; creditable effort when sharing third over this trip at Southwell (AW) in April and maybe the cheekpieces backfired when fifth at Wolverhampton one week ago; that equipment is now removed and she has form on turf, so she's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Dual 5f AW winner in February; 6f okay and has form on turf; not ruled out

runners.name: Kats Bob
runners.spotlight: Tends to lead; hit the ground running by winning his first four starts for Ruth Carr last year; plenty of good efforts followed including when he beat 18 others to score at York (6f, good to firm) in June; now 2lb below that last winning mark so a handicapping case to be made, but he did not sustain his effort when finishing well held at York a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Tends to lead; now 2lb below last winning mark; case to be made but well held latest

runners.name: Lord Bertie
runners.spotlight: Eight visits to Doncaster yielded one soft-ground win over this trip in October; made an encouraging return when sixth of 19 in March but didn't back that up when last behind Novello Lad (again C&D, good to soft) three weeks ago; he's not the most uncomplicated but mark is fine if he can bounce back.
runners.comment: C&D winner but finished last behind Novello Lad three weeks ago

runners.name: Bright
runners.spotlight: Both visits here came in the summer of 2024 and yielded a third followed by a win, both at 6f on good to firm; that was his last success on turf but he scored on AW three starts back and last month's return to grass from the widest stall at Chester can be ignored; not ruled out.
runners.comment: C&D form figures read 31; return to turf latest can be ignored and he's not ruled out

runners.name: Mythical Phoenix
runners.spotlight: A solid run when fourth in this race (good) last year but he struggled subsequently, with slow starts also becoming a feature; handicapped to be competitive if a 174-day break has freshened him up.
runners.comment: Last solid run came when fourth in this last year; mark fine if 174-day break has helped

runners.name: Media Darling
runners.spotlight: Made her debut only ten months ago and she bagged an Ayr maiden (6f, good to firm) in August on second start; couldn't defy a penalty on AW next time, while a swing at a Listed race proved beyond her when last seen; look for market interest on this handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: 6f maiden winner who started only ten months ago; market watch on handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Smart Vision
runners.spotlight: Versatile regards ground; six wins include two here (7f/5f) and he was second in this race last year; yet to get going this term but there was the faintest hint he might be on the way back at Chester (5f) last time and maybe the return to this trip can help; mark fine.
runners.comment: Course winner who offered faintest hint he might be on the way back last time; mark fine

runners.name: Tough Enough
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner who last won in August 2023; had reduced mark and regular hood removed when back to some form for second of six at Southwell (6f, AW; Alondra third) in April; could be rediscovering his level and he's not dismissed from the same mark.
runners.comment: Back to some form when second last time; could be rediscovering his level

runners.name: Misunderstood
runners.spotlight: Group 3 win and Group 1 third in France as a 2yo but lost his form in 2025 and beat only one home in both starts for this yard last month; best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Has lost his way and best watched for now unless the market suggests otherwise

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap

runners.name: Dryburgh
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden, with new yard this season; upped to 1m2f, hooded and came from the rear when third at Doncaster (good to soft) and second at Nottingham (good) this spring; enters the reckoning on that form and he may have had stamina issues over 1m4f in a Class 4 at York last time; not ruled out dropped back in trip and grade.
runners.comment: Two placed efforts this spring and 1m4f may have stretched him last time; not ruled out

runners.name: Faeberon
runners.spotlight: Big improvement after a gelding operation when a clear third on handicap debut at Thirsk (1m4f, good) last month; was only just caught in the closing stages of that race and he's just 1lb higher on this drop back in trip; big player with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Clear third on handicap debut at Thirsk and he's a big player on this drop back in trip

runners.name: Howsham
runners.spotlight: Well held in his four juvenile runs (6f-1m) and was tailed off at 80-1 on handicap debut at York (1m4f, good) last month; cheekpieces are now added and he needs a transformation back in trip.
runners.comment: Five-race maiden and he needs a transformation with cheekpieces added back in trip

runners.name: Madjid
runners.spotlight: Kicked off his handicap career with a close second over C&D (good to firm) in April but he failed to back that up when ninth of 14 at Newbury last month; still unexposed but he needs to resume his progress back at this track.
runners.comment: Runner-up on handicap debut over C&D last month but he was only ninth at Newbury last time
runners.owner: Dr Jamal Ahmadzadeh & Mrs D Swinburn

runners.name: Domination
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism when beating a clear second on his seasonal/stable debut at Ripon (1m, good to soft) in April; never dangerous when fourth in similar conditions at Nottingham last month but that was a Class 4 and he has shaped as though this new trip should be within range; needs a close look on this drop back in grade.
runners.comment: Won at Ripon on his penultimate run and he could find more progress at this new trip

runners.name: Battosai
runners.spotlight: Improved form with blinkers added in his second handicap when runner-up at Salisbury (1m2f, good) 13 days ago; looks interesting off only 1lb higher here and he should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Improved form with blinkers added when second at Salisbury (1m2f); interesting contender

runners.name: Aerial Silk
runners.spotlight: Reached the frame in his three 2yo runs (7f), but he returned after a gelding operation with a laboured efforts on handicap debut at Haydock (1m, good) last month; has bit to prove after that and he needs improvement at this new trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed gelding but he needs improvement at this new trip

runners.name: Filly Eilish
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in novice events at Southwell (1m) and Wolverhampton (9.4f) in her first two starts this year; disappointing when well backed at Southwell (1m) in March but she's had wind surgery since and this well-related filly should have a future in handicaps; needs watching in market for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Flopped last time but interesting to see if money arrives for her again on handicap debut

runners.name: Attention Seeker
runners.spotlight: Regressive nine-race maiden who has form figures of 77074 in handicaps for new yard this year; continues to drop down the weights but he was well held at Beverley (1m2f) last time and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Regressive nine-race maiden who was well held over 1m2f at Beverley latest; passed over

runners.name: Turtle Reef
runners.spotlight: All three runs have been on Tapeta; shaped with some promise in novice events at Newcastle (1m) in November but he was tailed off at Wolverhampton the following month; still early days and now goes handicapping but he has something to prove after six months off.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer but he's untried on turf and has something to prove on his return

runners.name: Always A Runner
