runners.spotlight: Had Meaning, Counting Stars and Prom Queen behind her when winning the Kentucky Oaks over this distance at Churchill Downs by just over 1l last month; few miles on the clock with this her fourth race; prospects.
runners.comment: Hard to ignore; a bunch of these behind her five weeks ago.
runners.owner: Douglas Scharbauer & Three Chimneys Farm LLC

runners.name: Meaning
runners.spotlight: Creditable second in the Kentucky Oaks at Churchill Downs behind Always A Runner 35 days ago; has made an impressive start to her career with three wins from five starts and won the Grade 2 Santa Anita Oaks in April; can figure.
runners.comment: In with a shout; second in the Kentucky Oaks five weeks ago.

runners.name: Prom Queen
runners.spotlight: Respectable effort in the Grade 1 Kentucky Oaks (beaten just under 5l) when behind the reopposing Always A Runner (Churchill Downs) five weeks ago; won a Grade 2 in March and winner of two of her four career runs.
runners.comment: Not beaten too far in Churchill Downs Grade 1 five weeks ago.

runners.name: Counting Stars
runners.spotlight: Just over 2l third in the Kentucky Oaks when behind the reopposing Always A Runner (Churchill Downs) five weeks ago; on target three times in her last six outings; can be in the mix.
runners.comment: Must be high on list; third in the Kentucky Oaks 35 days ago.

runners.name: Maximum Offer
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of an allowance optional claimer at Churchill Downs last month; managed two wins in her last four outings; Florent Geroux a notable booking; having her first crack at a big race and passed over.
runners.comment: Having her first crack at a big race and may have to settle for supporting role at best.
runners.owner: Baccari Racing Stable LLC, Alfred Riccio & Robert Cotrone

runners.name: Portfolio Duration
runners.spotlight: Promising filly; impressive in maiden at Tampa Bay last December (8.5f; by 6l) and an allowance there in March (1m; by 5l); favourite, smart effort when beaten neck by Classic Q in Grade 2 Churchill Distaff Turf Mile last month, running on strongly; solid place claims over longer trip.
runners.comment: Promising; strong-finishing neck second in Grade 2 at Churchill Downs; sound place claims

runners.name: Gezora
runners.spotlight: High class for Francis-Henri Graffard in 2025; won Group 1 Prix de Diane at Chantilly (10.5f) in June and Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Filly & Mare Turf at Del Mar (1m3f) in November; inadequate stamina test under 5lb penalty when second to Kathynmarissa in Grade 3 Modesty Stakes at Churchill Downs (1m1f) on return, sole start for trainer Chad Brown; 3lb better off with that winner and should go close on first start for Bill Mott, though trip again on short side.
runners.comment: High-class filly; second to Kathynmarissa on return; 3lb better off and should go close

runners.name: City Girl
runners.spotlight: Ex-French mare (useful form in the Provinces) who has made rapid improvement for current trainer; allowance winner at Tampa Bay in March before smart form to win Grade 3 Beaugay Stakes at Aqueduct (slow pace; won by under 1l but with something in hand, Laurelin third) in May; unexposed and not to be passed over lightly, though needs another step up.
runners.comment: Ex-French mare; improved on third US start to win Grade 3 at Aqueduct in May; needs more
runners.owner: Madaket Stables LLC, Michael Dubb Et Al

runners.name: Kathynmarissa
runners.spotlight: Very smart form in 2024 when second in Grade 1 American Oaks at Santa Anita in December; ran only twice last year, most impressive winner of allowance here (1m1f) in August on debut for current trainer, powering away from Immensitude (Grade 3 placed) to win by just over 4l; beat Gezora (who gave 5lb) by about 1l in Grade 3 Modesty Stakes (1m1f) last month, quickening impressively after missing break; should make bold bid to confirm placings on 3lb worse terms.
runners.comment: Grade 1 placed in 2024; beat Gezora (who gave 5lb) in Grade 3 on return; should go well

runners.name: Speed Shopper
runners.spotlight: In good form this year, with 1m4f Grade 3 wins in the Christophe Clement Stakes at Gulfstream in January and Bewitch Stakes at Keeneland in April; favourite, stayed on really strongly to lead straight in latter, beating Golden Sunshine by just over 1l; not likely to be suited by this shorter trip and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Winner of two 1m4f Grade 3 events this year, but not suited by this drop in trip
runners.owner: Gary Barber, Bridlewood Farm & Eclipse Thoroughbred Partners

runners.name: Pretty Picture
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and progressive filly in 2025; very useful form when placed in Grade 2 events for Sands Point Stakes at Aqueduct (1m1f) and Mrs Revere at Churchill Downs (8.5f) on final two starts; favourite, only workmanlike neck winner of allowance at Keeneland on return in April, when forced wide; needs to improve.
runners.comment: Placed in two Grade 2s last year; only a narrow allowance win on return, so must improve

runners.name: Cankoura
runners.spotlight: Very smart French filly; developed well last year, winning Group 3 Prix de Psyche (1m2f) at Deauville in August; promising return when under 2l fourth in Group 3 at Saint-Cloud, running on strongly after having too much to do; sure to make bold bid for superb trainer and has place claims, but others have stronger form.
runners.comment: French filly; won Group 3 last year and caught eye in same grade on return; place claims
runners.owner: H H The Aga Khan's Stud Sc

runners.name: Laurelin
runners.spotlight: Won first five starts, culminating in Grade 2 Saratoga Oaks last August over this C&D where she pressed the pace and took over willingly to win by 2l; creditable efforts since and shaped well in steadily run Grade 3 Beaugay at Aqueduct last month, travelling well long way; could do better back here.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner over C&D last August; creditable runs elsewhere since; return here suits

runners.name: Bellezza
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; continued her progress with wins in Grade 3 Sheepshead Bay at Aqueduct (1m3f) and Grade 2 Flower Bowl at Saratoga (1m3f) in 2025; favourite, may have needed race (headway final turn, soon tired) in Grade 3 Orchid at Gulfstream (1m4f) on reappearance but came only sixth; questions to answer over this shorter trip.
runners.comment: Grade 3 and Grade 2 winner over further in 2025; questionable after modest sixth on return

races.race_name: Ogden Phipps Stakes Presented By Ford (Grade 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Dirt)

runners.name: Fully Subscribed
runners.spotlight: Produced a good effort in the La Troienne Stakes to finish runner-up at Churchill Downs 35 days ago; has not had much racing and winner of three of her six career starts; likely to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Looks likely to go well; placed in the La Troienne Stakes last month.

runners.name: Nitrogen
runners.spotlight: Good effort in the Apple Blossom Handicap when 4 1/2l runner-up where just ahead of Regaled (Oaklawn Park) two months ago; consistent performer and yet to taste defeat at this track, winning on two visits; should not be far away; absence is no concern as has previously gone well off a break.
runners.comment: Has a good record when fresh and should not be ruled out.

runners.name: Cassiar
runners.spotlight: Went down by 1l at Belmont At The Big A 27 days ago in an allowance Grade 2; consistent performer; tackling an extra 1f now.
runners.comment: Just denied last month; has maintained her consistent level of form.

runners.name: Bless The Broken
runners.spotlight: Finished just behind Fully Subscribed when third (Churchill Downs) five weeks ago; has achieved three wins from 12 career runs and recent runs have been consistent.
runners.comment: Placed in the La Troienne Stakes last month; showing consistent form.
runners.owner: Qatar Racing, Mountmellick Farm & Fergus Galvin

runners.name: Regaled
runners.spotlight: Close up behind Nitrogen when fourth (Oaklawn Park) two months ago; successful in four of her 21 career starts; has been fairly consistent of late.
runners.comment: Unplaced in the Apple Blossom Handicap two months ago; often been there or thereabouts lately.
runners.owner: Ribble Farms & Front Page Equestrian LLC

runners.name: Alpine Princess
runners.spotlight: Got the job done in an allowance Grade 2 by a head at Keeneland 49 days ago; has won here previously; has chalked up three wins from her last six; likely to be a threat.
runners.comment: Ought to give it a shot; won by slim margin 49 days ago.
runners.owner: Full Of Run Racing LLC & Madaket Stables LLC
